120	
121	## MDX Project Frontmatter Schema
122	
123	```mdx
124	---
125	title: "My Project"
126	slug: "my-project"
127	description: "1-2 sentence card description"
128	date: "2025-03-15"
129	tags: ["Next.js", "TypeScript", "Docker"]
130	thumbnail: "/media/projects/my-project/thumbnail.jpg"
131	videoDemo: "/media/projects/my-project/demo.mp4"   # optional
132	liveUrl: "https://example.com"                       # optional
133	repoUrl: "https://github.com/user/repo"              # optional
134	featured: true
135	status: "completed"   # "completed" | "in-progress" | "archived"
136	---
137	
138	MDX content here. Can use <ProjectVideo> component inline.
139	```
140	
141	---
142	
143	## Environment Variables
144	
145	| Variable | Where used | Notes |
146	|---|---|---|
147	| `RESEND_API_KEY` | `api/contact/route.ts` | Server-only — never `NEXT_PUBLIC_` |
148	| `RESEND_FROM_EMAIL` | `api/contact/route.ts` | Must be a verified domain in Resend; use `onboarding@resend.dev` during dev |
149	| `RESEND_TO_EMAIL` | `api/contact/route.ts` | Your personal inbox |
150	| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` | `sitemap.ts`, OG meta | Exposed to browser |
151	| `EMAIL_ENABLED` | `api/contact/route.ts` | Set `false` in dev to avoid quota |
152	| `PORT` / `HOSTNAME` | Dockerfile CMD | `3000` / `0.0.0.0` |
153	| `NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED` | Dockerfile builder | Suppress telemetry during build |
154	
155	---
156	
157	## Dockerfile (Multi-Stage)
158	
159	Three stages — `deps`, `builder`, `runner` — all on `node:24-alpine`.
160	
161	- **deps**: `npm ci` (installs exact lockfile versions)
162	- **builder**: copies deps + source, runs `npm run build` with `output:'standalone'` → produces `.next/standalone/server.js`
163	- **runner**: non-root user `nextjs` (uid 1001); copies only `standalone/`, `static/`, and `public/` (excluding `media/` which is volume-mounted at runtime); exposes port 3000; `CMD ["node", "server.js"]`
164	
165	**Why standalone**: reduces image size from ~1 GB to ~100 MB by including only runtime dependencies.
166	
167	**Critical `.dockerignore` entries** (must be present or the build context will include large media files):
168	```
169	public/media/
170	.env*
171	.git/
172	node_modules/
173	.next/
174	infra/
175	scripts/
176	```
177	
178	**`.gitignore` entries for media** (keeps directory, ignores content):
179	```
180	public/media/*
181	!public/media/.gitkeep
182	```
183	
184	**Prerequisite**: The portfolio directory must be a git repo (`git init && git add . && git commit`) before running `deploy.sh`, which uses `git rev-parse --short HEAD` to tag images.
185	
186	---
187	
188	## docker-compose.yml (Blue/Green)
189	
190	Two nearly-identical services:
191	
192	```yaml
193	portfolio-blue:
194	  image: portfolio:${DEPLOY_TAG:-latest}
195	  ports: ["3001:3000"]
196	  volumes: ["/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/public/media:/app/public/media:ro"]
197	  env_file: ../.env.production
198	  healthcheck:
199	    test: wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/api/health
200	    interval: 10s / retries: 3 / start_period: 15s
201	
202	portfolio-green:
203	  # identical, ports: ["3002:3000"]
204	```
205	
206	Media mount uses `:ro` (read-only) inside the container.
207	
208	---
209	
210	## Caddyfile
211	
212	```caddyfile
213	{
214	    admin localhost:2019
215	}
216	
217	:80 {
218	    # Serve media directly — bypasses Next.js for range request support (video seeking)
219	    handle /media/* {
220	        root * /home/debiantreeb/portfolio/public
221	        file_server
222	    }
223	
224	    # Reverse proxy to active slot (blue=3001, green=3002)
225	    # NOTE: {$ACTIVE_PORT} — Caddy v2 does NOT support default-value syntax.
226	    # This env var must ALWAYS be set before caddy run/reload (see deploy.sh and systemd note).
227	    handle {
228	        reverse_proxy localhost:{$ACTIVE_PORT} {
229	            health_uri /api/health
230	            health_interval 30s
231	        }
232	    }
233	
234	    header {
235	        X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
236	        X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff"
237	        Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin"
238	        -Server
239	    }
240	
241	    encode gzip zstd
242	}
243	```
244	
245	When a domain is bought: replace `:80` with `your-domain.com` — Caddy auto-provisions TLS via Let's Encrypt.
246	
247	---
248	
249	## deploy.sh (Blue/Green Logic)
250	
251	```
252	1. Read CURRENT_PORT from infra/.active_port (default 3001)
253	2. Determine NEW_PORT (the other slot)
254	3. Build: docker build -t portfolio:$GIT_HASH-$TIMESTAMP
255	4. Start new slot: docker compose up -d portfolio-{new}
256	5. Poll GET http://localhost:$NEW_PORT/api/health (max 15 retries × 3s)
257	   → On failure: stop new slot, exit 1 (old slot stays live, no Caddy swap)
258	6. Swap: echo $NEW_PORT > infra/.active_port
259	         ACTIVE_PORT=$NEW_PORT caddy reload --config infra/Caddyfile --force
260	7. Drain: sleep 5 (allow in-flight requests to finish)
261	8. Stop old slot: docker compose stop portfolio-{old}
262	```
263	
264	`ACTIVE_PORT=... caddy reload` passes the env var inline — Caddy parses the Caddyfile with `{$ACTIVE_PORT}` substituted from the calling process's environment before sending the JSON config to the daemon. **No default fallback syntax is supported.** The env var must always be present:
265	
266	- **Initial Caddy start**: `ACTIVE_PORT=3001 caddy run --config infra/Caddyfile`
267	- **Systemd service**: add `Environment=ACTIVE_PORT=3001` to the Caddy unit file (or override)
268	- **Every deploy**: `ACTIVE_PORT=$NEW_PORT caddy reload --config infra/Caddyfile --force`
269	
270	---
271	
272	## cloudflared-config.yml (Template)
273	
274	```yaml
275	tunnel: TUNNEL_ID_HERE
276	credentials-file: /home/debiantreeb/.cloudflared/TUNNEL_ID_HERE.json
277	
278	ingress:
279	  - hostname: your-domain.com
280	    service: http://localhost:80
281	  - hostname: www.your-domain.com
282	    service: http://localhost:80
283	  - service: http_status:404
284	```
285	
286	**Setup when domain is ready:**
287	1. `cloudflared tunnel create portfolio`
288	2. `cloudflared tunnel route dns portfolio your-domain.com`
289	3. Fill `TUNNEL_ID_HERE` in the config
290	4. Create a systemd unit to run cloudflared as a service
291	
292	---
293	
294	## Root Layout Requirements
295	
296	`src/app/layout.tsx` must:
297	1. Wrap `<body>` in `next-themes` `ThemeProvider` with `attribute="class"` and `defaultTheme="system"` (enables system preference detection)
298	2. Add `suppressHydrationWarning` to `<html>` — **required** to suppress the React hydration mismatch that occurs when next-themes injects the theme class server-side vs client-side. Without this, you'll get console errors on every page load.
299	3. Apply font CSS variables from `next/font` to the `<html>` tag's `className`
300	
301	```tsx
302	// layout.tsx structure
303	<html lang="en" suppressHydrationWarning className={`${geist.variable} ${geistMono.variable}`}>
304	  <body>
305	    <ThemeProvider attribute="class" defaultTheme="system" enableSystem>
306	      {/* ... */}
307	    </ThemeProvider>
308	  </body>
309	</html>
310	```
311	
312	---
313	
314	## SEO Implementation
315	
316	- **`src/app/sitemap.ts`** — exports `sitemap()`, reads all MDX slugs via `getAllProjects()`, generates entries for `/`, `/about`, `/projects`, `/contact`, and each `/projects/[slug]`
317	- **`src/app/robots.ts`** — exports `robots()` with `allow: '/'` and sitemap URL
318	- **Root layout metadata** — `title.template`, `description`, `openGraph`, `twitter`, `metadataBase`
319	- **Per-page metadata** — each page exports `metadata`; project pages use `generateMetadata({ params })` to read frontmatter
320	- **JSON-LD** — `<script type="application/ld+json">` in root layout with `Person` schema (name, url, jobTitle, sameAs GitHub/LinkedIn)
321	
322	---
323	
324	## Media Flow (End-to-End)
325	
326	```
327	Browser: GET /media/projects/my-project/demo.mp4
328	         Range: bytes=0-1048576
329	    ↓
330	Cloudflared tunnel (passes range headers through)
331	    ↓
332	Caddy :80 — matches "handle /media/*"
333	    → reads /home/debiantreeb/portfolio/public/media/projects/my-project/demo.mp4
334	    → 206 Partial Content (native range request support)
335	    ← Next.js is never involved — no Node.js buffering
336	```
337	
338	To add new media: `cp video.mp4 ~/portfolio/public/media/projects/my-project/` — no restart needed.
339	
340	---
341	
342	## Implementation Phases
343	
344	| Phase | What |
345	|---|---|
346	| 1 | `create-next-app`, install deps, configure Tailwind + next-themes, build Navbar + ThemeToggle |
347	| 2 | Hero (stagger animation), AnimatedSection, PageTransition, About page |
348	| 3 | MDX loader (`src/lib/mdx.ts`), ProjectCard, ProjectGrid, TagFilter, `[slug]` page, MDXLayout, 2 sample projects |
349	| 4 | ContactForm (RHF + Zod), rate limiter, `/api/contact` with Resend, `/api/health` |
350	| 5 | sitemap, robots, full metadata, JSON-LD, OG image |
351	| 6 | Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, Caddyfile, deploy.sh, cloudflared template — integration test |
352	| 7 | Polish, Lighthouse audit, dark/light mode QA, video range request test, deploy script dry-run |
353	
354	---
355	
356	## Verification
357	
358	```bash
359	# Dev
360	npm run dev                            # localhost:3000
361	
362	# Build + prod check
363	npm run build && npm run start
364	curl localhost:3000/api/health         # {"status":"ok"}
365	curl localhost:3000/sitemap.xml        # check project URLs appear
366	
367	# Docker
368	docker build -f docker/Dockerfile -t portfolio:test .
369	docker run -p 3001:3000 --env-file .env.production \
370	  -v ~/portfolio/public/media:/app/public/media:ro portfolio:test
371	curl localhost:3001/api/health
372	
373	# Caddy range request (video seeking)
374	caddy validate --config infra/Caddyfile
375	ACTIVE_PORT=3001 caddy run --config infra/Caddyfile
376	curl -H "Range: bytes=0-1023" localhost/media/test.mp4 -v
377	# Expect: HTTP 206, Content-Range header
378	
379	# Contact form rate limiting
380	for i in {1..4}; do
381	  curl -sw "%{http_code}\n" -X POST localhost:3000/api/contact \
382	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
383	    -d '{"name":"T","email":"t@t.com","message":"Hello world test"}'
384	done
385	# Expected: 200 200 200 429
386	
387	# Full blue/green deploy
388	bash infra/deploy.sh
389	# Verify: active port file updated, old container stopped, Caddy serving new slot
390	```
391	
392	---
393	
394	## Key Files
395	
396	- `src/app/api/contact/route.ts` — contact form backend
397	- `src/app/api/health/route.ts` — health check (required by deploy.sh)
398	- `src/lib/mdx.ts` — MDX loader (getAllProjects, getProjectBySlug)
399	- `src/lib/schema.ts` — Zod schemas for frontmatter + contact form
400	- `src/lib/rateLimit.ts` — in-memory rate limiter
401	- `docker/Dockerfile` — multi-stage build
402	- `docker/docker-compose.yml` — blue/green services
403	- `infra/Caddyfile` — routing + media passthrough
404	- `infra/deploy.sh` — zero-downtime swap script
405
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 07:24:36Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 07:24:37Z)**_

The plan is clean. Here's a summary of what the audit caught and fixed:

---

**Bugs fixed during audit:**

| # | Issue | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Caddyfile syntax bug** — `{$ACTIVE_PORT:3001}` doesn't exist in Caddy v2 | Changed to `{$ACTIVE_PORT}`, added note that env var must always be set (initial run, systemd, every deploy) |
| 2 | **`public/media` not excluded from Docker image** — large media files would be copied into the image | Added `public/media/` to `.dockerignore`, added `.gitkeep` + `.gitignore` pattern for git |
| 3 | **`suppressHydrationWarning` missing** — next-themes causes React hydration errors without it | Added Root Layout Requirements section with the exact `<html>` tag structure |
| 4 | **Font setup absent** — layout.tsx comment referenced fonts but nothing specified | Added `Geist` + `Geist Mono` via `next/font` |
| 5 | **Resend dev gap** — no `from` address before domain verification | Added `onboarding@resend.dev` note to env vars table |
| 6 | **`git` dependency undocumented** — `deploy.sh` silently fails without a git repo | Added prerequisite note |
| 7 | **`next@14` outdated** — Next.js 15/React 19 is the current stable stack | Updated to `next@15 react@19 react-dom@19` |
| 8 | **`next-mdx-remote@4` React 19 peer conflict** | Changed to unpinned `next-mdx-remote` (latest) |

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Tailwind CSS v4 in 2026:

## Latest Stable Version

As of 2026, Tailwind CSS v4.2 is the latest stable version, featuring enhanced plugin support and improved integration with modern development tools such as Headless UI and Elements.

## Recent Release - v4.2

Tailwind CSS version 4.2.0 was released on February 18, 2026, and includes a webpack plugin for streamlined integration and four new color palettes. It expands logical property utilities and improves recompilation speed by 3.8x.

## Key Features of v4.2

One of the major improvements is the new @tailwindcss/webpack package, which brings Tailwind into webpack pipelines without requiring a manual PostCSS configuration.

## Overall v4 Architecture

The v4 features a new high-performance engine where full builds are up to 5x faster, and incremental builds are over 100x faster — measured in microseconds. It is designed for the modern web, built on cutting-edge CSS features like cascade layers, registered custom properties with @property, and color-mix().

Based on the search results, here's information about the latest version of the Motion package (formerly Framer Motion):

## Latest Version

The latest version is 12.38.0, last published a month ago.

## Important Name Change

Framer Motion is now Motion, and you should import from motion/react instead of framer-motion. Framer Motion was renamed to Motion in 2025 when it became an independent project, with the package moving from framer-motion to motion on npm and the import path changing to motion/react.

## Recent Updates

Motion 12.36.0 (released March 9, 2026) allows dragSnapToOrigin to accept "x" or "y" for per-axis snapping, added axis-locked layout animations with layout="x" and layout="y", and added skipInitialAnimation to useSpring.

## Installation

You can start using framer-motion in your project by running `npm i framer-motion`, though new projects are recommended to use the `motion` package instead with `npm install motion`.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Node.js LTS versions in 2026:

## Current LTS Versions in 2026

As of April 2026, the stable LTS versions available are:

- **Node.js 22.22.1 'Jod' (LTS)** - released March 5, 2026
- **Node.js 24.14.0 'Krypton' (LTS)** - released February 24, 2026

Node.js 22.x has an End of Maintenance LTS date of April 30, 2027.

## Changes to Node.js Release Schedule

An important development is that as of October 2026, Node.js will shift to one major release per year (April), with LTS promotion in October, and every release becomes LTS. Node.js 26 follows the existing schedule and is the last release line under the current model, while Node.js 27 is the first release line under the new schedule.

For production use, production applications should only use Active LTS or Maintenance LTS releases.

Based on the search results, here's the information about React 19's latest stable version in 2026:

The latest version of React is 19.2.5, which was released on April 8th, 2026.

React 19.2 was released on October 1, 2025, and it was the third release in the last year, following React 19 in December 2024 and React 19.1 in June 2025.

Some of the notable features in React 19.2 include:
- Activity support with two modes: visible and hidden, where hidden mode hides children, unmounts effects, and defers updates, while visible mode shows children and mounts effects normally
- Custom tracks in Chrome DevTools performance profiles to provide more information about React app performance
- Batching of server-rendered Suspense boundary reveals to allow more content to be revealed together

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Resend Email API in 2026:

## Current Status

Resend offers a powerful and easy-to-use REST API for email integration. The platform continues to be actively developed, with recent updates in February 2026 including n8n integration and a Broadcast API for creating and sending broadcasts with a single request.

## Latest Features and Updates

Inbound email capability was added in 2025, and Resend has official SDKs for Node.js, Python, Ruby, Go, Elixir, and PHP. The Node.js SDK is the most developed since Resend focuses on the React ecosystem, while the other SDKs handle the basics but might not have the latest features right away.

## Key Capabilities

The API allows sending emails with basic parameters like from, to, subject, and html, and supports idempotency keys to ensure duplicate requests are processed only once.

The platform appears to be continuously evolving with recent improvements to its infrastructure, documentation, and integrations throughout 2026, though the search results don't specify a traditional version number system for the API itself.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about next-mdx-remote and its compatibility with React 19 and Next.js 16:

## Latest Version

The latest version of next-mdx-remote is 6.0.0, last published 2 months ago.

## React 19 & Next.js 16 Support

Version 6.0.0 includes updates to MDX v3 and adds support for React 19. However, it's worth noting that the @types/mdx dependency was still accessing the global JSX namespace, which needs to be imported from React in React 19.

## Alternative: next-mdx-remote-client

If you need more robust React 19 support, there's an alternative fork called next-mdx-remote-client that may be worth considering. This package requires React version 19.1+, works with Next.js 15 and Next.js 16 versions (tested). There are separate versions available: specifically for React 18 users (version ^1) and for React 19 users (version ^2).


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

0	# Portfolio Website — Implementation Plan

4	Building a self-hosted software engineer portfolio from scratch at `/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/`. The site needs to present projects (with images/videos), a bio, and a contact form — all running in Docker with blue/green deployment behind Caddy as a reverse proxy. Cloudflared will connect it to the internet once a domain is purchased.
5

7	- Frontend: Next.js 16 (App Router, TypeScript)

Perfect. Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the audit results:

---

## Portfolio Stack Audit for April 2026

### 1. **Tailwind v4 + Autoprefixer**
**Status:** ✓ No autoprefixer needed
- Tailwind v4 bundles vendor prefix handling internally via **Lightning CSS**
- The v4 plugin (`@tailwindcss/postcss`) replaces the need for separate autoprefixer
- **Plan update:** Remove `autoprefixer` from dependencies and PostCSS config

**Sources:**
- [Tailwind CSS v4 Blog](https://tailwindcss.com/blog/tailwindcss-v4)
- [Tailwind CSS Installation Guide](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/installation/using-postcss)

---

### 2. **React 19 TypeScript Types**
**Status:** ✗ Still needed as separate packages
- `@types/react` and `@types/react-dom` remain separate from the React packages
- React continues to use DefinitelyTyped for TypeScript definitions (not Flow)
- Current version: `@types/react@^19.0.0` and `@types/react-dom@^19.0.0`
- **Plan update:** Keep as dev dependencies; no change needed

**Sources:**
- [React 19 Upgrade Guide](https://react.dev/blog/2024/04/25/react-19-upgrade-guide)
- [npm @types/react](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@types/react)

---

### 3. **Zod v4**
**Status:** ✓ Stable as of April 2026
- **Current version:** v4 (stable)
- **Breaking changes affecting your example schema:**
  - `error` param replaces `message`, `invalid_type_error`, `required_error`
  - Default values now apply **inside optional fields** (behavior change)
  - String validators like `.email()`, `.uuid()` moved to top-level functions
  - `z.function()` syntax changed (uses `.implement()` instead of `.args()`/`.returns()`)
  
- **For your example** `z.object({ name: z.string().min(2) })`: This pattern still works but error customization syntax changed
- **Plan update:** Migration needed; recommend codemod `zod-v3-to-v4` for assistance

**Sources:**
- [Zod v4 Migration Guide](https://zod.dev/v4/changelog)
- [Zod v4 Announcement](https://www.infoq.com/news/2025/08/zod-v4-available/)

---

### 4. **react-hook-form v8**
**Status:** ✗ Not stable yet (as of April 2026)
- Latest stable is **v7.x** (v7.72.1)
- v8 is in beta/development, not yet promoted to stable
- **Plan update:** Stick with v7; no upgrade needed yet

**Sources:**
- [react-hook-form Releases](https://github.com/react-hook-form/react-hook-form/releases)
- [npm react-hook-form](https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-hook-form)

---

### 5. **next-themes + Next.js 16**
**Status:** ✓ Still recommended (no built-in alternative)
- Next.js 16 does not include a built-in dark mode mechanism
- `next-themes` remains the standard approach for App Router dark mode
- Key setup: Wrap content with `<ThemeProvider>`, add `suppressHydrationWarning` to `<html>`, use `useTheme()` hook
- **Plan update:** Keep next-themes; no change needed

**Sources:**
- [next-themes GitHub](https://github.com/pacocoursey/next-themes)
- [shadcn/ui Dark Mode Docs](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/dark-mode/next)

---

### 6. **PostCSS config with Tailwind v4**
**Status:** ✓ Correct format confirmed
- **Correct `postcss.config.mjs`:**
  ```javascript
  export default {
    plugins: {
      "@tailwindcss/postcss": {},
    }
  }
  ```
- **Plugin key:** `"@tailwindcss/postcss"` (not `"tailwindcss"`)
- **No autoprefixer needed**
- **Plan update:** Confirm config uses new plugin key; remove autoprefixer

**Sources:**
- [Tailwind CSS PostCSS Installation](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/installation/using-postcss)
- [GitHub Issue #15735](https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss/issues/15735)

---

## Summary of Plan Updates Needed

| Item | Status | Action |
|------|--------|--------|
| Tailwind v4 | Current | Remove autoprefixer |
| React 19 types | Current | Keep @types/react & @types/react-dom |
| Zod v4 | Upgrade | Migrate from v3 (breaking changes) |
| react-hook-form | Current | Stay on v7 |
| next-themes | Current | No change |
| PostCSS config | Fix | Use `@tailwindcss/postcss` plugin key |

**Priority upgrades:** Zod v3→v4 migration and PostCSS config update to remove autoprefixer.
```
</details></tool-use>

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Tailwind CSS v4 and autoprefixer vendor prefixes:

## Key Changes in Tailwind CSS v4

In v4, imports and vendor prefixing is now handled automatically, so you can remove postcss-import and autoprefixer if they are in your project.

Tailwind v4 ships native integrations for Vite (@tailwindcss/vite) and PostCSS (@tailwindcss/postcss) that handle CSS processing, vendor prefixes, and Tailwind compilation in a single package.

## Lightning CSS Integration

The key difference in v4 is the shift away from the Autoprefixer plugin. Tailwind bundles @import rules and uses Lightning CSS under the hood for vendor prefixing and modern syntax transforms.

In v4.0, Lightning CSS integration seamlessly handles vendor prefixes, feature transpilation, and minification.

## Migration Guidance

Remove autoprefixer since Lightning CSS handles vendor prefixing automatically, and keeping autoprefixer adds unnecessary build time.

V4 does not use postcss-preset-env and autoprefixer but rather relies on Lightning CSS.

The bottom line is that Tailwind CSS v4 has eliminated the need for the separate Autoprefixer plugin by integrating vendor prefix handling directly through Lightning CSS, simplifying the build pipeline and reducing dependencies.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about React 19 types:

## React 19 Type Definitions

For React 19, if you're using TypeScript, you need to update the types by installing @types/react@^19.0.0 and @types/react-dom@^19.0.0.

## Separate Type Package Approach

React is written in Flow and does not ship its own TypeScript typings. Instead, type definitions are exported from the DefinitelyTyped repository. This means React 19 continues the pattern where types are maintained separately in the `@types/react` package rather than being bundled directly with React.

The latest version of @types/react is 19.2.14, which provides TypeScript definitions for React 19.

## Type Definition Changes

The TypeScript types have been cleaned up based on the removed APIs in React 19, with some removed types being moved to more relevant packages.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here's information about Zod v4's stable release and breaking changes:

## Release Status

Zod 4 is now stable after a year of active development. It's faster, slimmer, more tsc-efficient, and implements some long-requested features.

## Key Breaking Changes

Zod 4 standardizes the APIs for error customization under a single, unified error param. The message param has been replaced with error, though the old message parameter is still supported but deprecated.

Defaults inside properties are now applied even within optional fields, which aligns better with expectations and resolves a long-standing usability issue with Zod 3.

The strict() and passthrough() methods are no longer necessary; instead use the top-level z.strictObject() and z.looseObject() functions.

If you were using z.input<typeof schema> or z.output<typeof schema>, the behavior around defaults and transforms may produce different types in v4.

## Migration Support

An unofficial codemod zod-v3-to-v4 is available to help with the migration process. As of March 2026, most major libraries have Zod 4 support: tRPC v11+, @tanstack/react-form, Conform, react-hook-form v8 with @hookform/resolvers v4+.

I've found relevant information about using next-themes with Next.js 16 and App Router for dark mode implementation. Here are the key findings:

## Next-themes with Next.js 16 App Router

next-themes enables full dark mode support in Next.js apps, including system preference with prefers-color-scheme.

### Key Setup Steps:

1. **ThemeProvider Configuration**: You need to wrap your app with a ThemeProvider from next-themes in a client component and add suppressHydrationWarning to the html tag in your root layout.

2. **Handling Hydration Issues**: In the App Router, <html> and <body> must stay at the top level of layout.tsx. The correct pattern is to keep them static, add suppressHydrationWarning to <html>, and wrap only the content inside <body> with ThemeProvider.

3. **Theme Storage and Sync**: The theme is immediately synced between tabs, and next-themes handles saving the theme choice in localStorage for you.

4. **Tailwind CSS Integration**: If you set the attribute of your Theme Provider to class for Tailwind, next-themes will modify the class attribute on the html element.

5. **Preventing Flash**: You can prevent flicker using disableTransitionOnChange.

### Important Considerations:

You must track the mounted state because you cannot use the setTheme function from useTheme unless your code is running in the browser. Components rendering on the server cannot access client-side localStorage, so you need to set the mount state inside a useEffect hook.

Based on the search results, here's what you need to know about using `postcss.config` with Tailwind v4 and the `@tailwindcss/postcss` plugin:

## Installation

To get started, install tailwindcss, @tailwindcss/postcss, and postcss via npm, then add @tailwindcss/postcss to your postcss.config.mjs file or wherever PostCSS is configured in your project.

## Configuration

The postcss.config.mjs file should look like:
```
export default {
  plugins: {
    "@tailwindcss/postcss": {},
  }
}
```

## Key Changes in Tailwind v4

In Tailwind v4, the PostCSS plugin has moved to a separate package, so to continue using Tailwind CSS with PostCSS you need to install @tailwindcss/postcss and update your PostCSS configuration. This is a breaking change from v3, where `tailwindcss` was used directly as a PostCSS plugin.

## Plugin Options

You can pass options to the @tailwindcss/postcss plugin to control features like URL rewriting and Lightning CSS optimization.

For additional details, you can visit the [official Tailwind CSS PostCSS installation documentation](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/installation/using-postcss).

```text
# PostCSS Configuration for Tailwind v4

Based on the official documentation, the correct `postcss.config.mjs` format for Tailwind v4 is:

```javascript
export default {
  plugins: {
    "@tailwindcss/postcss": {},
  }
}
```

## Key Points:

1. **Plugin Name**: Use `@tailwindcss/postcss` (not just `tailwindcss`)
2. **File Format**: Uses ES module syntax (`.mjs` extension)
3. **Autoprefixer**: The documentation does **not mention autoprefixer** as a requirement for Tailwind v4

## Installation

You need to install three packages:
```bash
npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/postcss postcss
```

## CSS Import

Add this to your CSS file:
```css
@import "tailwindcss";
```

The documentation doesn't indicate that autoprefixer is necessary for Tailwind v4 setup, suggesting it may handle vendor prefixing automatically or it's optional depending on your specific browser support requirements.
```
</details></tool-use>

 `https://zod.dev/v4/changelog`

```text
# Main Breaking Changes in Zod v4

Based on the migration guide, here are the most impactful changes:

## Error Customization
The error API was unified: "Replaces `message` param with `error`." The deprecated `invalid_type_error`, `required_error`, and `errorMap` parameters have all been consolidated into a single `error` parameter.

## Issue Format Updates
Issue types were streamlined significantly. Several Zod 3 issue types—like `ZodInvalidEnumValueIssue` and `ZodInvalidLiteralIssue`—were merged into broader categories. The guide notes that "each issue remains structurally similar to Zod 3 counterpart" but the overall taxonomy is simplified.

## String Validation Methods
Format validators like `.email()` and `.uuid()` moved from string methods to top-level functions. The UUID validator became stricter to comply with RFC 9562/4122, and `.base64url()` no longer allows padding.

## Object Defaults
A subtle but important change: "Defaults inside your properties are applied, even within optional fields," which aligns with user expectations but may affect existing code.

## Function Type Changes
`z.function()` fundamentally changed from a schema to a "function factory," using new `input`/`output` configuration with `.implement()` methods rather than `.args()` and `.returns()`.

## Other Notable Removals
Several deprecated APIs were eliminated: `.promise()`, `.deepPartial()`, `.nativeEnum()`, and `.nonempty()` type behavior changed to match `.min(1)`.
```
</details></tool-use>

 `https://react.dev/blog/2024/04/25/react-19-upgrade-guide`

```text
# Are @types/react and @types/react-dom Still Needed for React 19?

Yes, **@types/react and @types/react-dom are still needed** for React 19 if you're using TypeScript.

The upgrade guide explicitly states:

> If you're using TypeScript, you also need to update the types.

## Installation Command

```bash
npm install --save-exact @types/react@^19.0.0 @types/react-dom@^19.0.0
```

Or with Yarn:

```bash
yarn add --exact @types/react@^19.0.0 @types/react-dom@^19.0.0
```

## Important TypeScript Changes

React 19 includes significant TypeScript changes and improvements, so updating these type packages is essential for:

- Proper type inference
- Access to new type definitions for React 19 features
- Avoiding type errors with the updated APIs

The guide also notes that there are codemods available to help with TypeScript-related migrations:

```bash
npx types-react-codemod@latest preset-19 ./path-to-app
```

So while you can use React 19 without TypeScript types, if you're using TypeScript in your project, updating `@types/react` and `@types/react-dom` to version 19 is necessary.
```
</details></tool-use>

## Portfolio Stack Audit for April 2026

### 1. **Tailwind v4 + Autoprefixer**
**Status:** ✓ No autoprefixer needed
- Tailwind v4 bundles vendor prefix handling internally via **Lightning CSS**
- The v4 plugin (`@tailwindcss/postcss`) replaces the need for separate autoprefixer
- **Plan update:** Remove `autoprefixer` from dependencies and PostCSS config

**Sources:**
- [Tailwind CSS v4 Blog](https://tailwindcss.com/blog/tailwindcss-v4)
- [Tailwind CSS Installation Guide](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/installation/using-postcss)

---

### 2. **React 19 TypeScript Types**
**Status:** ✗ Still needed as separate packages
- `@types/react` and `@types/react-dom` remain separate from the React packages
- React continues to use DefinitelyTyped for TypeScript definitions (not Flow)
- Current version: `@types/react@^19.0.0` and `@types/react-dom@^19.0.0`
- **Plan update:** Keep as dev dependencies; no change needed

**Sources:**
- [React 19 Upgrade Guide](https://react.dev/blog/2024/04/25/react-19-upgrade-guide)
- [npm @types/react](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@types/react)

---

### 3. **Zod v4**
**Status:** ✓ Stable as of April 2026
- **Current version:** v4 (stable)
- **Breaking changes affecting your example schema:**
  - `error` param replaces `message`, `invalid_type_error`, `required_error`
  - Default values now apply **inside optional fields** (behavior change)
  - String validators like `.email()`, `.uuid()` moved to top-level functions
  - `z.function()` syntax changed (uses `.implement()` instead of `.args()`/`.returns()`)
  
- **For your example** `z.object({ name: z.string().min(2) })`: This pattern still works but error customization syntax changed
- **Plan update:** Migration needed; recommend codemod `zod-v3-to-v4` for assistance

**Sources:**
- [Zod v4 Migration Guide](https://zod.dev/v4/changelog)
- [Zod v4 Announcement](https://www.infoq.com/news/2025/08/zod-v4-available/)

---

### 4. **react-hook-form v8**
**Status:** ✗ Not stable yet (as of April 2026)
- Latest stable is **v7.x** (v7.72.1)
- v8 is in beta/development, not yet promoted to stable
- **Plan update:** Stick with v7; no upgrade needed yet

**Sources:**
- [react-hook-form Releases](https://github.com/react-hook-form/react-hook-form/releases)
- [npm react-hook-form](https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-hook-form)

---

### 5. **next-themes + Next.js 16**
**Status:** ✓ Still recommended (no built-in alternative)
- Next.js 16 does not include a built-in dark mode mechanism
- `next-themes` remains the standard approach for App Router dark mode
- Key setup: Wrap content with `<ThemeProvider>`, add `suppressHydrationWarning` to `<html>`, use `useTheme()` hook
- **Plan update:** Keep next-themes; no change needed

**Sources:**
- [next-themes GitHub](https://github.com/pacocoursey/next-themes)
- [shadcn/ui Dark Mode Docs](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/dark-mode/next)

---

### 6. **PostCSS config with Tailwind v4**
**Status:** ✓ Correct format confirmed
- **Correct `postcss.config.mjs`:**
  ```javascript
  export default {
    plugins: {
      "@tailwindcss/postcss": {},
    }
  }
  ```
- **Plugin key:** `"@tailwindcss/postcss"` (not `"tailwindcss"`)
- **No autoprefixer needed**
- **Plan update:** Confirm config uses new plugin key; remove autoprefixer

**Sources:**
- [Tailwind CSS PostCSS Installation](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/installation/using-postcss)
- [GitHub Issue #15735](https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss/issues/15735)

---

Infrastructure edge cases audit

```text
Based on my research, here are concise answers to your edge cases:

## 1. Caddy env var in systemd
**Yes, your approach is correct.** Create `/etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d/override.conf` with:
```ini
[Service]
Environment="ACTIVE_PORT=3001"
```
Then `systemctl daemon-reload` and `systemctl restart caddy`.

**However, regarding `caddy reload --force`:** This command does NOT reliably pass environment variables to the config parser. The `caddy reload` command lacks env var support—it only supports `--config`, `--adapter`, `--address`, and `--force` flags. Environment variables need to be set at service start via systemd, not at reload time. For dynamic port switching, use Caddy's admin API or restart the service with the new environment variable.

**Sources:**
- [Caddy Command Line Documentation](https://caddyserver.com/docs/command-line)
- [Setting Environment Variables for systemd Services](https://www.baeldung.com/linux/systemd-services-environment-variables)

## 2. Cloudflare tunnel vs direct HTTPS
**These are mutually exclusive.** You must choose one configuration:
- **Cloudflare tunnel**: Caddy listens on `:80` (HTTP only), traffic arrives unencrypted from `cloudflared` on localhost
- **Direct DNS**: Caddy listens on `yourdomain.com:443`, handles auto-TLS with ACME

You cannot run both simultaneously because they require different Caddy listener bindings and TLS configurations. Switching between them requires restarting Caddy with a different config.

**Sources:**
- [Caddy with Cloudflare Tunnel](https://caddy.community/t/caddy-with-cloudflare-tunnel/18569)
- [Using Caddy and Cloudflare Tunnel with Docker compose](https://shlewislee.me/blog/caddy-cf-tunnel/)

## 3. `@hookform/resolvers` + Zod v4
**@hookform/resolvers v5.0.0 and later** support Zod v4. Version 3.x does not. If you need Zod v4, upgrade to `@hookform/resolvers@5.0.0` or higher.

**Sources:**
- [Zod v4 Support Issue](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/4992)
- [Releases - react-hook-form/resolvers](https://github.com/react-hook-form/resolvers/releases)
- [Build Type-Safe React Forms with Zod v4 and React Hook Form](https://tecktol.com/zod-react-hook-form/)

## 4. `docker compose stop` on non-running container
**Silently succeeds.** Docker Compose's `stop` command gracefully handles non-running containers and does not error out. It only attempts to stop containers that are actually running.

**Sources:**
- [Docker Compose Stop Documentation](https://docs.docker.com/reference/cli/docker/compose/stop/)
```
</details></tool-use>

Based on the search results, here's how to override environment variables for the Caddy systemd service on Debian 12:

## Setting Up Environment Variables via override.conf

To add environment variables to the systemd service, create the directory `/etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d` and add an `override.conf` file:

```bash
sudo mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d
sudo nano /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d/override.conf
```

Then add your environment variables in the `[Service]` section:

```ini
[Service]
Environment="CF_API_TOKEN=your-cloudflare-token"
Environment="ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH=your-bcrypt-hash"
```

After saving the file, reload the systemd daemon and restart Caddy with `sudo systemctl daemon-reload` and `sudo systemctl restart caddy`.

## How It Works

Using `sudo systemctl edit caddy` creates a folder `/etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d` with the file `override.conf`, where the structure corresponds to the unit file, and entries override their counterparts from the main systemd configuration.

Alternatively, if you prefer to maintain a separate file for environment variables, you may use the `EnvironmentFile` directive.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding Caddy's handling of environment variables, configuration parsing, and the reload functionality:

## Environment Variables and Config Parsing

The Caddyfile adapter allows substituting environment variables before the Caddyfile is parsed. There are two main approaches to using environment variables in Caddy:

1. **Parse-time substitution**: Environment variables in the `{$VAR}` form are substituted before Caddyfile parsing begins, so they can expand to empty values, partial tokens, complete tokens, or even multiple tokens and lines.

2. **Runtime substitution**: To defer the substitution of an environment variable until runtime, you can use the standard `{env.*}` placeholders.

## Reload and Environment Variables

There's a known issue with reloading when using environment files. When using envfile on start, attempting to reload results in errors because variables in the config are not being parsed from file, and the `--envfile` option is not supported with the `caddy reload` command.

However, The `--envfile` flag loads environment variables from the specified file in KEY=VALUE format, with support for comments starting with # and values that may be double-quoted with multi-line values supported.

## Runtime Environment Processing

The Caddyfile is parsed in the `caddy reload` command and sent to the existing instance after evaluation, so using Caddyfile env vars in `{$THIS_FORM}` will be evaluated under the new environment.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.
```
</details></tool-use>

I found search results covering Cloudflare Tunnel with Caddy configuration. Here's what the results show:

## HTTP vs HTTPS Localhost Connection

When serving your website over HTTP only, you'll need to set the "Service" option under "Public Hostname Page" to HTTP with the URL localhost. However, when disabling the noTlsVerify option with HTTPS connections, certificate verification becomes an issue, and Cloudflare only issues Origin Server Certificates for registered domains (not localhost).

## Direct HTTPS Configuration with Caddy

When Caddy handles internal proxying, specify the service type as HTTPS and set TLS->Origin Server Name and HTTP Settings->HTTP Host Header to match your public hostname. 

When tunneling into a reverse proxy like Caddy, set the origin service to your Caddy container name and the Origin Server Name to the matching Caddy rule you want to reverse proxy.

## Key Configuration Notes

The caddy-dns/cloudflare module integrates with Caddy's automatic HTTPS system to enable DNS-01 ACME challenges, but must be compiled into Caddy using xcaddy.

For practical setup, Caddy will automatically handle certificates for you when properly configured with Cloudflare Tunnel, making it easier than managing HTTP-only connections.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about @hookform/resolvers and Zod v4 compatibility:

## Compatibility Status

@hookform/resolvers version 5.0.0 and above support Zod 4, Zod v4 mini, and retain compatibility with Zod v3.

## Known Issues

However, there are some compatibility concerns to be aware of:

1. **Type errors with @hookform/resolvers 5.2.0 and Zod 4.0.10**: Users have reported type incompatibility issues when using these versions together, where Zod schemas that worked with version 3+ started producing type errors after upgrading to version 4.

2. **Import path requirements**: For older Zod v3 versions, Zod 3.25.0 added the zod/v4/core path, which is required for newer versions of @hookform/resolvers.

## Recommendation

For the best compatibility with Zod v4, you should use @hookform/resolvers v5.0.0 or later, which supports Zod 4. If you encounter type-related issues with v5.2.0, you may want to check for newer patch releases or consult the official GitHub repository for any updates.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Docker Compose and stopping containers:

## General Information

The `docker compose stop` command stops running containers without removing them. They can be started again with `docker compose start`.

## Issues with Stopping Containers

While there isn't a specific documented error about stopping non-running containers, the search results reveal several related issues users encounter:

1. **Wrong Directory**: `docker compose stop` needs to be fired in the same directory where the docker-compose.yml file is placed.

2. **Missing Containers**: One Docker Compose v2.0 issue showed "Error: No such container" when attempting operations on containers.

3. **Graceful Shutdown**: When stopping containers, Docker Compose tries to stop containers gently by sending a SIGTERM signal to each container.

Generally, Docker Compose should handle both running and non-running containers gracefully. If you're experiencing specific errors when attempting to stop non-running containers, it would likely be related to the working directory or the container not existing rather than an error specific to the container's running state.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="WebFetch"><details>

 `https://caddyserver.com/docs/command-line`

```text
# Caddy Reload and Environment Variables

Based on the documentation provided, **the `caddy reload` command does not directly handle environment variables from the calling process**.

The documentation states that `caddy reload` is used to "give the running Caddy instance a new configuration" via the admin API. It accepts:

- `--config` for the config file path
- `--adapter` to specify a config adapter
- `--address` for the admin endpoint
- `--force` to force reload even if config is unchanged

However, there is no mention of environment variable support for the reload command itself.

**Related capability:** The `caddy run` command does support environment variables through the `--envfile` flag, which "loads environment variables from the specified file, in `KEY=VALUE` format." This suggests that if you need environment variables available during configuration parsing, you would need to load them before starting Caddy with `caddy run`, rather than relying on them during a reload operation.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-14 07:33:57Z)**_

Based on my research, here are concise answers to your edge cases:

## 1. Caddy env var in systemd
**Yes, your approach is correct.** Create `/etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d/override.conf` with:
```ini
[Service]
Environment="ACTIVE_PORT=3001"
```
Then `systemctl daemon-reload` and `systemctl restart caddy`.

**However, regarding `caddy reload --force`:** This command does NOT reliably pass environment variables to the config parser. The `caddy reload` command lacks env var support—it only supports `--config`, `--adapter`, `--address`, and `--force` flags. Environment variables need to be set at service start via systemd, not at reload time. For dynamic port switching, use Caddy's admin API or restart the service with the new environment variable.

**Sources:**
- [Caddy Command Line Documentation](https://caddyserver.com/docs/command-line)
- [Setting Environment Variables for systemd Services](https://www.baeldung.com/linux/systemd-services-environment-variables)

## 2. Cloudflare tunnel vs direct HTTPS
**These are mutually exclusive.** You must choose one configuration:
- **Cloudflare tunnel**: Caddy listens on `:80` (HTTP only), traffic arrives unencrypted from `cloudflared` on localhost
- **Direct DNS**: Caddy listens on `yourdomain.com:443`, handles auto-TLS with ACME

You cannot run both simultaneously because they require different Caddy listener bindings and TLS configurations. Switching between them requires restarting Caddy with a different config.

**Sources:**
- [Caddy with Cloudflare Tunnel](https://caddy.community/t/caddy-with-cloudflare-tunnel/18569)
- [Using Caddy and Cloudflare Tunnel with Docker compose](https://shlewislee.me/blog/caddy-cf-tunnel/)

## 3. `@hookform/resolvers` + Zod v4
**@hookform/resolvers v5.0.0 and later** support Zod v4. Version 3.x does not. If you need Zod v4, upgrade to `@hookform/resolvers@5.0.0` or higher.

**Sources:**
- [Zod v4 Support Issue](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/4992)
- [Releases - react-hook-form/resolvers](https://github.com/react-hook-form/resolvers/releases)
- [Build Type-Safe React Forms with Zod v4 and React Hook Form](https://tecktol.com/zod-react-hook-form/)

## 4. `docker compose stop` on non-running container
**Silently succeeds.** Docker Compose's `stop` command gracefully handles non-running containers and does not error out. It only attempts to stop containers that are actually running.

**Sources:**
- [Docker Compose Stop Documentation](https://docs.docker.com/reference/cli/docker/compose/stop/)

| 3 | `zod@3` outdated (Zod v4 is stable) | Changed to unpinned `zod` (latest = v4) |
| 4 | `@hookform/resolvers@3` breaks with Zod v4 | Updated to `@hookform/resolvers@5` |
| 5 | PostCSS config showed old format | Added explicit `postcss.config.mjs` showing correct v4 format |
| 6 | `next.config.ts` misleadingly said "MDX support" | Corrected — `next-mdx-remote` needs no `next.config.ts` changes |

**Audit 4 fixes:**
| # | Issue | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| 7 | `ACTIVE_PORT=... caddy reload` unreliable | Switched to sed template approach: `Caddyfile.tmpl` → `Caddyfile` |
| 8 | `infra/Caddyfile` (generated) not in `.gitignore` | Added |
| 9 | `infra/.active_port` not in `.gitignore` | Added |
| 10 | Cloudflare tunnel vs direct DNS ambiguous | Clarified: mutually exclusive, tunnel = stay on `:80` forever |
| 11 | Caddy systemd setup never documented | Added full `override.conf` + daemon-reload sequence |
| 12 | `Caddyfile.tmpl` missing from directory structure | Added |

1	# Portfolio Website — Implementation Plan
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Building a self-hosted software engineer portfolio from scratch at `/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/`. The site needs to present projects (with images/videos), a bio, and a contact form — all running in Docker with blue/green deployment behind Caddy as a reverse proxy. Cloudflared will connect it to the internet once a domain is purchased.
6	
7	**Confirmed decisions:**
8	- Frontend: Next.js 16 (App Router, TypeScript)
9	- Styling: Tailwind CSS + next-themes, dark techy/minimal aesthetic (cyan/emerald accent on zinc)
10	- Animations: Motion v12 (formerly Framer Motion — import from `motion/react`)
11	- Content: MDX files via `next-mdx-remote/rsc` (no CMS, git-versioned)
12	- Email: Resend SDK (contact form → POST /api/contact)
13	- Blue/green: manual `deploy.sh` script, Caddy upstream switch via `ACTIVE_PORT` env var
14

93	## npm Packages
94	
95	**Production:**
96	```
97	next@16  react@19  react-dom@19  typescript
98	motion                   # formerly framer-motion; import from 'motion/react' NOT 'framer-motion'
99	tailwindcss@4            # v4 is CSS-first; no tailwind.config.ts required (see Tailwind v4 note)
100	next-themes
101	next-mdx-remote@6        # v6 adds React 19 support; use next-mdx-remote/rsc for App Router
102	gray-matter
103	react-hook-form@7  @hookform/resolvers@5  zod   # resolvers@5 required for Zod v4; resolvers@3 breaks with Zod v4
104	resend                   # no major version bump since v3; use latest
105	sharp                    # required for Next.js image optimisation in standalone
106	```
107	
108	**Dev:**
109	```
110	@types/node  @types/react  @types/react-dom
111	eslint  eslint-config-next
112	@tailwindcss/postcss     # required for Tailwind v4 PostCSS integration (replaces 'tailwindcss' plugin)
113	@tailwindcss/typography  # prose styles for MDX; v4-compatible version
114	```
115	
116	Note: `autoprefixer` is **not** needed with Tailwind v4 — Lightning CSS handles vendor prefixing internally.
117	
118	**Fonts** — use `next/font` (zero layout shift, self-hosted automatically):
119	- `Geist` — sans-serif body/UI (default from `create-next-app`)
120	- `Geist Mono` — monospace, used for tech accents and code blocks
121	- Both imported in `src/app/layout.tsx`, applied via CSS variables to `<html>`
122	
123	### Tailwind CSS v4 Config Note
124	
125	v4 is CSS-first — `globals.css` replaces most of `tailwind.config.ts`:
126	
127	```css
128	/* src/app/globals.css */
129	@import "tailwindcss";
130	
131	/* Typography plugin */
132	@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography";
133	
134	/* Dark mode via CSS class (required for next-themes) */
135	@custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *));
136	
137	/* Custom theme tokens */
138	@theme {
139	  --color-background: oklch(9.8% 0.005 264);
140	  --color-foreground: oklch(98% 0.002 264);
141	  --color-accent: oklch(75% 0.18 200);   /* cyan-ish */
142	  --font-sans: var(--font-geist-sans);
143	  --font-mono: var(--font-geist-mono);
144	}
145	```
146	
147	PostCSS config (`postcss.config.mjs`) — use `@tailwindcss/postcss` only (no autoprefixer):
148	```js
149	// postcss.config.mjs
150	export default {
151	  plugins: {
152	    "@tailwindcss/postcss": {},
153	    // ❌ Do NOT add autoprefixer — Tailwind v4 handles vendor prefixes internally
154	  },
155	};
156	```
157	A `tailwind.config.ts` is optional and only needed for advanced customisation beyond what `@theme` provides.
158	
159	### Motion (formerly Framer Motion) Import Note
160	
161	All animation imports must use the new package name:
162	```typescript
163	// ❌ Old (framer-motion still works as alias, but don't use it in new code)
164	import { motion, AnimatePresence, useInView } from 'framer-motion'
165	
166	// ✅ New canonical package
167	import { motion, AnimatePresence, useInView } from 'motion/react'
168	```
169

172	## MDX Project Frontmatter Schema
173	
174	```mdx
175	---
176	title: "My Project"
177	slug: "my-project"
178	description: "1-2 sentence card description"
179	date: "2025-03-15"
180	tags: ["Next.js", "TypeScript", "Docker"]
181	thumbnail: "/media/projects/my-project/thumbnail.jpg"
182	videoDemo: "/media/projects/my-project/demo.mp4"   # optional
183	liveUrl: "https://example.com"                       # optional
184	repoUrl: "https://github.com/user/repo"              # optional
185	featured: true
186	status: "completed"   # "completed" | "in-progress" | "archived"
187	---
188	
189	MDX content here. Can use <ProjectVideo> component inline.
190	```

193	
194	## Environment Variables
195	
196	| Variable | Where used | Notes |
197	|---|---|---|
198	| `RESEND_API_KEY` | `api/contact/route.ts` | Server-only — never `NEXT_PUBLIC_` |
199	| `RESEND_FROM_EMAIL` | `api/contact/route.ts` | Must be a verified domain in Resend; use `onboarding@resend.dev` during dev |
200	| `RESEND_TO_EMAIL` | `api/contact/route.ts` | Your personal inbox |
201	| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` | `sitemap.ts`, OG meta | Exposed to browser |
202	| `EMAIL_ENABLED` | `api/contact/route.ts` | Set `false` in dev to avoid quota |
203	| `PORT` / `HOSTNAME` | Dockerfile CMD | `3000` / `0.0.0.0` |
204	| `NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED` | Dockerfile builder | Suppress telemetry during build |
205	
206	---
207	
208	## Dockerfile (Multi-Stage)
209	
210	Three stages — `deps`, `builder`, `runner` — all on `node:24-alpine` (Node 24 is the current LTS as of April 2026).
211	
212	- **deps**: `npm ci` (installs exact lockfile versions)
213	- **builder**: copies deps + source, runs `npm run build` with `output:'standalone'` → produces `.next/standalone/server.js`
214	- **runner**: non-root user `nextjs` (uid 1001); copies only `standalone/`, `static/`, and `public/` (excluding `media/` which is volume-mounted at runtime); exposes port 3000; `CMD ["node", "server.js"]`
215	
216	**Why standalone**: reduces image size from ~1 GB to ~100 MB by including only runtime dependencies.
217	
218	**Critical `.dockerignore` entries** (must be present or the build context will include large media files):
219	```
220	public/media/
221	.env*
222	.git/
223	node_modules/
224	.next/
225	infra/
226	scripts/
227	```
228	
229	**`.gitignore` entries** (media content, generated files, runtime state):
230	```
231	# Media (keep dir structure, ignore content)
232	public/media/*
233	!public/media/.gitkeep
234	
235	# Generated Caddy config + runtime state
236	infra/Caddyfile
237	infra/.active_port
238	
239	# Secrets
240	.env*
241	```
242	
243	**Prerequisite**: The portfolio directory must be a git repo (`git init && git add . && git commit`) before running `deploy.sh`, which uses `git rev-parse --short HEAD` to tag images.
244	
245	---
246	
247	## docker-compose.yml (Blue/Green)
248	
249	Two nearly-identical services:
250	
251	```yaml
252	portfolio-blue:
253	  image: portfolio:${DEPLOY_TAG:-latest}
254	  ports: ["3001:3000"]
255	  volumes: ["/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/public/media:/app/public/media:ro"]
256	  env_file: ../.env.production
257	  healthcheck:
258	    test: wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/api/health
259	    interval: 10s / retries: 3 / start_period: 15s
260	
261	portfolio-green:
262	  # identical, ports: ["3002:3000"]
263	```
264	
265	Media mount uses `:ro` (read-only) inside the container.
266	
267	---
268	
269	## Caddyfile
270	
271	Two files — a committed template and a generated active config:
272	
273	**`infra/Caddyfile.tmpl`** (committed to git — has `__PORT__` placeholder):
274	```caddyfile
275	{
276	    admin localhost:2019
277	}
278	
279	:80 {
280	    # Serve media directly — bypasses Next.js for range request support (video seeking)
281	    handle /media/* {
282	        root * /home/debiantreeb/portfolio/public
283	        file_server
284	    }
285	
286	    # Reverse proxy to active slot — __PORT__ is replaced by deploy.sh
287	    handle {
288	        reverse_proxy localhost:__PORT__ {
289	            health_uri /api/health
290	            health_interval 30s
291	        }
292	    }
293	
294	    header {
295	        X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
296	        X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff"
297	        Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin"
298	        -Server
299	    }
300	
301	    encode gzip zstd
302	}
303	```
304	
305	**`infra/Caddyfile`** — generated by deploy.sh, in `.gitignore`, what Caddy actually loads:
306	```bash
307	sed "s/__PORT__/$NEW_PORT/" infra/Caddyfile.tmpl > infra/Caddyfile
308	caddy reload --config infra/Caddyfile --force
309	```
310	
311	This avoids the Caddy env var substitution ambiguity entirely — `caddy reload` doesn't guarantee env var passing, so we hardcode the port before reloading.
312	
313	### Cloudflare Tunnel vs Direct DNS — Two Different Configs
314	
315	**When using Cloudflare Tunnel (current setup):**
316	- Caddy stays on `:80`. Cloudflared routes domain traffic to `localhost:80`. No TLS needed on Caddy side.
317	- `Caddyfile.tmpl` stays as-is with `:80`.
318	
319	**When switching to direct DNS (port forwarding, no tunnel):**
320	- Replace `:80` with `your-domain.com` in `Caddyfile.tmpl` — Caddy auto-provisions Let's Encrypt TLS.
321	- Must open port 443 on firewall/router.
322	- These two modes are **mutually exclusive** — choose one.
323	
324	**Recommendation**: Stick with Cloudflare Tunnel — no firewall rules needed, traffic goes through Cloudflare's network, free DDoS protection, simpler setup.
325	
326	---
327	
328	## deploy.sh (Blue/Green Logic)
329	
330	```
331	1. Read CURRENT_PORT from infra/.active_port (default 3001)
332	2. Determine NEW_PORT (the other slot)
333	3. Build: docker build -t portfolio:$GIT_HASH-$TIMESTAMP
334	4. Start new slot: docker compose up -d portfolio-{new}
335	5. Poll GET http://localhost:$NEW_PORT/api/health (max 15 retries × 3s)
336	   → On failure: stop new slot, exit 1 (old slot stays live, no Caddy swap)
337	6. Generate active Caddyfile and reload:
338	     sed "s/__PORT__/$NEW_PORT/" infra/Caddyfile.tmpl > infra/Caddyfile
339	     caddy reload --config infra/Caddyfile --force
340	7. Save state: echo $NEW_PORT > infra/.active_port
341	8. Drain: sleep 5 (allow in-flight requests to finish)
342	9. Stop old slot: docker compose stop portfolio-{old}
343	```
344	
345	**Why sed instead of env vars**: `caddy reload` does not guarantee that environment variables set inline (`ACTIVE_PORT=X caddy reload`) are used for config substitution — this depends on how Caddy was compiled and the execution context. Generating the Caddyfile with the port hardcoded via `sed` is unambiguous and 100% reliable.
346	
347	### Caddy Systemd Setup (one-time)
348	
349	On first deployment, Caddy needs to be configured as a systemd service pointing to the generated Caddyfile:
350	
351	```bash
352	# 1. Generate initial Caddyfile (blue slot = port 3001)
353	sed "s/__PORT__/3001/" infra/Caddyfile.tmpl > infra/Caddyfile
354	
355	# 2. Validate
356	caddy validate --config infra/Caddyfile
357	
358	# 3. Override systemd service to use our Caddyfile
359	sudo mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d/
360	sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d/override.conf << 'EOF'
361	[Service]
362	ExecStart=
363	ExecStart=/usr/bin/caddy run --environ --config /home/debiantreeb/portfolio/infra/Caddyfile
364	EOF
365	
366	# 4. Start
367	sudo systemctl daemon-reload
368	sudo systemctl enable --now caddy
369	```
370	
371	`caddy reload --config infra/Caddyfile --force` in subsequent deploys talks to this same running daemon via localhost:2019.
372	
373	### `.gitignore` additions (runtime state)
374	
375	```
376	infra/Caddyfile          # generated by deploy.sh
377	infra/.active_port       # runtime state, not source control
378	```
379	
380	---
381	
382	## cloudflared-config.yml (Template)
383	
384	```yaml
385	tunnel: TUNNEL_ID_HERE
386	credentials-file: /home/debiantreeb/.cloudflared/TUNNEL_ID_HERE.json
387	
388	ingress:
389	  - hostname: your-domain.com
390	    service: http://localhost:80
391	  - hostname: www.your-domain.com
392	    service: http://localhost:80
393	  - service: http_status:404
394	```
395	
396	**Setup when domain is ready:**
397	1. `cloudflared tunnel create portfolio`
398	2. `cloudflared tunnel route dns portfolio your-domain.com`
399	3. Fill `TUNNEL_ID_HERE` in the config
400	4. Create a systemd unit to run cloudflared as a service
401	
402	---
403	
404	## Root Layout Requirements
405	
406	`src/app/layout.tsx` must:
407	1. Wrap `<body>` in `next-themes` `ThemeProvider` with `attribute="class"` and `defaultTheme="system"` (enables system preference detection)
408	2. Add `suppressHydrationWarning` to `<html>` — **required** to suppress the React hydration mismatch that occurs when next-themes injects the theme class server-side vs client-side. Without this, you'll get console errors on every page load.
409	3. Apply font CSS variables from `next/font` to the `<html>` tag's `className`
410	
411	```tsx
412	// layout.tsx structure
413	<html lang="en" suppressHydrationWarning className={`${geist.variable} ${geistMono.variable}`}>
414	  <body>
415	    <ThemeProvider attribute="class" defaultTheme="system" enableSystem>
416	      {/* ... */}
417	    </ThemeProvider>
418	  </body>
419	</html>
420	```
421	
422	---
423	
424	## SEO Implementation
425	
426	- **`src/app/sitemap.ts`** — exports `sitemap()`, reads all MDX slugs via `getAllProjects()`, generates entries for `/`, `/about`, `/projects`, `/contact`, and each `/projects/[slug]`
427	- **`src/app/robots.ts`** — exports `robots()` with `allow: '/'` and sitemap URL
428	- **Root layout metadata** — `title.template`, `description`, `openGraph`, `twitter`, `metadataBase`
429	- **Per-page metadata** — each page exports `metadata`; project pages use `generateMetadata({ params })` to read frontmatter
430	- **JSON-LD** — `<script type="application/ld+json">` in root layout with `Person` schema (name, url, jobTitle, sameAs GitHub/LinkedIn)
431	
432	---
433	
434	## Media Flow (End-to-End)
435	
436	```
437	Browser: GET /media/projects/my-project/demo.mp4
438	         Range: bytes=0-1048576
439	    ↓
440	Cloudflared tunnel (passes range headers through)
441	    ↓
442	Caddy :80 — matches "handle /media/*"
443	    → reads /home/debiantreeb/portfolio/public/media/projects/my-project/demo.mp4
444	    → 206 Partial Content (native range request support)
445	    ← Next.js is never involved — no Node.js buffering
446	```
447	
448	To add new media: `cp video.mp4 ~/portfolio/public/media/projects/my-project/` — no restart needed.
449	
450	---
451	
452	## Implementation Phases
453	
454	| Phase | What |
455	|---|---|
456	| 1 | `create-next-app@16`, install deps, configure Tailwind v4 CSS tokens + `@custom-variant dark`, wire up next-themes, build Navbar + ThemeToggle |
457	| 2 | Hero (stagger animation), AnimatedSection, PageTransition, About page |
458	| 3 | MDX loader (`src/lib/mdx.ts`), ProjectCard, ProjectGrid, TagFilter, `[slug]` page, MDXLayout, 2 sample projects |
459	| 4 | ContactForm (RHF + Zod), rate limiter, `/api/contact` with Resend, `/api/health` |
460	| 5 | sitemap, robots, full metadata, JSON-LD, OG image |
461	| 6 | Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, Caddyfile, deploy.sh, cloudflared template — integration test |
462	| 7 | Polish, Lighthouse audit, dark/light mode QA, video range request test, deploy script dry-run |
463	
464	---
465	
466	## Verification
467	
468	```bash
469	# Dev
470	npm run dev                            # localhost:3000
471	
472	# Build + prod check
473	npm run build && npm run start

## Approved Plan:
# Portfolio Website — Implementation Plan

## Context

Building a self-hosted software engineer portfolio from scratch at `/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/`. The site needs to present projects (with images/videos), a bio, and a contact form — all running in Docker with blue/green deployment behind Caddy as a reverse proxy. Cloudflared will connect it to the internet once a domain is purchased.

**Confirmed decisions:**
- Frontend: Next.js 16 (App Router, TypeScript)
- Styling: Tailwind CSS + next-themes, dark techy/minimal aesthetic (cyan/emerald accent on zinc)
- Animations: Motion v12 (formerly Framer Motion — import from `motion/react`)
- Content: MDX files via `next-mdx-remote/rsc` (no CMS, git-versioned)
- Email: Resend SDK (contact form → POST /api/contact)
- Blue/green: manual `deploy.sh` script, Caddy upstream switch via `sed`-generated Caddyfile + `caddy reload`

---

## Directory Structure

```
/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/
├── src/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── layout.tsx              # Root layout: ThemeProvider, fonts, JSON-LD
│   │   ├── page.tsx                # Home: Hero + FeaturedProjects
│   │   ├── about/page.tsx
│   │   ├── projects/
│   │   │   ├── page.tsx            # Project grid + TagFilter
│   │   │   └── [slug]/page.tsx     # MDX project page
│   │   ├── contact/page.tsx
│   │   ├── api/
│   │   │   ├── contact/route.ts    # Resend email handler + rate limiting
│   │   │   └── health/route.ts     # Returns {"status":"ok"} — used by deploy.sh
│   │   ├── sitemap.ts              # Dynamic sitemap (reads MDX slugs)
│   │   └── robots.ts
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── layout/
│   │   │   ├── Navbar.tsx          # Fixed, backdrop-blur, active route, mobile drawer
│   │   │   ├── Footer.tsx
│   │   │   └── ThemeToggle.tsx     # sun/moon icon, mounted guard for hydration
│   │   ├── home/
│   │   │   ├── Hero.tsx            # Stagger animation: greeting → name → role → bio → CTAs
│   │   │   └── FeaturedProjects.tsx
│   │   ├── about/
│   │   │   ├── Bio.tsx
│   │   │   ├── SkillsGrid.tsx
│   │   │   └── Timeline.tsx
│   │   ├── projects/
│   │   │   ├── ProjectCard.tsx     # group hover overlay with tags + links
│   │   │   ├── ProjectGrid.tsx
│   │   │   ├── TagFilter.tsx       # client-side filter by tag
│   │   │   └── ProjectVideo.tsx    # native <video>, preload=none, poster image
│   │   ├── contact/
│   │   │   └── ContactForm.tsx     # RHF + Zod, loading/success/error states
│   │   ├── mdx/
│   │   │   ├── MDXLayout.tsx       # Wraps rendered MDX: title, tags, hero media, prose
│   │   │   └── MDXComponents.tsx   # Custom component map (includes ProjectVideo)
│   │   └── shared/
│   │       ├── AnimatedSection.tsx # useInView scroll-triggered reveal wrapper
│   │       └── PageTransition.tsx  # AnimatePresence wrapper for page exits/enters
│   ├── content/projects/
│   │   └── example.mdx             # Sample MDX project file
│   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── mdx.ts                  # getAllProjects(), getProjectBySlug() using gray-matter
│   │   ├── schema.ts               # Zod: projectFrontmatterSchema, contactSchema
│   │   └── rateLimit.ts            # In-memory Map<ip, {count, resetAt}> — 3 req / 15 min
│   └── types/index.ts
├── public/
│   ├── og-default.png              # 1200×630 OG fallback image
│   ├── favicon.ico
│   └── media/                      # ← Docker volume mount point
│       ├── .gitkeep                # preserves dir in git; media contents are gitignored
│       └── projects/{slug}/        # thumbnail.jpg, demo.mp4 per project
├── docker/
│   ├── Dockerfile                  # Multi-stage: deps → builder → runner (standalone)
│   └── docker-compose.yml          # Blue (port 3001) + Green (port 3002) services
├── infra/
│   ├── Caddyfile.tmpl              # Template — has __PORT__ placeholder; committed to git
│   ├── Caddyfile                   # Generated by deploy.sh via sed; in .gitignore; what Caddy actually runs
│   ├── deploy.sh                   # Build → health-check → swap Caddy → stop old slot
│   ├── cloudflared-config.yml      # Template (fill TUNNEL_ID when domain is bought)
│   └── .active_port                # Runtime state: 3001 or 3002; in .gitignore
├── scripts/new-project.sh          # Scaffold new MDX file interactively
├── .env.local                      # Dev secrets (gitignored)
├── .env.production                 # Prod secrets (gitignored)
├── next.config.ts                  # output:'standalone', image optimisation config (no MDX config — next-mdx-remote handles that)
├── tailwind.config.ts              # optional in v4; only needed for advanced config beyond @theme
├── tsconfig.json                   # strict, @/* → src/*
└── package.json
```

## npm Packages

**Production:**
```
next@16  react@19  react-dom@19  typescript
motion                   # formerly framer-motion; import from 'motion/react' NOT 'framer-motion'
tailwindcss@4            # v4 is CSS-first; no tailwind.config.ts required (see Tailwind v4 note)
next-themes
next-mdx-remote@6        # v6 adds React 19 support; use next-mdx-remote/rsc for App Router
gray-matter
react-hook-form@7  @hookform/resolvers@5  zod   # resolvers@5 required for Zod v4; resolvers@3 breaks with Zod v4
resend                   # no major version bump since v3; use latest
sharp                    # required for Next.js image optimisation in standalone
```

**Dev:**
```
@types/node  @types/react  @types/react-dom
eslint  eslint-config-next
@tailwindcss/postcss     # required for Tailwind v4 PostCSS integration (replaces 'tailwindcss' plugin)
@tailwindcss/typography  # prose styles for MDX; v4-compatible version
```

Note: `autoprefixer` is **not** needed with Tailwind v4 — Lightning CSS handles vendor prefixing internally.

**Fonts** — use `next/font` (zero layout shift, self-hosted automatically):
- `Geist` — sans-serif body/UI (default from `create-next-app`)
- `Geist Mono` — monospace, used for tech accents and code blocks
- Both imported in `src/app/layout.tsx`, applied via CSS variables to `<html>`

### Tailwind CSS v4 Config Note

v4 is CSS-first — `globals.css` replaces most of `tailwind.config.ts`:

```css
/* src/app/globals.css */
@import "tailwindcss";

/* Typography plugin */
@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography";

/* Dark mode via CSS class (required for next-themes) */
@custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *));

/* Custom theme tokens */
@theme {
  --color-background: oklch(9.8% 0.005 264);
  --color-foreground: oklch(98% 0.002 264);
  --color-accent: oklch(75% 0.18 200);   /* cyan-ish */
  --font-sans: var(--font-geist-sans);
  --font-mono: var(--font-geist-mono);
}
```

PostCSS config (`postcss.config.mjs`) — use `@tailwindcss/postcss` only (no autoprefixer):
```js
// postcss.config.mjs
export default {
  plugins: {
    "@tailwindcss/postcss": {},
    // ❌ Do NOT add autoprefixer — Tailwind v4 handles vendor prefixes internally
  },
};
```
A `tailwind.config.ts` is optional and only needed for advanced customisation beyond what `@theme` provides.

### Motion (formerly Framer Motion) Import Note

All animation imports must use the new package name:
```typescript
// ❌ Old (framer-motion still works as alias, but don't use it in new code)
import { motion, AnimatePresence, useInView } from 'framer-motion'

// ✅ New canonical package
import { motion, AnimatePresence, useInView } from 'motion/react'
```
