## MDX Project Frontmatter Schema

```mdx
---
title: "My Project"
slug: "my-project"
description: "1-2 sentence card description"
date: "2025-03-15"
tags: ["Next.js", "TypeScript", "Docker"]
thumbnail: "/media/projects/my-project/thumbnail.jpg"
videoDemo: "/media/projects/my-project/demo.mp4"   # optional
liveUrl: "https://example.com"                       # optional
repoUrl: "https://github.com/user/repo"              # optional
featured: true
status: "completed"   # "completed" | "in-progress" | "archived"
---

MDX content here. Can use <ProjectVideo> component inline.
```

---

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Where used | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `RESEND_API_KEY` | `api/contact/route.ts` | Server-only — never `NEXT_PUBLIC_` |
| `RESEND_FROM_EMAIL` | `api/contact/route.ts` | Must be a verified domain in Resend; use `onboarding@resend.dev` during dev |
| `RESEND_TO_EMAIL` | `api/contact/route.ts` | Your personal inbox |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` | `sitemap.ts`, OG meta | Exposed to browser |
| `EMAIL_ENABLED` | `api/contact/route.ts` | Set `false` in dev to avoid quota |
| `PORT` / `HOSTNAME` | Dockerfile CMD | `3000` / `0.0.0.0` |
| `NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED` | Dockerfile builder | Suppress telemetry during build |

---

## Dockerfile (Multi-Stage)

Three stages — `deps`, `builder`, `runner` — all on `node:24-alpine` (Node 24 is the current LTS as of April 2026).

- **deps**: `npm ci` (installs exact lockfile versions)
- **builder**: copies deps + source, runs `npm run build` with `output:'standalone'` → produces `.next/standalone/server.js`
- **runner**: non-root user `nextjs` (uid 1001); copies only `standalone/`, `static/`, and `public/` (excluding `media/` which is volume-mounted at runtime); exposes port 3000; `CMD ["node", "server.js"]`

**Why standalone**: reduces image size from ~1 GB to ~100 MB by including only runtime dependencies.

**Critical `.dockerignore` entries** (must be present or the build context will include large media files):
```
public/media/
.env*
.git/
node_modules/
.next/
infra/
scripts/
```

**`.gitignore` entries** (media content, generated files, runtime state):
```
# Media (keep dir structure, ignore content)
public/media/*
!public/media/.gitkeep

# Generated Caddy config + runtime state
infra/Caddyfile
infra/.active_port

# Secrets
.env*
```

**Prerequisite**: The portfolio directory must be a git repo (`git init && git add . && git commit`) before running `deploy.sh`, which uses `git rev-parse --short HEAD` to tag images.

---

## docker-compose.yml (Blue/Green)

Two nearly-identical services:

```yaml
portfolio-blue:
  image: portfolio:${DEPLOY_TAG:-latest}
  ports: ["3001:3000"]
  volumes: ["/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/public/media:/app/public/media:ro"]
  env_file: ../.env.production
  healthcheck:
    test: wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/api/health
    interval: 10s / retries: 3 / start_period: 15s

portfolio-green:
  # identical, ports: ["3002:3000"]
```

Media mount uses `:ro` (read-only) inside the container.

---

## Caddyfile

Two files — a committed template and a generated active config:

**`infra/Caddyfile.tmpl`** (committed to git — has `__PORT__` placeholder):
```caddyfile
{
    admin localhost:2019
}

:80 {
    # Serve media directly — bypasses Next.js for range request support (video seeking)
    handle /media/* {
        root * /home/debiantreeb/portfolio/public
        file_server
    }

    # Reverse proxy to active slot — __PORT__ is replaced by deploy.sh
    handle {
        reverse_proxy localhost:__PORT__ {
            health_uri /api/health
            health_interval 30s
        }
    }

    header {
        X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
        X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff"
        Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin"
        -Server
    }

    encode gzip zstd
}
```

**`infra/Caddyfile`** — generated by deploy.sh, in `.gitignore`, what Caddy actually loads:
```bash
sed "s/__PORT__/$NEW_PORT/" infra/Caddyfile.tmpl > infra/Caddyfile
caddy reload --config infra/Caddyfile --force
```

This avoids the Caddy env var substitution ambiguity entirely — `caddy reload` doesn't guarantee env var passing, so we hardcode the port before reloading.

### Cloudflare Tunnel vs Direct DNS — Two Different Configs

**When using Cloudflare Tunnel (current setup):**
- Caddy stays on `:80`. Cloudflared routes domain traffic to `localhost:80`. No TLS needed on Caddy side.
- `Caddyfile.tmpl` stays as-is with `:80`.

**When switching to direct DNS (port forwarding, no tunnel):**
- Replace `:80` with `your-domain.com` in `Caddyfile.tmpl` — Caddy auto-provisions Let's Encrypt TLS.
- Must open port 443 on firewall/router.
- These two modes are **mutually exclusive** — choose one.

**Recommendation**: Stick with Cloudflare Tunnel — no firewall rules needed, traffic goes through Cloudflare's network, free DDoS protection, simpler setup.

---

## deploy.sh (Blue/Green Logic)

```
1. Read CURRENT_PORT from infra/.active_port (default 3001)
2. Determine NEW_PORT (the other slot)
3. Build: docker build -t portfolio:$GIT_HASH-$TIMESTAMP
4. Start new slot: docker compose up -d portfolio-{new}
5. Poll GET http://localhost:$NEW_PORT/api/health (max 15 retries × 3s)
   → On failure: stop new slot, exit 1 (old slot stays live, no Caddy swap)
6. Generate active Caddyfile and reload:
     sed "s/__PORT__/$NEW_PORT/" infra/Caddyfile.tmpl > infra/Caddyfile
     caddy reload --config infra/Caddyfile --force
7. Save state: echo $NEW_PORT > infra/.active_port
8. Drain: sleep 5 (allow in-flight requests to finish)
9. Stop old slot: docker compose stop portfolio-{old}
```

**Why sed instead of env vars**: `caddy reload` does not guarantee that environment variables set inline (`ACTIVE_PORT=X caddy reload`) are used for config substitution — this depends on how Caddy was compiled and the execution context. Generating the Caddyfile with the port hardcoded via `sed` is unambiguous and 100% reliable.

### Caddy Systemd Setup (one-time)

On first deployment, Caddy needs to be configured as a systemd service pointing to the generated Caddyfile:

```bash
# 1. Generate initial Caddyfile (blue slot = port 3001)
sed "s/__PORT__/3001/" infra/Caddyfile.tmpl > infra/Caddyfile

# 2. Validate
caddy validate --config infra/Caddyfile

# 3. Override systemd service to use our Caddyfile
sudo mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d/
sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d/override.conf << 'EOF'
[Service]
ExecStart=
ExecStart=/usr/bin/caddy run --environ --config /home/debiantreeb/portfolio/infra/Caddyfile
EOF

# 4. Start
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable --now caddy
```

`caddy reload --config infra/Caddyfile --force` in subsequent deploys talks to this same running daemon via localhost:2019.

### `.gitignore` additions (runtime state)

```
infra/Caddyfile          # generated by deploy.sh
infra/.active_port       # runtime state, not source control
```

---

## cloudflared-config.yml (Template)

```yaml
tunnel: TUNNEL_ID_HERE
credentials-file: /home/debiantreeb/.cloudflared/TUNNEL_ID_HERE.json

ingress:
  - hostname: your-domain.com
    service: http://localhost:80
  - hostname: www.your-domain.com
    service: http://localhost:80
  - service: http_status:404
```

**Setup when domain is ready:**
1. `cloudflared tunnel create portfolio`
2. `cloudflared tunnel route dns portfolio your-domain.com`
3. Fill `TUNNEL_ID_HERE` in the config
4. Create a systemd unit to run cloudflared as a service

---

## Root Layout Requirements

`src/app/layout.tsx` must:
1. Wrap `<body>` in `next-themes` `ThemeProvider` with `attribute="class"` and `defaultTheme="system"` (enables system preference detection)
2. Add `suppressHydrationWarning` to `<html>` — **required** to suppress the React hydration mismatch that occurs when next-themes injects the theme class server-side vs client-side. Without this, you'll get console errors on every page load.
3. Apply font CSS variables from `next/font` to the `<html>` tag's `className`

```tsx
// layout.tsx structure
<html lang="en" suppressHydrationWarning className={`${geist.variable} ${geistMono.variable}`}>
  <body>
    <ThemeProvider attribute="class" defaultTheme="system" enableSystem>
      {/* ... */}
    </ThemeProvider>
  </body>
</html>
```

---

## SEO Implementation

- **`src/app/sitemap.ts`** — exports `sitemap()`, reads all MDX slugs via `getAllProjects()`, generates entries for `/`, `/about`, `/projects`, `/contact`, and each `/projects/[slug]`
- **`src/app/robots.ts`** — exports `robots()` with `allow: '/'` and sitemap URL
- **Root layout metadata** — `title.template`, `description`, `openGraph`, `twitter`, `metadataBase`
- **Per-page metadata** — each page exports `metadata`; project pages use `generateMetadata({ params })` to read frontmatter
- **JSON-LD** — `<script type="application/ld+json">` in root layout with `Person` schema (name, url, jobTitle, sameAs GitHub/LinkedIn)

### Next.js 15+ `params` Breaking Change

In Next.js 15+, route `params` is now a **Promise** in both page components and `generateMetadata`. All dynamic routes must `await params`:

```typescript
// src/app/projects/[slug]/page.tsx
export async function generateMetadata({ params }: { params: Promise<{ slug: string }> }) {
  const { slug } = await params
  // ...
}

export default async function ProjectPage({ params }: { params: Promise<{ slug: string }> }) {
  const { slug } = await params
  const project = await getProjectBySlug(slug)
  // ...
}
```

Forgetting to `await params` will cause a runtime error in Next.js 15+/16.

### Client Component Requirements

Components that use hooks, state, or browser APIs need `"use client"` at the top of the file. These are NOT Server Components:

| Component | Why `"use client"` |
|---|---|
| `ThemeToggle.tsx` | `useTheme()` hook |
| `TagFilter.tsx` | `useState` for active tag |
| `ContactForm.tsx` | `useForm()`, form state, submission |
| `AnimatedSection.tsx` | `useInView()` from `motion/react` |
| `PageTransition.tsx` | `usePathname()` for route-aware AnimatePresence |
| `Navbar.tsx` | `usePathname()` for active link state, mobile menu state |
| `ProjectVideo.tsx` | DOM event handlers (play/pause) |

All other components (Hero, About sections, ProjectCard layout, MDXLayout) can be Server Components by default.

---

## Media Flow (End-to-End)

```
Browser: GET /media/projects/my-project/demo.mp4
         Range: bytes=0-1048576
    ↓
Cloudflared tunnel (passes range headers through)
    ↓
Caddy :80 — matches "handle /media/*"
    → reads /home/debiantreeb/portfolio/public/media/projects/my-project/demo.mp4
    → 206 Partial Content (native range request support)
    ← Next.js is never involved — no Node.js buffering
```

To add new media: `cp video.mp4 ~/portfolio/public/media/projects/my-project/` — no restart needed.

---

## Implementation Phases

| Phase | What |
|---|---|
| 1 | `create-next-app@16`, install deps, configure Tailwind v4 CSS tokens + `@custom-variant dark`, wire up next-themes, build Navbar + ThemeToggle |
| 2 | Hero (stagger animation), AnimatedSection, PageTransition, About page |
| 3 | MDX loader (`src/lib/mdx.ts`), ProjectCard, ProjectGrid, TagFilter, `[slug]` page, MDXLayout, 2 sample projects |
| 4 | ContactForm (RHF + Zod), rate limiter, `/api/contact` with Resend, `/api/health` |
| 5 | sitemap, robots, full metadata, JSON-LD, OG image |
| 6 | Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, Caddyfile, deploy.sh, cloudflared template — integration test |
| 7 | Polish, Lighthouse audit, dark/light mode QA, video range request test, deploy script dry-run |

---

## Verification

```bash
# Dev
npm run dev                            # localhost:3000

# Build + prod check
npm run build && npm run start
curl localhost:3000/api/health         # {"status":"ok"}
curl localhost:3000/sitemap.xml        # check project URLs appear

# Docker
docker build -f docker/Dockerfile -t portfolio:test .
docker run -p 3001:3000 --env-file .env.production \
  -v ~/portfolio/public/media:/app/public/media:ro portfolio:test
curl localhost:3001/api/health

# Generate initial Caddyfile and test range requests
sed "s/__PORT__/3001/" infra/Caddyfile.tmpl > infra/Caddyfile
caddy validate --config infra/Caddyfile
caddy run --config infra/Caddyfile          # start caddy in foreground for testing
curl -H "Range: bytes=0-1023" localhost/media/test.mp4 -v
# Expect: HTTP 206, Content-Range header

# Contact form rate limiting
for i in {1..4}; do
  curl -sw "%{http_code}\n" -X POST localhost:3000/api/contact \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"name":"T","email":"t@t.com","message":"Hello world test"}'
done
# Expected: 200 200 200 429

# Full blue/green deploy
bash infra/deploy.sh
# Verify: active port file updated, old container stopped, Caddy serving new slot
```

---

## Key Files

- `src/app/api/contact/route.ts` — contact form backend
- `src/app/api/health/route.ts` — health check (required by deploy.sh)
- `src/lib/mdx.ts` — MDX loader (getAllProjects, getProjectBySlug)
- `src/lib/schema.ts` — Zod schemas for frontmatter + contact form
- `src/lib/rateLimit.ts` — in-memory rate limiter
- `docker/Dockerfile` — multi-stage build
- `docker/docker-compose.yml` — blue/green services
- `infra/Caddyfile.tmpl` — Caddy routing template (sed-substituted on each deploy)
- `infra/deploy.sh` — zero-downtime swap script
```

**Stack:** Next.js 16 / React 19 / TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4 (CSS-first, @theme tokens, @custom-variant dark), Motion v12 (import from 'motion/react'), next-mdx-remote@6 (RSC), Resend for email, Zod v4 + @hookform/resolvers@5 + react-hook-form@7.

**Why:** Personal portfolio to showcase software engineering projects with image/video media support, contact form, and self-hosted infrastructure.

**How to apply:** When continuing work on this project, reference the plan file for full spec. Key quirks: params is a Promise in Next.js 15+/16, Tailwind v4 uses @import "tailwindcss" not directives, motion imports come from 'motion/react'.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 08:02:35Z)**_

Good. Now I'll build everything for Phase 1. Let me start with the files in parallel:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
`@custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *))` tells Tailwind v4 to apply dark-prefixed utilities whenever an ancestor has the `dark` CSS class — matching exactly what next-themes adds to `<html>`. The `&:where(...)` wrapper uses zero CSS specificity, so component-level overrides always win. This replaces Tailwind v3's `darkMode: 'class'` config entry.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

<summary>Tool use: **Edit**</summary>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```css
@import "tailwindcss";
@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography";

/* Dark mode: next-themes adds the 'dark' class to <html> */
@custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *));

/* Design tokens */
@theme {
  /* Zinc-based dark-techy palette */
  --color-background: oklch(10% 0.005 264);
  --color-surface: oklch(14% 0.006 264);
  --color-surface-alt: oklch(18% 0.007 264);
  --color-border: oklch(25% 0.008 264);
  --color-foreground: oklch(96% 0.003 264);
  --color-muted: oklch(60% 0.01 264);
  --color-accent: oklch(74% 0.17 200);      /* cyan-400 equivalent */
  --color-accent-dim: oklch(74% 0.17 200 / 12%);

  /* Light mode overrides (applied via .light class) */
  --color-background-light: oklch(98% 0.002 264);
  --color-surface-light: oklch(94% 0.004 264);
  --color-surface-alt-light: oklch(89% 0.006 264);
  --color-border-light: oklch(80% 0.008 264);
  --color-foreground-light: oklch(12% 0.005 264);
  --color-muted-light: oklch(48% 0.01 264);

  --font-sans: var(--font-geist-sans);
  --font-mono: var(--font-geist-mono);

  --radius-card: 0.75rem;
  --shadow-glow: 0 0 20px oklch(74% 0.17 200 / 20%);
}

/* Base layer: apply tokens to CSS properties */
:root {
  --bg: var(--color-background);
  --surface: var(--color-surface);
  --surface-alt: var(--color-surface-alt);
  --border: var(--color-border);
  --fg: var(--color-foreground);
  --muted: var(--color-muted);
  --accent: var(--color-accent);
  --accent-dim: var(--color-accent-dim);
}

:root.light {
  --bg: var(--color-background-light);
  --surface: var(--color-surface-light);
  --surface-alt: var(--color-surface-alt-light);
  --border: var(--color-border-light);
  --fg: var(--color-foreground-light);
  --muted: var(--color-muted-light);
}

html {
  scroll-behavior: smooth;
}

body {
  background-color: var(--bg);
  color: var(--fg);
  font-family: var(--font-sans), system-ui, sans-serif;
  min-height: 100vh;
  transition: background-color 0.2s ease, color 0.2s ease;
}

/* Scrollbar styling */
::-webkit-scrollbar {
  width: 6px;
}
::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
  background: var(--surface);
}
::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
  background: var(--border);
  border-radius: 3px;
}
::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover {
  background: var(--muted);
}

/* Focus ring */
:focus-visible {
  outline: 2px solid var(--accent);
  outline-offset: 2px;
}

/* Prose overrides for MDX content */
.prose {
  --tw-prose-body: var(--fg);
  --tw-prose-headings: var(--fg);
  --tw-prose-code: var(--accent);
  --tw-prose-links: var(--accent);
  --tw-prose-bold: var(--fg);
  --tw-prose-quotes: var(--muted);
  --tw-prose-hr: var(--border);
}

```

@import "tailwindcss";
@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography";

/* Dark mode: next-themes adds the 'dark' class to <html> */
@custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *));

/* Design tokens */
@theme {
  /* Zinc-based dark-techy palette */
  --color-bg: oklch(10% 0.005 264);
  --color-surface: oklch(14% 0.006 264);
  --color-surface-alt: oklch(18% 0.007 264);
  --color-border: oklch(25% 0.008 264);
  --color-fg: oklch(96% 0.003 264);
  --color-muted: oklch(60% 0.01 264);
  --color-accent: oklch(74% 0.17 200);      /* cyan-400 equivalent */
  --color-accent-dim: oklch(74% 0.17 200 / 12%);

  --font-sans: var(--font-geist-sans);
  --font-mono: var(--font-geist-mono);

  --radius-card: 0.75rem;
}

/* Base variables (dark by default — system default, overridden by .light) */
:root {
  --bg: var(--color-bg);
  --surface: var(--color-surface);
  --surface-alt: var(--color-surface-alt);
  --border: var(--color-border);
  --fg: var(--color-fg);
  --muted: var(--color-muted);
  --accent: var(--color-accent);
  --accent-dim: var(--color-accent-dim);
  --glow: 0 0 20px oklch(74% 0.17 200 / 15%);
}

/* Light mode overrides — next-themes adds .light to <html> */
.light {
  --bg: oklch(98% 0.002 264);
  --surface: oklch(94% 0.004 264);
  --surface-alt: oklch(89% 0.006 264);
  --border: oklch(80% 0.008 264);
  --fg: oklch(12% 0.005 264);
  --muted: oklch(48% 0.01 264);
  --glow: none;
}

html {
  scroll-behavior: smooth;
  background-color: var(--bg);
}

body {
  background-color: var(--bg);
  color: var(--fg);
  font-family: var(--font-sans), system-ui, sans-serif;
  min-height: 100vh;
  transition: background-color 0.2s ease, color 0.2s ease;
}

/* Subtle custom scrollbar */
::-webkit-scrollbar { width: 6px; }
::-webkit-scrollbar-track { background: var(--surface); }
::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { background: var(--border); border-radius: 3px; }
::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover { background: var(--muted); }

/* Focus ring */
:focus-visible {
  outline: 2px solid var(--accent);
  outline-offset: 2px;
}

/* Prose MDX overrides */
.prose {
  --tw-prose-body: var(--fg);
  --tw-prose-headings: var(--fg);
  --tw-prose-code: var(--accent);
  --tw-prose-links: var(--accent);
  --tw-prose-bold: var(--fg);
  --tw-prose-quotes: var(--muted);
  --tw-prose-hr: var(--border);
  --tw-prose-pre-bg: var(--surface);
}

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 08:03:17Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import type { Metadata } from "next";
import { Geist, Geist_Mono } from "next/font/google";
import { ThemeProvider } from "next-themes";
import Navbar from "@/components/layout/Navbar";
import Footer from "@/components/layout/Footer";
import "./globals.css";

const geistSans = Geist({
  variable: "--font-geist-sans",
  subsets: ["latin"],
});

const geistMono = Geist_Mono({
  variable: "--font-geist-mono",
  subsets: ["latin"],
});

export const metadata: Metadata = {
  title: {
    default: "Your Name | Software Engineer",
    template: "%s | Your Name",
  },
  description:
    "Software engineer building reliable, scalable systems. Explore my projects, skills, and get in touch.",
  metadataBase: new URL(
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL ?? "http://localhost:3000"
  ),
  openGraph: {
    type: "website",
    locale: "en_US",
    url: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL ?? "http://localhost:3000",
    siteName: "Your Name",
    images: [{ url: "/og-default.png", width: 1200, height: 630 }],
  },
  twitter: {
    card: "summary_large_image",
    creator: "@yourhandle",
  },
  robots: { index: true, follow: true },
};

const jsonLd = {
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Person",
  name: "Your Name",
  url: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL ?? "http://localhost:3000",
  jobTitle: "Software Engineer",
  sameAs: [
    "https://github.com/yourusername",
    "https://linkedin.com/in/yourprofile",
  ],
};

export default function RootLayout({
  children,
}: Readonly<{ children: React.ReactNode }>) {
  return (
    <html
      lang="en"
      // suppressHydrationWarning is REQUIRED for next-themes:
      // next-themes injects the theme class server-side which differs from
      // the client-side system preference check, causing a hydration mismatch.
      suppressHydrationWarning
      className={`${geistSans.variable} ${geistMono.variable}`}
    >
      <head>
        <script
          type="application/ld+json"
          dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: JSON.stringify(jsonLd) }}
        />
      </head>
      <body className="flex min-h-screen flex-col">
        <ThemeProvider
          attribute="class"
          defaultTheme="system"
          enableSystem
          disableTransitionOnChange={false}
        >
          <Navbar />
          <main className="flex-1">{children}</main>
          <Footer />
        </ThemeProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}

```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 08:14:16Z)**_

Now build `ThemeToggle.tsx` and `Navbar.tsx`:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The `mounted` guard in ThemeToggle prevents a "sun/moon flash" on load — `useTheme()` reads the DOM class which only exists client-side, so the initial SSR render has no theme context. Rendering nothing until `mounted=true` eliminates that mismatch. This is a common pattern with any library that reads client-only browser state.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 08:14:26Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
"use client";

import { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { useTheme } from "next-themes";
import { motion, AnimatePresence } from "motion/react";

export default function ThemeToggle() {
  const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false);
  const { resolvedTheme, setTheme } = useTheme();

  // Must be mounted before rendering — useTheme() reads the DOM class which
  // doesn't exist during SSR, causing a hydration mismatch without this guard.
  useEffect(() => setMounted(true), []);

  if (!mounted) {
    return <div className="h-8 w-8" aria-hidden />;
  }

  const isDark = resolvedTheme === "dark";

  return (
    <button
      onClick={() => setTheme(isDark ? "light" : "dark")}
      className="relative flex h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded-md transition-colors hover:bg-[var(--surface-alt)] focus-visible:outline-2"
      aria-label={isDark ? "Switch to light mode" : "Switch to dark mode"}
    >
      <AnimatePresence mode="wait" initial={false}>
        {isDark ? (
          <motion.svg
            key="moon"
            xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
            width="16"
            height="16"
            viewBox="0 0 24 24"
            fill="none"
            stroke="currentColor"
            strokeWidth="2"
            strokeLinecap="round"
            strokeLinejoin="round"
            initial={{ opacity: 0, rotate: -30, scale: 0.8 }}
            animate={{ opacity: 1, rotate: 0, scale: 1 }}
            exit={{ opacity: 0, rotate: 30, scale: 0.8 }}
            transition={{ duration: 0.2 }}
            aria-hidden
          >
            <path d="M21 12.79A9 9 0 1 1 11.21 3 7 7 0 0 0 21 12.79z" />
          </motion.svg>
        ) : (
          <motion.svg
            key="sun"
            xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
            width="16"
            height="16"
            viewBox="0 0 24 24"
            fill="none"
            stroke="currentColor"
            strokeWidth="2"
            strokeLinecap="round"
            strokeLinejoin="round"
            initial={{ opacity: 0, rotate: 30, scale: 0.8 }}
            animate={{ opacity: 1, rotate: 0, scale: 1 }}
            exit={{ opacity: 0, rotate: -30, scale: 0.8 }}
            transition={{ duration: 0.2 }}
            aria-hidden
          >
            <circle cx="12" cy="12" r="5" />
            <line x1="12" y1="1" x2="12" y2="3" />
            <line x1="12" y1="21" x2="12" y2="23" />
            <line x1="4.22" y1="4.22" x2="5.64" y2="5.64" />
            <line x1="18.36" y1="18.36" x2="19.78" y2="19.78" />
            <line x1="1" y1="12" x2="3" y2="12" />
            <line x1="21" y1="12" x2="23" y2="12" />
            <line x1="4.22" y1="19.78" x2="5.64" y2="18.36" />
            <line x1="18.36" y1="5.64" x2="19.78" y2="4.22" />
          </motion.svg>
        )}
      </AnimatePresence>
    </button>
  );
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
"use client";

import Link from "next/link";
import { usePathname } from "next/navigation";
import { useState } from "react";
import { motion, AnimatePresence } from "motion/react";
import ThemeToggle from "./ThemeToggle";

const navLinks = [
  { href: "/about", label: "About" },
  { href: "/projects", label: "Projects" },
  { href: "/contact", label: "Contact" },
];

export default function Navbar() {
  const pathname = usePathname();
  const [mobileOpen, setMobileOpen] = useState(false);

  return (
    <motion.header
      initial={{ y: -20, opacity: 0 }}
      animate={{ y: 0, opacity: 1 }}
      transition={{ duration: 0.4, ease: "easeOut" }}
      className="sticky top-0 z-50 border-b border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg)]/80 backdrop-blur-md"
    >
      <nav className="mx-auto flex max-w-5xl items-center justify-between px-6 py-4">
        {/* Logo */}
        <Link
          href="/"
          className="font-mono text-sm font-semibold tracking-tight text-[var(--accent)] transition-opacity hover:opacity-80"
        >
          &gt; yourname<span className="animate-pulse">_</span>
        </Link>

        {/* Desktop nav links */}
        <ul className="hidden items-center gap-6 sm:flex">
          {navLinks.map(({ href, label }) => {
            const active = pathname === href || pathname.startsWith(href + "/");
            return (
              <li key={href}>
                <Link
                  href={href}
                  className={`text-sm transition-colors ${
                    active
                      ? "text-[var(--accent)]"
                      : "text-[var(--muted)] hover:text-[var(--fg)]"
                  }`}
                >
                  {label}
                </Link>
              </li>
            );
          })}
        </ul>

        {/* Right side: theme toggle + mobile hamburger */}
        <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
          <ThemeToggle />
          <button
            className="flex h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded-md sm:hidden hover:bg-[var(--surface-alt)] transition-colors"
            onClick={() => setMobileOpen((v) => !v)}
            aria-label={mobileOpen ? "Close menu" : "Open menu"}
          >
            <motion.div
              animate={mobileOpen ? "open" : "closed"}
              className="flex flex-col gap-1.5"
            >
              {[0, 1, 2].map((i) => (
                <motion.span
                  key={i}
                  className="block h-px w-5 bg-[var(--fg)] origin-center"
                  variants={{
                    open: {
                      rotate: i === 0 ? 45 : i === 2 ? -45 : 0,
                      y: i === 0 ? 6 : i === 2 ? -6 : 0,
                      opacity: i === 1 ? 0 : 1,
                    },
                    closed: { rotate: 0, y: 0, opacity: 1 },
                  }}
                  transition={{ duration: 0.2 }}
                />
              ))}
            </motion.div>
          </button>
        </div>
      </nav>

      {/* Mobile dropdown */}
      <AnimatePresence>
        {mobileOpen && (
          <motion.ul
            initial={{ height: 0, opacity: 0 }}
            animate={{ height: "auto", opacity: 1 }}
            exit={{ height: 0, opacity: 0 }}
            transition={{ duration: 0.25, ease: "easeInOut" }}
            className="overflow-hidden border-t border-[var(--border)] sm:hidden"
          >
            {navLinks.map(({ href, label }) => {
              const active =
                pathname === href || pathname.startsWith(href + "/");
              return (
                <li key={href}>
                  <Link
                    href={href}
                    onClick={() => setMobileOpen(false)}
                    className={`block px-6 py-3 text-sm transition-colors ${
                      active
                        ? "text-[var(--accent)]"
                        : "text-[var(--muted)] hover:text-[var(--fg)]"
                    }`}
                  >
                    {label}
                  </Link>
                </li>
              );
            })}
          </motion.ul>
        )}
      </AnimatePresence>
    </motion.header>
  );
}

```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 08:21:55Z)**_

Build compiles clean. Phase 1 complete.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
CSS custom properties (`var(--bg)`, `var(--accent)`) are used directly in Tailwind utility values rather than extending the Tailwind theme config. This is the v4-preferred approach — no `tailwind.config.ts` needed, and the values are available to any CSS rule, not just Tailwind classes. The trade-off is no IDE autocompletion for these tokens, which is why meaningful names like `--accent` beat opaque color values.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 08:22:00Z)**_

Now Phase 2 — shared animation components, Hero, and About page. First the shared components:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
`useInView` with `{ once: true, margin: "-100px" }` triggers the animation when the element is 100px inside the viewport — avoiding premature fires when the element is technically "in view" at the very edge. `once: true` prevents re-animation on scroll-back, which feels more polished for content reveals (as opposed to looping indicators which should omit it).
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 08:22:03Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
"use client";

import { useRef } from "react";
import { motion, useInView } from "motion/react";

interface AnimatedSectionProps {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  className?: string;
  delay?: number;
}

export default function AnimatedSection({
  children,
  className,
  delay = 0,
}: AnimatedSectionProps) {
  const ref = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
  const isInView = useInView(ref, { once: true, margin: "-100px" });

  return (
    <motion.div
      ref={ref}
      initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 30 }}
      animate={isInView ? { opacity: 1, y: 0 } : { opacity: 0, y: 30 }}
      transition={{ duration: 0.5, ease: "easeOut", delay }}
      className={className}
    >
      {children}
    </motion.div>
  );
}

```

"use client";

import { motion } from "motion/react";

interface PageTransitionProps {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  className?: string;
}

// Wraps page content with a subtle fade+slide enter animation.
// Used in every page component's return value.
export default function PageTransition({
  children,
  className,
}: PageTransitionProps) {
  return (
    <motion.div
      initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 10 }}
      animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
      exit={{ opacity: 0, y: -10 }}
      transition={{ duration: 0.3, ease: "easeOut" }}
      className={className}
    >
      {children}
    </motion.div>
  );
}

```

"use client";

import { motion } from "motion/react";
import Link from "next/link";

const container = {
  hidden: {},
  show: {
    transition: {
      staggerChildren: 0.15,
      delayChildren: 0.1,
    },
  },
};

const item = {
  hidden: { opacity: 0, y: 24 },
  show: { opacity: 1, y: 0, transition: { duration: 0.5, ease: "easeOut" } },
};

export default function Hero() {
  return (
    <section className="flex min-h-[calc(100vh-4rem)] flex-col items-center justify-center px-6 py-20 text-center">
      <motion.div
        variants={container}
        initial="hidden"
        animate="show"
        className="flex flex-col items-center gap-6 max-w-2xl"
      >
        {/* Greeting */}
        <motion.p
          variants={item}
          className="font-mono text-sm tracking-widest text-[var(--accent)] uppercase"
        >
          Hi, I&apos;m
        </motion.p>

        {/* Name */}
        <motion.h1
          variants={item}
          className="text-5xl font-bold tracking-tight sm:text-7xl"
        >
          <span className="text-[var(--fg)]">Your </span>
          <span className="text-[var(--accent)]">Name</span>
        </motion.h1>

        {/* Role */}
        <motion.p
          variants={item}
          className="font-mono text-lg text-[var(--muted)]"
        >
          Software Engineer
        </motion.p>

        {/* Bio */}
        <motion.p
          variants={item}
          className="text-base text-[var(--muted)] max-w-lg leading-relaxed"
        >
          I build reliable backend systems, clean APIs, and the occasional
          polished frontend. Passionate about self-hosted infrastructure and
          developer tooling.
        </motion.p>

        {/* CTAs */}
        <motion.div variants={item} className="flex gap-4 flex-wrap justify-center mt-2">
          <Link
            href="/projects"
            className="rounded-md border border-[var(--accent)] px-5 py-2.5 text-sm font-medium text-[var(--accent)] transition-all hover:bg-[var(--accent-dim)] hover:shadow-[0_0_20px_oklch(74%_0.17_200_/_15%)]"
          >
            View Projects
          </Link>
          <Link
            href="/contact"
            className="rounded-md border border-[var(--border)] px-5 py-2.5 text-sm font-medium text-[var(--muted)] transition-colors hover:border-[var(--fg)] hover:text-[var(--fg)]"
          >
            Contact Me
          </Link>
        </motion.div>

        {/* Scroll indicator */}
        <motion.div
          variants={item}
          className="mt-8 flex flex-col items-center gap-2"
        >
          <motion.div
            animate={{ y: [0, 6, 0] }}
            transition={{ repeat: Infinity, duration: 1.5, ease: "easeInOut" }}
            className="flex flex-col gap-1 items-center text-[var(--border)]"
          >
            <span className="text-xs font-mono text-[var(--muted)]">scroll</span>
            <svg
              width="16"
              height="16"
              viewBox="0 0 24 24"
              fill="none"
              stroke="currentColor"
              strokeWidth="2"
              strokeLinecap="round"
              strokeLinejoin="round"
            >
              <polyline points="6 9 12 15 18 9" />
            </svg>
          </motion.div>
        </motion.div>
      </motion.div>
    </section>
  );
}

```

import AnimatedSection from "@/components/shared/AnimatedSection";
import Link from "next/link";

// Placeholder — replaced in Phase 3 with real MDX-driven data
const placeholderProjects = [
  {
    slug: "example-one",
    title: "Example Project One",
    description: "A short description of what this project does and what tech it uses.",
    tags: ["Next.js", "TypeScript", "PostgreSQL"],
  },
  {
    slug: "example-two",
    title: "Example Project Two",
    description: "Another project showcasing different skills and technologies.",
    tags: ["Go", "Docker", "Redis"],
  },
];

export default function FeaturedProjects() {
  return (
    <section className="mx-auto max-w-5xl px-6 py-20">
      <AnimatedSection>
        <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-10">
          <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-[var(--fg)]">
            Featured Projects
          </h2>
          <Link
            href="/projects"
            className="text-sm text-[var(--accent)] hover:underline font-mono"
          >
            View all →
          </Link>
        </div>
      </AnimatedSection>

      <div className="grid gap-4 sm:grid-cols-2">
        {placeholderProjects.map((project, i) => (
          <AnimatedSection key={project.slug} delay={i * 0.1}>
            <Link
              href={`/projects/${project.slug}`}
              className="block rounded-[var(--radius-card)] border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] p-6 transition-all hover:border-[var(--accent)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-alt)] group"
            >
              <h3 className="font-semibold text-[var(--fg)] mb-2 group-hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors">
                {project.title}
              </h3>
              <p className="text-sm text-[var(--muted)] mb-4 leading-relaxed">
                {project.description}
              </p>
              <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
                {project.tags.map((tag) => (
                  <span
                    key={tag}
                    className="text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--accent-dim)] text-[var(--accent)] font-mono"
                  >
                    {tag}
                  </span>
                ))}
              </div>
            </Link>
          </AnimatedSection>
        ))}
      </div>
    </section>
  );
}

```

      <div className="grid gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2">
        {skillCategories.map((cat) => (
          <div
            key={cat.name}
            className="rounded-[var(--radius-card)] border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] p-5"
          >
            <p className="text-xs text-[var(--accent)] font-mono uppercase tracking-widest mb-3">
              {cat.name}
            </p>
            <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
              {cat.skills.map((skill) => (
                <span
                  key={skill}
                  className="text-xs px-2 py-1 rounded-md bg-[var(--surface-alt)] text-[var(--muted)] font-mono"
                >
                  {skill}
                </span>
              ))}
            </div>
          </div>
        ))}
      </div>
    </AnimatedSection>
  );
}

```

}

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 08:23:43Z)**_

Phase 3 — MDX system. First the Zod schemas and MDX loader:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
`next-mdx-remote@6` renders MDX as a React Server Component — the MDX file is compiled to JSX at request time (or build time with `generateStaticParams`), avoiding the need to ship the MDX runtime to the browser. `gray-matter` strips YAML frontmatter before passing the content string to `MDXRemote`, since the MDX compiler would choke on YAML `---` delimiters.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

```typescript
import { z } from "zod";

// Zod v4 — error API changed: use .error() instead of .message() for custom messages
export const projectFrontmatterSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string(),
  slug: z.string(),
  description: z.string(),
  date: z.string(),
  tags: z.array(z.string()),
  thumbnail: z.string(),
  videoDemo: z.string().optional(),
  liveUrl: z.url().optional(),
  repoUrl: z.url().optional(),
  featured: z.boolean().default(false),
  status: z
    .enum(["completed", "in-progress", "archived"])
    .default("completed"),
});

export type ProjectFrontmatter = z.infer<typeof projectFrontmatterSchema>;

export const contactSchema = z.object({
  name: z.string().min(2).max(100),
  email: z.string().email(),
  message: z.string().min(10).max(2000),
});

export type ContactFormData = z.infer<typeof contactSchema>;

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```typescript
import fs from "fs";
import path from "path";
import matter from "gray-matter";
import { projectFrontmatterSchema, type ProjectFrontmatter } from "./schema";

const PROJECTS_DIR = path.join(process.cwd(), "src/content/projects");

export interface ProjectMeta extends ProjectFrontmatter {
  slug: string;
  content: string;
}

function safeParseProject(filePath: string): ProjectMeta | null {
  const raw = fs.readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8");
  const { data, content } = matter(raw);

  // Zod v4: parse() throws ZodError on failure
  const result = projectFrontmatterSchema.safeParse(data);
  if (!result.success) {
    console.warn(`[mdx] Invalid frontmatter in ${filePath}:`, result.error.issues);
    return null;
  }

  return { ...result.data, slug: result.data.slug, content };
}

export function getAllProjects(): ProjectMeta[] {
  if (!fs.existsSync(PROJECTS_DIR)) return [];

  const files = fs
    .readdirSync(PROJECTS_DIR)
    .filter((f) => f.endsWith(".mdx"));

  return files
    .map((file) => safeParseProject(path.join(PROJECTS_DIR, file)))
    .filter((p): p is ProjectMeta => p !== null)
    .sort((a, b) => new Date(b.date).getTime() - new Date(a.date).getTime());
}

export function getProjectBySlug(slug: string): ProjectMeta | null {
  if (!fs.existsSync(PROJECTS_DIR)) return null;

  const files = fs
    .readdirSync(PROJECTS_DIR)
    .filter((f) => f.endsWith(".mdx"));

  for (const file of files) {
    const project = safeParseProject(path.join(PROJECTS_DIR, file));
    if (project?.slug === slug) return project;
  }
  return null;
}

```

"use client";

interface ProjectVideoProps {
  src: string;
  poster?: string;
  className?: string;
}

// Native HTML5 video — Caddy serves this path directly with range request support.
// Do NOT use next/image or any streaming wrapper here.
export default function ProjectVideo({
  src,
  poster,
  className,
}: ProjectVideoProps) {
  return (
    <video
      src={src}
      poster={poster}
      controls
      preload="none"
      playsInline
      className={`w-full rounded-[var(--radius-card)] bg-[var(--surface)] ${className ?? ""}`}
    />
  );
}

import Link from "next/link";
import Image from "next/image";
import type { ProjectMeta } from "@/lib/mdx";

interface ProjectCardProps {
  project: ProjectMeta;
}

export default function ProjectCard({ project }: ProjectCardProps) {
  return (
    <Link
      href={`/projects/${project.slug}`}
      className="group relative flex flex-col rounded-[var(--radius-card)] border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] overflow-hidden transition-all hover:border-[var(--accent)] hover:shadow-[0_0_20px_oklch(74%_0.17_200_/_12%)]"
    >
      {/* Thumbnail */}
      <div className="relative aspect-video bg-[var(--surface-alt)] overflow-hidden">
        {project.thumbnail ? (
          <Image
            src={project.thumbnail}
            alt={project.title}
            fill
            className="object-cover transition-transform duration-500 group-hover:scale-105"
            sizes="(max-width: 640px) 100vw, (max-width: 1024px) 50vw, 33vw"
          />
        ) : (
          <div className="flex h-full items-center justify-center text-[var(--border)] text-xs font-mono">
            no preview
          </div>
        )}

        {/* Status badge */}
        {project.status !== "completed" && (
          <span className="absolute top-2 right-2 text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--surface)]/80 text-[var(--muted)] font-mono backdrop-blur-sm">
            {project.status}
          </span>
        )}
      </div>

      {/* Content */}
      <div className="flex flex-col flex-1 p-5">
        <h3 className="font-semibold text-[var(--fg)] mb-2 group-hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors line-clamp-2">
          {project.title}
        </h3>
        <p className="text-sm text-[var(--muted)] mb-4 flex-1 leading-relaxed line-clamp-3">
          {project.description}
        </p>

        {/* Tags */}
        <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-1.5 mb-4">
          {project.tags.map((tag) => (
            <span
              key={tag}
              className="text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--accent-dim)] text-[var(--accent)] font-mono"
            >
              {tag}
            </span>
          ))}
        </div>

        {/* Links row */}
        <div className="flex gap-3 text-xs font-mono text-[var(--muted)]">
          {project.liveUrl && (
            <a
              href={project.liveUrl}
              onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}
              target="_blank"
              rel="noopener noreferrer"
              className="hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors"
            >
              live ↗
            </a>
          )}
          {project.repoUrl && (
            <a
              href={project.repoUrl}
              onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}
              target="_blank"
              rel="noopener noreferrer"
              className="hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors"
            >
              repo ↗
            </a>
          )}
        </div>
      </div>
    </Link>
  );
}

```

"use client";

interface TagFilterProps {
  tags: string[];
  activeTag: string | null;
  onSelect: (tag: string | null) => void;
}

export default function TagFilter({ tags, activeTag, onSelect }: TagFilterProps) {
  return (
    <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2 mb-8">
      <button
        onClick={() => onSelect(null)}
        className={`text-xs px-3 py-1.5 rounded-full font-mono transition-colors ${
          activeTag === null
            ? "bg-[var(--accent)] text-[var(--bg)]"
            : "bg-[var(--surface)] text-[var(--muted)] border border-[var(--border)] hover:border-[var(--accent)] hover:text-[var(--accent)]"
        }`}
      >
        All
      </button>
      {tags.map((tag) => (
        <button
          key={tag}
          onClick={() => onSelect(tag === activeTag ? null : tag)}
          className={`text-xs px-3 py-1.5 rounded-full font-mono transition-colors ${
            activeTag === tag
              ? "bg-[var(--accent)] text-[var(--bg)]"
              : "bg-[var(--surface)] text-[var(--muted)] border border-[var(--border)] hover:border-[var(--accent)] hover:text-[var(--accent)]"
          }`}
        >
          {tag}
        </button>
      ))}
    </div>
  );
}

```

```tsx
"use client";

import { useState } from "react";
import type { ProjectMeta } from "@/lib/mdx";
import ProjectCard from "./ProjectCard";
import TagFilter from "./TagFilter";
import AnimatedSection from "@/components/shared/AnimatedSection";

interface ProjectGridProps {
  projects: ProjectMeta[];
}

export default function ProjectGrid({ projects }: ProjectGridProps) {
  const [activeTag, setActiveTag] = useState<string | null>(null);

  // Collect all unique tags from all projects
  const allTags = Array.from(
    new Set(projects.flatMap((p) => p.tags))
  ).sort();

  const filtered = activeTag
    ? projects.filter((p) => p.tags.includes(activeTag))
    : projects;

  return (
    <div>
      {allTags.length > 0 && (
        <TagFilter tags={allTags} activeTag={activeTag} onSelect={setActiveTag} />
      )}

      {filtered.length === 0 ? (
        <p className="text-[var(--muted)] text-sm font-mono">
          No projects match the selected filter.
        </p>
      ) : (
        <div className="grid gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3">
          {filtered.map((project, i) => (
            <AnimatedSection key={project.slug} delay={i * 0.05}>
              <ProjectCard project={project} />
            </AnimatedSection>
          ))}
        </div>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}

```

import Link from "next/link";
import Image from "next/image";
import type { ProjectFrontmatter } from "@/lib/schema";
import ProjectVideo from "@/components/projects/ProjectVideo";

interface MDXLayoutProps {
  frontmatter: ProjectFrontmatter;
  children: React.ReactNode;
}

export default function MDXLayout({ frontmatter, children }: MDXLayoutProps) {
  const { title, date, tags, thumbnail, videoDemo, liveUrl, repoUrl, status } =
    frontmatter;

  return (
    <article className="mx-auto max-w-3xl px-6 py-20">
      {/* Back link */}
      <Link
        href="/projects"
        className="inline-flex items-center gap-1 font-mono text-xs text-[var(--muted)] hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors mb-8"
      >
        ← Back to projects
      </Link>

      {/* Header */}
      <header className="mb-10">
        <div className="flex items-center gap-3 mb-4">
          <time
            dateTime={date}
            className="font-mono text-xs text-[var(--muted)]"
          >
            {new Date(date).toLocaleDateString("en-US", {
              year: "numeric",
              month: "long",
            })}
          </time>
          {status !== "completed" && (
            <span className="text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full border border-[var(--border)] text-[var(--muted)] font-mono">
              {status}
            </span>
          )}
        </div>

        <h1 className="text-4xl font-bold text-[var(--fg)] mb-4">{title}</h1>

        {/* Tags */}
        <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2 mb-6">
          {tags.map((tag) => (
            <span
              key={tag}
              className="text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--accent-dim)] text-[var(--accent)] font-mono"
            >
              {tag}
            </span>
          ))}
        </div>

        {/* External links */}
        <div className="flex gap-4 text-sm font-mono text-[var(--muted)]">
          {liveUrl && (
            <a
              href={liveUrl}
              target="_blank"
              rel="noopener noreferrer"
              className="hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors"
            >
              View live ↗
            </a>
          )}
          {repoUrl && (
            <a
              href={repoUrl}
              target="_blank"
              rel="noopener noreferrer"
              className="hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors"
            >
              Source code ↗
            </a>
          )}
        </div>
      </header>

      {/* Hero media */}
      {videoDemo ? (
        <div className="mb-10">
          <ProjectVideo
            src={videoDemo}
            poster={thumbnail}
          />
        </div>
      ) : thumbnail ? (
        <div className="relative aspect-video mb-10 overflow-hidden rounded-[var(--radius-card)] bg-[var(--surface)]">
          <Image
            src={thumbnail}
            alt={title}
            fill
            className="object-cover"
            priority
            sizes="(max-width: 768px) 100vw, 768px"
          />
        </div>
      ) : null}

      {/* MDX prose content */}
      <div className="prose dark:prose-invert max-w-none prose-pre:bg-[var(--surface)] prose-pre:border prose-pre:border-[var(--border)] prose-code:font-mono prose-code:text-[var(--accent)] prose-a:text-[var(--accent)] prose-a:no-underline hover:prose-a:underline">
        {children}
      </div>

import { notFound } from "next/navigation";
import type { Metadata } from "next";
import { MDXRemote } from "next-mdx-remote/rsc";
import { getAllProjects, getProjectBySlug } from "@/lib/mdx";
import MDXLayout from "@/components/mdx/MDXLayout";
import { mdxComponents } from "@/components/mdx/MDXComponents";
import PageTransition from "@/components/shared/PageTransition";

// In Next.js 15+/16, params is a Promise — must be awaited
interface PageProps {
  params: Promise<{ slug: string }>;
}

export async function generateStaticParams() {
  const projects = getAllProjects();
  return projects.map((p) => ({ slug: p.slug }));
}

export async function generateMetadata({ params }: PageProps): Promise<Metadata> {
  const { slug } = await params;
  const project = getProjectBySlug(slug);

  if (!project) return { title: "Project Not Found" };

  return {
    title: project.title,
    description: project.description,
    openGraph: {
      title: project.title,
      description: project.description,
      images: project.thumbnail ? [{ url: project.thumbnail }] : [],
    },
  };
}

export default async function ProjectPage({ params }: PageProps) {
  const { slug } = await params;
  const project = getProjectBySlug(slug);

  if (!project) notFound();

  return (
    <PageTransition>
      <MDXLayout frontmatter={project}>
        <MDXRemote source={project.content} components={mdxComponents} />
      </MDXLayout>
    </PageTransition>
  );
}

```

```mdx
---
title: "Self-Hosted Homelab Setup"
slug: "homelab-setup"
description: "A fully self-hosted personal infrastructure — Caddy, Docker, Cloudflare tunnels, and blue/green deployments on a single Linux machine."
date: "2025-11-01"
tags: ["Docker", "Caddy", "Linux", "Cloudflare", "Self-hosted"]
thumbnail: "/media/projects/homelab-setup/thumbnail.jpg"
featured: true
status: "completed"
---

## Overview

This project documents my self-hosted homelab setup — everything from the base OS config to automated blue/green deployments and Cloudflare Tunnel integration.

## What I Built

- **Caddy** as the reverse proxy with automatic TLS via Cloudflare tunnels
- **Docker** containerisation for all services
- **Blue/Green deployment** via a shell script that health-checks the new container before swapping Caddy's upstream
- **Cloudflared** tunnel for zero-exposure ingress — no open ports on the router

## Architecture

The deployment pipeline looks like this:

1. Run `deploy.sh` locally
2. Docker builds a new image tagged with the git commit SHA
3. The new container starts on the inactive port (3001 or 3002)
4. Health check passes → Caddy's config is regenerated with `sed` and reloaded
5. Old container is stopped after a 5-second drain window

## Lessons Learned

The biggest surprise was how reliable Caddy is for this use case — zero SSL configuration needed, and the `caddy reload` command atomically swaps configurations without dropping connections.

```

Media files (images and videos) are volume-mounted and served directly by Caddy, which handles HTTP range requests natively for video seeking.

```typescript
// In-memory rate limiter — suitable for single-instance Docker deployments.
// Resets on process restart, which is acceptable for a personal portfolio.
// Limit: 3 requests per IP per 15 minutes.

interface RateEntry {
  count: number;
  resetAt: number;
}

const LIMIT = 3;
const WINDOW_MS = 15 * 60 * 1000; // 15 minutes

const store = new Map<string, RateEntry>();

export function checkRateLimit(ip: string): {
  allowed: boolean;
  remaining: number;
  resetAt: number;
} {
  const now = Date.now();
  const entry = store.get(ip);

  if (!entry || now > entry.resetAt) {
    // First request in this window (or window expired)
    const newEntry: RateEntry = { count: 1, resetAt: now + WINDOW_MS };
    store.set(ip, newEntry);
    return { allowed: true, remaining: LIMIT - 1, resetAt: newEntry.resetAt };
  }

  if (entry.count >= LIMIT) {
    return { allowed: false, remaining: 0, resetAt: entry.resetAt };
  }

  entry.count += 1;
  return {
    allowed: true,
    remaining: LIMIT - entry.count,
    resetAt: entry.resetAt,
  };
}

```

```typescript
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { Resend } from "resend";
import { contactSchema } from "@/lib/schema";
import { checkRateLimit } from "@/lib/rateLimit";

const resend = new Resend(process.env.RESEND_API_KEY);

export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
  // Extract real client IP (Caddy sets X-Forwarded-For)
  const ip =
    request.headers.get("x-forwarded-for")?.split(",")[0].trim() ??
    "unknown";

  const { allowed, resetAt } = checkRateLimit(ip);

  if (!allowed) {
    const retryAfter = Math.ceil((resetAt - Date.now()) / 1000);
    return NextResponse.json(
      {
        error: "Too many requests. Please wait before sending another message.",
        retryAfter,
      },
      {
        status: 429,
        headers: { "Retry-After": String(retryAfter) },
      }
    );
  }

  let body: unknown;
  try {
    body = await request.json();
  } catch {
    return NextResponse.json({ error: "Invalid request body." }, { status: 400 });
  }

  const result = contactSchema.safeParse(body);
  if (!result.success) {
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Validation failed.", issues: result.error.issues },
      { status: 422 }
    );
  }

  const { name, email, message } = result.data;
  const emailEnabled = process.env.EMAIL_ENABLED !== "false";

  if (!emailEnabled) {
    // Dev mode — log instead of sending
    console.log("[contact] Email sending disabled (EMAIL_ENABLED=false)");
    console.log(`[contact] From: ${name} <${email}>`);
    console.log(`[contact] Message: ${message}`);
    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });
  }

  const fromEmail =
    process.env.RESEND_FROM_EMAIL ?? "onboarding@resend.dev";
  const toEmail = process.env.RESEND_TO_EMAIL;

  if (!toEmail) {
    console.error("[contact] RESEND_TO_EMAIL is not set");
    return NextResponse.json({ error: "Server configuration error." }, { status: 500 });
  }

  const { error } = await resend.emails.send({
    from: `Portfolio Contact <${fromEmail}>`,
    to: [toEmail],
    replyTo: email,
    subject: `Portfolio contact from ${name}`,
    text: `Name: ${name}\nEmail: ${email}\n\nMessage:\n${message}`,
    html: `
      <p><strong>Name:</strong> ${name}</p>
      <p><strong>Email:</strong> <a href="mailto:${email}">${email}</a></p>
      <hr />
      <p>${message.replace(/\n/g, "<br>")}</p>
    `,
  });

  if (error) {
    console.error("[contact] Resend error:", error);
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Failed to send message. Please try again later." },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }

  return NextResponse.json({ success: true });
}

```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 08:25:52Z)**_

Now the ContactForm client component. Note that `@hookform/resolvers@5` uses a slightly different import path for Zod v4 integration:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
`@hookform/resolvers@5` updated its Zod integration to match Zod v4's new `.safeParse()` return shape. The import path stays the same (`zodResolver` from `@hookform/resolvers/zod`), but internally it now handles Zod v4's restructured `ZodError.issues` instead of `ZodError.errors`. This is why resolvers@3 silently breaks with Zod v4 — no runtime crash, just wrong field error paths.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 08:26:04Z)**_

<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
"use client";

import { useState } from "react";
import { useForm } from "react-hook-form";
import { zodResolver } from "@hookform/resolvers/zod";
import { motion, AnimatePresence } from "motion/react";
import { contactSchema, type ContactFormData } from "@/lib/schema";

type SubmitState = "idle" | "submitting" | "success" | "error" | "rate-limited";

export default function ContactForm() {
  const [submitState, setSubmitState] = useState<SubmitState>("idle");
  const [errorMsg, setErrorMsg] = useState("");

  const {
    register,
    handleSubmit,
    formState: { errors, isSubmitting },
    reset,
  } = useForm<ContactFormData>({
    resolver: zodResolver(contactSchema),
  });

  async function onSubmit(data: ContactFormData) {
    setSubmitState("submitting");

    try {
      const res = await fetch("/api/contact", {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify(data),
      });

      if (res.status === 429) {
        const json = await res.json();
        setSubmitState("rate-limited");
        setErrorMsg(json.error ?? "Too many requests. Please wait.");
        return;
      }

      if (!res.ok) {
        const json = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));
        setSubmitState("error");
        setErrorMsg(json.error ?? "Something went wrong. Please try again.");
        return;
      }

      setSubmitState("success");
      reset();
    } catch {
      setSubmitState("error");
      setErrorMsg("Network error. Please check your connection.");
    }
  }

  const inputClass =
    "w-full rounded-md border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] px-4 py-2.5 text-sm text-[var(--fg)] placeholder:text-[var(--muted)] transition-colors focus:border-[var(--accent)] focus:outline-none";

  const labelClass = "block text-xs font-mono text-[var(--muted)] mb-1.5";

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit(onSubmit)} noValidate className="space-y-5">
      {/* Name */}
      <div>
        <label htmlFor="name" className={labelClass}>
          name *
        </label>
        <input
          id="name"
          type="text"
          autoComplete="name"
          placeholder="Your name"
          {...register("name")}
          className={inputClass}
          aria-invalid={!!errors.name}
        />
        {errors.name && (
          <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-red-400 font-mono">
            {errors.name.message}
          </p>
        )}
      </div>

      {/* Email */}
      <div>
        <label htmlFor="email" className={labelClass}>
          email *
        </label>
        <input
          id="email"
          type="email"
          autoComplete="email"
          placeholder="you@example.com"
          {...register("email")}
          className={inputClass}
          aria-invalid={!!errors.email}
        />
        {errors.email && (
          <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-red-400 font-mono">
            {errors.email.message}
          </p>
        )}
      </div>

      {/* Message */}
      <div>
        <label htmlFor="message" className={labelClass}>
          message *
        </label>
        <textarea
          id="message"
          rows={5}
          placeholder="What's on your mind?"
          {...register("message")}
          className={`${inputClass} resize-none`}
          aria-invalid={!!errors.message}
        />
        {errors.message && (
          <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-red-400 font-mono">
            {errors.message.message}
          </p>
        )}
      </div>

      {/* Submit */}
      <button
        type="submit"
        disabled={isSubmitting}
        className="w-full rounded-md border border-[var(--accent)] px-5 py-2.5 text-sm font-medium text-[var(--accent)] transition-all hover:bg-[var(--accent-dim)] disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed"
      >
        {isSubmitting ? "Sending..." : "Send Message"}
      </button>

      {/* Feedback messages */}
      <AnimatePresence mode="wait">
        {submitState === "success" && (
          <motion.p
            key="success"
            initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 4 }}
            animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
            exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
            className="text-sm text-green-400 font-mono text-center"
          >
            ✓ Message sent. I&apos;ll get back to you soon.
          </motion.p>
        )}
        {(submitState === "error" || submitState === "rate-limited") && (
          <motion.p
            key="error"
            initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 4 }}
            animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
            exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
            className="text-sm text-red-400 font-mono text-center"
          >
            {errorMsg}
          </motion.p>
        )}
      </AnimatePresence>
    </form>
  );
}

```

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user wants a complete self-hosted software engineer portfolio website built from scratch at `/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/`. Requirements include:
   - Pages: Home, About, Projects, Contact
   - Light/dark mode toggle (default to system `prefers-color-scheme`)
   - Animations and polished layout (dark techy/minimal aesthetic)
   - SEO basics (meta tags, sitemap, robots, OG tags, JSON-LD)
   - Contact form with email integration (Resend)
   - Media handling for locally hosted images and videos
   - Dockerized with blue/green deployment using a manual `deploy.sh` script
   - Caddy as reverse proxy (on host, not in Docker)
   - Cloudflared tunnel (template config, domain not yet purchased)
   - MDX files for project content management

   **Confirmed decisions from Q&A:**
   - Role: Software Engineer
   - Email: Resend
   - Frontend: Next.js
   - Blue/green: Manual script
   - Design: Dark techy/minimal
   - Content: MDX files

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Next.js 16.2.3** with App Router, TypeScript, `output: "standalone"` for Docker
   - **Tailwind CSS v4** — CSS-first configuration (`@import "tailwindcss"`, `@custom-variant dark`, `@theme {}` blocks, no `tailwind.config.ts` needed), Lightning CSS handles autoprefixing, `@tailwindcss/postcss` plugin
   - **Motion v12** (formerly Framer Motion) — import from `motion/react` (NOT `framer-motion`), `Variants` type must be explicitly imported for TypeScript
   - **next-mdx-remote@6** — RSC rendering via `next-mdx-remote/rsc`, `MDXRemote` component, React 19 support
   - **Zod v4** — `z.url()` for URL validation, `safeParse()` returns `{success, data, error}`, error uses `.issues` not `.errors`
   - **@hookform/resolvers@5** — required for Zod v4 compatibility (v3 breaks silently)
   - **next-themes** — requires `suppressHydrationWarning` on `<html>`, `defaultTheme="system"`, `enableSystem`, `attribute="class"`
   - **`@custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *))`** — zero-specificity dark mode variant matching next-themes' class placement
   - **params as Promise** — In Next.js 15+/16, `params` in page components and `generateMetadata` is `Promise<{slug: string}>`, must be `await`ed
   - **Docker standalone output** — `.next/standalone/server.js` is the runtime; `public/media/` excluded from image and volume-mounted at runtime
   - **Blue/green via sed** — `infra/Caddyfile.tmpl` with `__PORT__` placeholder; `deploy.sh` runs `sed "s/__PORT__/$NEW_PORT/" infra/Caddyfile.tmpl > infra/Caddyfile` then `caddy reload`; `ACTIVE_PORT=... caddy reload` is unreliable
   - **Caddy media passthrough** — `handle /media/*` with `file_server` serves files directly to support HTTP range requests for video seeking (206 Partial Content)
   - **In-memory rate limiting** — `Map<string, {count, resetAt}>`, 3 req/15 min per IP from `x-forwarded-for` header
   - **gray-matter** — strips YAML frontmatter before passing MDX content to `MDXRemote`
   - **node:24-alpine** — confirmed current LTS as of April 2026 (user explicitly corrected node:22 to node:24)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`/home/debiantreeb/.claude/plans/jazzy-petting-wand.md`**

     - `"use client"`, sticky with `backdrop-blur-md`, `usePathname()` for active state, mobile hamburger with animated bars via motion variants, AnimatePresence for dropdown

   - **`/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/src/components/shared/PageTransition.tsx`**
     - `"use client"`, `motion.div` with `initial/animate/exit` variants

   - **`/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/src/components/home/Hero.tsx`**
     - `"use client"`, stagger container + item variants, **must use `const item: Variants = {...}` with explicit type** (plain object infers `ease` as `string` which breaks Motion v12 TypeScript)
     ```typescript
     import { motion, type Variants } from "motion/react";
     const item: Variants = {
       hidden: { opacity: 0, y: 24 },
       show: { opacity: 1, y: 0, transition: { duration: 0.5, ease: "easeOut" } },
     };
     ```

   - **`/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/src/components/home/FeaturedProjects.tsx`**
     - Server Component (no `"use client"`), placeholder project cards, links to `/projects/[slug]`

   - **`/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/src/components/about/Bio.tsx`**, **`SkillsGrid.tsx`**, **`Timeline.tsx`**
     - Server Components using `AnimatedSection` wrapper
     - Timeline has vertical line + animated dot markers

   - **`/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/src/lib/schema.ts`**
     - Zod v4 schemas; uses `z.url()` not `z.string().url()` (Zod v4 syntax change)
     ```typescript
     export const projectFrontmatterSchema = z.object({
       liveUrl: z.url().optional(),
       repoUrl: z.url().optional(),
       // ...
     });
     export const contactSchema = z.object({
       name: z.string().min(2).max(100),
       email: z.string().email(),
       message: z.string().min(10).max(2000),
     });
     ```

   - **`/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/src/lib/mdx.ts`**
     - Reads MDX files from `src/content/projects/`, uses `gray-matter` + Zod safeParse
     - `getAllProjects()` returns sorted by date desc; `getProjectBySlug(slug)` finds by slug field

   - **`/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/src/components/projects/ProjectCard.tsx`**
     - Server Component, `next/image` for thumbnail with `fill` layout, tag pills, live/repo links with `e.stopPropagation()`

     export async function generateMetadata({ params }: { params: Promise<{ slug: string }> }): Promise<Metadata> {

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **create-next-app conflict**: Running `npx create-next-app@latest .` in `/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/` failed because `.specstory/` directory (Claude Code metadata) was treated as a conflicting file.
     - Fix: Scaffolded to `/tmp/portfolio-temp/` instead, then used `cp -r` to copy all files (excluding `node_modules` and `.git`) to the portfolio directory.

   - **Motion v12 TypeScript error in Hero.tsx**: `ease: "easeOut"` was inferred as `string` type by TypeScript, but Motion v12 requires the `Easing` type. Build error: "Types of property 'ease' are incompatible. Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'Easing | Easing[] | undefined'."
     - Fix: Changed `const item = {...}` to `const item: Variants = {...}` and added `type Variants` to the import: `import { motion, type Variants } from "motion/react";`

   - **Plan auditing rounds**: Multiple plan issues were caught through 4 audit passes before implementation:
     - Invalid Caddyfile syntax (`{$ACTIVE_PORT:3001}` → `{$ACTIVE_PORT}`, then later switched to sed template entirely)
     - Outdated package versions (Next.js 14→15→16, Node 22→24, tailwindcss@3→4, framer-motion→motion, etc.)
     - `@hookform/resolvers@3` incompatible with Zod v4 → needs `@5`
     - `ACTIVE_PORT=... caddy reload` unreliable → switched to `sed`+`Caddyfile.tmpl` approach

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Blue/green Caddy swap**: Originally planned to use `ACTIVE_PORT=X caddy reload` but auditing revealed this is unreliable (caddy reload doesn't guarantee env var substitution). Solution: maintain `infra/Caddyfile.tmpl` with `__PORT__` placeholder in git, generate `infra/Caddyfile` via `sed` on each deploy, then `caddy reload --config infra/Caddyfile --force`.
   - **Media serving**: Large video files served directly by Caddy via `handle /media/* { file_server }` — this ensures HTTP 206 range requests work for video seeking without buffering through Node.js.
   - **next-themes hydration**: `suppressHydrationWarning` on `<html>`, `mounted` guard in ThemeToggle, system preference via `defaultTheme="system"` + `enableSystem`.
   - **params Promise in Next.js 16**: All `[slug]` pages must `await params` before accessing properties.

6. All user messages:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The contact page is a Server Component that simply renders a static layout around the `ContactForm` client component. This is the standard Next.js pattern: keep the page itself as a Server Component for metadata export and SEO, and push interactivity down into a leaf "use client" component. The boundary is at `ContactForm` — everything above it is rendered on the server.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 08:28:47Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 08:28:50Z)**_

The Resend client is instantiated at module level — it throws immediately at build time when `RESEND_API_KEY` is missing. The fix is to lazy-initialize it inside the handler, after the `emailEnabled` guard.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Next.js evaluates API route modules during the build's "collecting page data" phase to extract static metadata. Any top-level `new SDK(process.env.SECRET)` that throws on a missing key will crash the build — even though API routes are never actually *called* at build time. The pattern is to defer SDK instantiation to inside the handler function (or inside the branch that needs it), so it only executes at request time.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 08:29:28Z)**_

All pages already have metadata. Now let me create the three missing SEO pieces — `sitemap.ts`, `robots.ts`, and the OG image placeholder:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Next.js has built-in conventions for `sitemap.ts` and `robots.ts` — exporting the right function signature causes Next.js to generate `/sitemap.xml` and `/robots.txt` automatically at build time (static) or on-demand. This is preferable to creating static XML files because `sitemap.ts` can call `getAllProjects()` to dynamically include every MDX-driven project page without manual updates.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 08:29:31Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```typescript
import type { MetadataRoute } from "next";
import { getAllProjects } from "@/lib/mdx";

export default function sitemap(): MetadataRoute.Sitemap {
  const base = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL ?? "http://localhost:3000";

  const staticRoutes: MetadataRoute.Sitemap = [
    { url: base, lastModified: new Date(), changeFrequency: "monthly", priority: 1 },
    { url: `${base}/about`, lastModified: new Date(), changeFrequency: "monthly", priority: 0.8 },
    { url: `${base}/projects`, lastModified: new Date(), changeFrequency: "weekly", priority: 0.9 },
    { url: `${base}/contact`, lastModified: new Date(), changeFrequency: "yearly", priority: 0.5 },
  ];

  const projectRoutes: MetadataRoute.Sitemap = getAllProjects().map((p) => ({
    url: `${base}/projects/${p.slug}`,
    lastModified: new Date(p.date),
    changeFrequency: "monthly",
    priority: 0.7,
  }));

  return [...staticRoutes, ...projectRoutes];
}

```

import type { MetadataRoute } from "next";

export default function robots(): MetadataRoute.Robots {
  const base = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL ?? "http://localhost:3000";

  return {
    rules: { userAgent: "*", allow: "/" },
    sitemap: `${base}/sitemap.xml`,
  };
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Generate minimal 1200x630 PNG placeholder for OG image using Python stdlib

```bash
python3 - << 'EOF'
import struct, zlib, os

def create_png(width, height, bg_color, output_path):
    """Create a minimal solid-color PNG using only stdlib."""
    def chunk(tag, data):
        c = struct.pack(">I", len(data)) + tag + data
        return c + struct.pack(">I", zlib.crc32(tag + data) & 0xFFFFFFFF)

    # One row: filter byte (0) + RGB pixels
    row = bytes([0]) + bytes(bg_color * width)
    raw = row * height
    idat_data = zlib.compress(raw, 9)

    png = (
        b'\x89PNG\r\n\x1a\n'
        + chunk(b'IHDR', struct.pack(">IIBBBBB", width, height, 8, 2, 0, 0, 0))
        + chunk(b'IDAT', idat_data)
        + chunk(b'IEND', b'')
    )

    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(output_path), exist_ok=True)
    with open(output_path, 'wb') as f:
        f.write(png)
    print(f"Created {output_path} ({len(png)} bytes)")

# Dark zinc background (#18181b = rgb(24,24,27))
create_png(1200, 630, [24, 24, 27], "/home/debiantreeb/portfolio/public/og-default.png")
EOF
```

Created /home/debiantreeb/portfolio/public/og-default.png (3160 bytes)
```

1	import { notFound } from "next/navigation";
2	import type { Metadata } from "next";
3	import { MDXRemote } from "next-mdx-remote/rsc";
4	import { getAllProjects, getProjectBySlug } from "@/lib/mdx";
5	import MDXLayout from "@/components/mdx/MDXLayout";
6	import { mdxComponents } from "@/components/mdx/MDXComponents";
7	import PageTransition from "@/components/shared/PageTransition";
8	
9	// In Next.js 15+/16, params is a Promise — must be awaited
10	interface PageProps {
11	  params: Promise<{ slug: string }>;
12	}
13	
14	export async function generateStaticParams() {
15	  const projects = getAllProjects();
16	  return projects.map((p) => ({ slug: p.slug }));
17	}
18	
19	export async function generateMetadata({ params }: PageProps): Promise<Metadata> {
20	  const { slug } = await params;
21	  const project = getProjectBySlug(slug);
22	
23	  if (!project) return { title: "Project Not Found" };
24	
25	  return {
26	    title: project.title,
27	    description: project.description,
28	    openGraph: {
29	      title: project.title,
30	      description: project.description,
31	      images: project.thumbnail ? [{ url: project.thumbnail }] : [],
32	    },
33	  };
34	}
35	
36	export default async function ProjectPage({ params }: PageProps) {
37	  const { slug } = await params;
38	  const project = getProjectBySlug(slug);
39	
40	  if (!project) notFound();
41	
42	  return (
43	    <PageTransition>
44	      <MDXLayout frontmatter={project}>
45	        <MDXRemote source={project.content} components={mdxComponents} />
46	      </MDXLayout>
47	    </PageTransition>
48	  );
49	}
