_**User (2026-04-14 13:36:13Z)**_

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

The session started as a continuation from a previous conversation that had built a complete self-hosted portfolio website for Thomas (Tom) Swart, PhD — a Data Engineer / Founder. The work in this session covered:

**Phase completion work:**
- Complete Phase 4 (contact form) by updating contact page and fixing a build-time crash in the Resend API route
- Phase 5 (SEO: sitemap.ts, robots.ts, og-default.png)
- Phase 6 (Docker, docker-compose, Caddyfile.tmpl, deploy.sh, cloudflared template)
- Phase 7 (polish, new-project.sh script, media gitkeep, README)

**Audit and container testing:** User asked to audit the work and run the container locally to test it.

**Portfolio personalisation:** User provided CV files (`DE_CV_Upwork.pdf` and `Comaea DE - TS - 2025.txt`) to personalise the portfolio. After 3 plan audit rounds, changes were implemented. User also provided avatar photo, Grocery Lens logo SVG, and requested Grocery Lens be added as a project.

**Most recent user message (what was being actively worked on):** User reported several issues and requested changes:
1. Fix `<a>` inside `<a>` HTML error in ProjectCard (nested link bug)
2. Navbar still shows `> yourname_` — should this be there? What does it represent?
3. Make Grocery Lens the featured project at the top
4. Add D3 implementation as its own mini-project — what thumbnails to use for other projects?
5. Add RESTful API and PDF ingestion to Ingestion skills
6. Add AWS DevOps infrastructure skills (EC2, CloudWatch/"cloudstream" etc.)

---

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- **Next.js 16.2.3** App Router, `output: "standalone"`, `params` as Promise in dynamic routes
- **Tailwind CSS v4** — CSS-first `@theme`, `@custom-variant dark`, no tailwind.config.ts
- **Motion v12** — imports from `motion/react`, `type Variants` must be explicit
- **next-mdx-remote@6** — RSC rendering via `next-mdx-remote/rsc`
- **Zod v4** — `z.url()` not `z.string().url()`, `@hookform/resolvers@5` required
- **next-themes** — `suppressHydrationWarning` on `<html>`, `mounted` guard in ThemeToggle
- **Blue/green deployment** — `infra/Caddyfile.tmpl` with `__PORT__` placeholder; `deploy.sh` uses `sed` + `caddy reload`; `eval` is unsafe, `"$@"` array form used instead
- **Caddy media passthrough** — `handle /media/*` with `file_server` for HTTP 206 range requests
- **In-memory rate limiting** — `Map<string, {count, resetAt}>`, 3 req/15 min, `.unref()` cleanup
- **`next/image` + Caddy media bug** — `/_next/image?url=/media/...` makes internal fetch but `/media/` is deleted from Docker image and served by Caddy. Fix: `unoptimized` prop on all `<Image>` components referencing `/media/` paths
- **Nested `<a>` HTML bug** — `ProjectCard` wraps entire card in `<Link>` (renders `<a>`) but also has `<a>` tags for liveUrl/repoUrl inside. Fix: overlay link pattern (transparent absolute-positioned `<Link>` at z-0, content at z-10)
- **Build-time SDK init** — `new Resend(key)` at module level throws when key is unset at build time (page data collection phase). Fix: lazy init inside handler
- **`NEXT_PUBLIC_` baked at build time** — sitemap.ts and robots.ts used `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` but were statically generated. Fix: `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"`
- **XSS in email template** — user-controlled fields interpolated raw into HTML string. Fix: `escapeHtml()` utility
- **WSL media file access** — Windows files at `C:\Users\liltimmy\...` accessible via `/mnt/c/Users/liltimmy/...`

---

3. Files and Code Sections:

- **`src/app/contact/page.tsx`** — Updated from stub to full page with `<ContactForm />`. Added description header section.

- **`src/app/api/contact/route.ts`** — Fixed build-time crash (moved `new Resend()` inside handler). Added `escapeHtml()` function. All three user fields (`name`, `email`, `message`) are now escaped before HTML interpolation.
  ```typescript
  function escapeHtml(str: string): string {
    return str
      .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
      .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
      .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
      .replace(/"/g, "&quot;")
      .replace(/'/g, "&#x27;");
  }
  // Inside handler:
  const resend = new Resend(process.env.RESEND_API_KEY); // lazy init
  html: `<p><strong>Name:</strong> ${escapeHtml(name)}</p>...`
  ```

- **`src/lib/rateLimit.ts`** — Added stale-entry cleanup via `setInterval`:
  ```typescript
  setInterval(() => {
    const now = Date.now();
    for (const [ip, entry] of store) {
      if (now > entry.resetAt) store.delete(ip);
    }
  }, CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS).unref();
  ```

- **`src/app/sitemap.ts`** — Created with `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"` so it reads `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` at request time. Calls `getAllProjects()` to include all MDX slugs.

---

4. Errors and fixes:

- **Build crash: `new Resend(undefined)` at module level** — Resend constructor throws when API key is unset. During Next.js build "collecting page data" phase, modules are evaluated. Fix: moved `new Resend(process.env.RESEND_API_KEY)` from module level into the POST handler body, after the `emailEnabled` check.

- **Rate limiter unbounded Map growth** — Expired entries never cleaned up. Fix: `setInterval` with `.unref()` every 30 minutes deletes entries where `Date.now() > entry.resetAt`.

- **XSS in HTML email template** — User-controlled `name`, `email`, `message` were raw-interpolated into the `html:` field sent to Resend. Fix: added `escapeHtml()` function escaping `&`, `<`, `>`, `"`, `'`.

- **deploy.sh `eval "$@"` unsafe** — `eval` is dangerous with paths containing spaces. Fix: rewrote `run()` to use `"$@"` array form (no eval), handled redirects (`>`) inline with explicit dry-run if/else blocks. `DEPLOY_TAG` env var passed via `env DEPLOY_TAG=... docker compose ...` prefix.

- **sitemap/robots URLs hardcoded to localhost:3000** — `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` is baked at build time; statically generated sitemap used the build-time value (unset → localhost:3000). Fix: added `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"` to both files so they read the env var at request time from the running container.

- **`next/image` + Caddy media 404 in production** — `next/image` optimiser makes internal request to `/_next/image?url=/media/...` which goes to Next.js process, but `/media/` files are served by Caddy and deleted from the Docker image. Internal fetch gets 404. Fix: add `unoptimized` prop to all `<Image>` components with `/media/` src paths. Applied to `ProjectCard.tsx`. Bio.tsx avatar also uses `unoptimized`.

- **Nested `<a>` inside `<a>` in ProjectCard** — `ProjectCard` is wrapped in `<Link>` (renders `<a>`), and inside has `<a>` tags for `liveUrl`/`repoUrl`. Invalid HTML, causes hydration error. Fix needed: overlay link pattern — change outer element to `<div>`, add absolutely-positioned transparent `<Link>` at z-0, keep inner links at z-10. **NOT YET FIXED.**

- **Hero.tsx bio text Match Error** — First Edit attempt used `&amp;` HTML entity to match `&` in source, but the actual file contained literal `&`. Fixed by re-reading the file and using the exact literal string.

- **Bio.tsx "file modified" error** — Bio.tsx had been modified by user/linter between initial read and edit attempt. Fixed by re-reading before writing.

- **Plan audit: "co-led" vs "led"** — User corrected: "say I led DPS, not co-led it". Updated all timeline and description text accordingly.

- **FeaturedProjects still using placeholder data** — Discovered while explaining content editing. Fixed to call `getAllProjects().filter(p => p.featured).slice(0, 4)`.

- **Duplicate `key` prop on FeaturedProjects** — After fixing FeaturedProjects, accidentally added `key` to both outer `AnimatedSection` and inner `Link`. Fixed by removing duplicate.

---

- **`<a>` nested inside `<a>` (PENDING)**: The overlay link pattern is the correct semantic solution. The outer wrapper becomes a `<div>` with `role` implied by context, an absolutely positioned transparent `<Link>` covers the entire card at `z-0`, and the inner live/repo `<a>` tags remain as proper links sitting above at `z-10`.

- **Grocery Lens ordering (PENDING)**: `getAllProjects()` sorts by date descending. Grocery Lens date is "2025-07-01" but portfolio-website is "2026-04-01" so it sorts last. Fix: update grocery-lens.mdx date to today "2026-04-14" (semantically correct since it's ongoing).

```text
45	                      ? "text-[var(--accent)]"
46	                      : "text-[var(--muted)] hover:text-[var(--fg)]"
47	                  }`}
48	                >
49	                  {label}
50	                </Link>
51	              </li>
52	            );
53	          })}
54	        </ul>
55	
56	        {/* Right side: theme toggle + mobile hamburger */}
57	        <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
58	          <ThemeToggle />
59	          <button
60	            className="flex h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded-md sm:hidden hover:bg-[var(--surface-alt)] transition-colors"
61	            onClick={() => setMobileOpen((v) => !v)}
62	            aria-label={mobileOpen ? "Close menu" : "Open menu"}

66	              className="flex flex-col gap-1.5"

70	                  key={i}

72	                  variants={{

76	                      opacity: i === 1 ? 0 : 1,
77	                    },

93	            animate={{ height: "auto", opacity: 1 }}
94	            exit={{ height: 0, opacity: 0 }}

97	          >
```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 13:36:35Z)**_

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The **overlay link pattern** is the standard fix for nested `<a>` elements in card UIs. The key insight: z-index stacking lets you put a transparent full-card `<Link>` at `z-0`, then lift the content div to `z-10`. Clicks on inner `<a>` tags hit `z-10` first; clicks on empty card space fall through to the `z-0` overlay. No `stopPropagation` needed.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Starting all fixes in parallel:

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 13:36:44Z)**_

```tsx
import Link from "next/link";
import Image from "next/image";
import type { ProjectMeta } from "@/lib/mdx";

interface ProjectCardProps {
  project: ProjectMeta;
}

export default function ProjectCard({ project }: ProjectCardProps) {
  return (
    <div className="group relative flex flex-col rounded-[var(--radius-card)] border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] overflow-hidden transition-all hover:border-[var(--accent)] hover:shadow-[0_0_20px_oklch(74%_0.17_200_/_12%)]">
      {/* Invisible full-card link — sits behind all content at z-0 */}
      <Link
        href={`/projects/${project.slug}`}
        className="absolute inset-0 z-0"
        aria-label={`View ${project.title}`}
      />

      {/* All content lifted to z-10 so inner <a> tags work without conflict */}
      <div className="relative z-10 flex flex-col flex-1">
        {/* Thumbnail */}
        <div className="relative aspect-video bg-[var(--surface-alt)] overflow-hidden">
          {project.thumbnail ? (
            <Image
              src={project.thumbnail}
              alt={project.title}
              fill
              unoptimized
              className="object-cover transition-transform duration-500 group-hover:scale-105"
              sizes="(max-width: 640px) 100vw, (max-width: 1024px) 50vw, 33vw"
            />
          ) : (
            <div className="flex h-full items-center justify-center text-[var(--border)] text-xs font-mono">
              no preview
            </div>
          )}

          {/* Status badge */}
          {project.status !== "completed" && (
            <span className="absolute top-2 right-2 text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--surface)]/80 text-[var(--muted)] font-mono backdrop-blur-sm">
              {project.status}
            </span>
          )}
        </div>

        {/* Content */}
        <div className="flex flex-col flex-1 p-5">
          <h3 className="font-semibold text-[var(--fg)] mb-2 group-hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors line-clamp-2">
            {project.title}
          </h3>
          <p className="text-sm text-[var(--muted)] mb-4 flex-1 leading-relaxed line-clamp-3">
            {project.description}
          </p>

          {/* Tags */}
          <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-1.5 mb-4">
            {project.tags.map((tag) => (
              <span
                key={tag}
                className="text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--accent-dim)] text-[var(--accent)] font-mono"
              >
                {tag}
              </span>
            ))}
          </div>

          {/* Links row */}
          <div className="flex gap-3 text-xs font-mono text-[var(--muted)]">
            {project.liveUrl && (
              <a
                href={project.liveUrl}
                target="_blank"
                rel="noopener noreferrer"
                className="hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors"
              >
                live ↗
              </a>
            )}
            {project.repoUrl && (
              <a
                href={project.repoUrl}
                target="_blank"
                rel="noopener noreferrer"
                className="hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors"
              >
                repo ↗
              </a>
            )}
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

```

```mdx
---
title: "Company Hierarchy Tree"
slug: "dbt-hierarchy-tree"
description: "An interactive D3.js tree visualiser for navigating company ownership hierarchies, built for and deployed across the Department for Business & Trade."
date: "2023-06-01"
tags: ["D3.js", "JavaScript", "PostgreSQL", "SQL"]
liveUrl: "https://company-hierarchy-tree-chart.data.trade.gov.uk/"
featured: false
status: "completed"
---

## Overview

An interactive tree visualiser for exploring company ownership hierarchies, built during my time as Data Analyst at the Department for Business & Trade. The tool lets analysts navigate parent-subsidiary relationships across large company datasets — something flat tables can't represent intuitively.

Deployed department-wide as an internal analytical tool.

## What I Built

- **Recursive SQL:** CTEs to traverse parent-subsidiary hierarchies of arbitrary depth in PostgreSQL, producing the nested node structure D3 expects
- **D3.js tree layout:** collapsible, pannable, zoomable hierarchy tree — click a node to expand or collapse its subtree
- **Dun & Bradstreet integration:** matched against D&B company data; improving coverage from ~65% to above 80% meant more of the tree was populated and navigable

## Technical Choices

- **D3.js** — the `d3.hierarchy` and `d3.tree` layout APIs handle the node positioning; SVG rendering keeps it sharp at any zoom level
- **PostgreSQL recursive CTEs** — `WITH RECURSIVE` to walk the ownership graph without application-level looping
- **Vanilla JS frontend** — no framework overhead; the visualisation itself is the whole product

```

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Making `thumbnail` optional rather than adding an empty string is the right call here — the Zod schema is the single source of truth for frontmatter shape. The `ProjectCard` already has a graceful `"no preview"` fallback branch, so the optional field flows correctly all the way from schema → type → component with no extra code.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 13:48:35Z)**_

You're right that the nav fix was broken — the z-0/z-10 sibling pattern doesn't work because clicks on the content div don't bubble to a sibling link. The correct fix is the **`::after` stretch** technique: put the `<Link>` on the title element and use a `::after` pseudo-element to expand it to cover the whole card. Inner links then sit above it at `z-10`.

```tsx
import Link from "next/link";
import Image from "next/image";
import type { ProjectMeta } from "@/lib/mdx";

interface ProjectCardProps {
  project: ProjectMeta;
}

export default function ProjectCard({ project }: ProjectCardProps) {
  return (
    <article className="group relative flex flex-col rounded-[var(--radius-card)] border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] overflow-hidden transition-all hover:border-[var(--accent)] hover:shadow-[0_0_20px_oklch(74%_0.17_200_/_12%)]">
      {/* Thumbnail */}
      <div className="relative aspect-video bg-[var(--surface-alt)] overflow-hidden">
        {project.thumbnail ? (
          <Image
            src={project.thumbnail}
            alt={project.title}
            fill
            unoptimized
            className="object-cover transition-transform duration-500 group-hover:scale-105"
            sizes="(max-width: 640px) 100vw, (max-width: 1024px) 50vw, 33vw"
          />
        ) : (
          <div className="flex h-full items-center justify-center text-[var(--border)] text-xs font-mono">
            no preview
          </div>
        )}

        {/* Status badge */}
        {project.status !== "completed" && (
          <span className="absolute top-2 right-2 text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--surface)]/80 text-[var(--muted)] font-mono backdrop-blur-sm">
            {project.status}
          </span>
        )}
      </div>

      {/* Content */}
      <div className="flex flex-col flex-1 p-5">
        {/*
          Title link with ::after stretched to cover the whole card.
          The card has position:relative so ::after (absolute + inset-0) fills it.
          This avoids nested <a> while keeping the whole card clickable.
        */}
        <h3 className="font-semibold text-[var(--fg)] mb-2 group-hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors line-clamp-2">
          <Link
            href={`/projects/${project.slug}`}
            className="after:absolute after:inset-0"
          >
            {project.title}
          </Link>
        </h3>
        <p className="text-sm text-[var(--muted)] mb-4 flex-1 leading-relaxed line-clamp-3">
          {project.description}
        </p>

        {/* Tags */}
        <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-1.5 mb-4">
          {project.tags.map((tag) => (
            <span
              key={tag}
              className="text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--accent-dim)] text-[var(--accent)] font-mono"
            >
              {tag}
            </span>
          ))}
        </div>

        {/* Links row — relative + z-10 lifts these above the ::after overlay */}
        <div className="relative z-10 flex gap-3 text-xs font-mono text-[var(--muted)]">
          {project.liveUrl && (
            <a
              href={project.liveUrl}
              target="_blank"
              rel="noopener noreferrer"
              className="hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors"
            >
              live ↗
            </a>
          )}
          {project.repoUrl && (
            <a
              href={project.repoUrl}
              target="_blank"
              rel="noopener noreferrer"
              className="hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors"
            >
              repo ↗
            </a>
          )}
        </div>
      </div>
    </article>
  );
}

```

```mdx
---
title: "Grocery Lens — Dietary Tree Visualiser"
slug: "grocery-lens-diet-tree"
description: "An interactive D3.js tree visualiser for navigating ingredient taxonomy and dietary filter hierarchies within Grocery Lens."
date: "2025-11-01"
tags: ["D3.js", "JavaScript", "Django", "PostgreSQL"]
thumbnail: "/media/projects/grocery-lens-diet-tree/thumbnail.png"
liveUrl: "https://grocerylens.co.uk/diet/4/low-fodmap-template/?view=tree"
featured: false
status: "completed"
---

## Overview

Part of [Grocery Lens](/projects/grocery-lens). The dietary filter system models ingredient taxonomy as a tree — a diet like Low-FODMAP is a root node, its rules are branches, and individual ingredients are leaves. This visualiser makes that structure navigable in the browser.

## What I Built

- **D3.js tree layout** — collapsible, pannable, zoomable hierarchy using `d3.hierarchy` and `d3.tree`; nodes expand and collapse on click
- **Django backend** — the taxonomy lives in PostgreSQL; a single API endpoint serialises the full subtree for a given diet template as nested JSON
- **SVG rendering** — sharp at any zoom level; colour-coded nodes distinguish allow/restrict/conditional ingredient statuses

## Technical Choices

- **D3.js** — `d3.hierarchy` handles the nested data structure; `d3.tree` computes node positions; SVG `<path>` elements draw the curved links
- **Django REST endpoint** — recursive serialisation of the diet node tree via a single query with `select_related` and prefetch; avoids N+1
- **Tailwind + vanilla JS** — no React; the page is a Django template with a single `<script>` block initialising D3 on the serialised data embedded in the page

```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 14:06:10Z)**_

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The `::after` approach works because CSS pseudo-elements are positioned relative to their **generating element's nearest positioned ancestor** — which is the card `<div>` with `position: relative`. So `after:absolute after:inset-0` on the title `<Link>` literally stretches an invisible overlay to fill the entire card. The inner "live ↗" / "repo ↗" links then need `position: relative` + `z-index: 10` to sit above that overlay in the stacking order.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

---

**What changed:**

- **ProjectCard navigation** — fixed with `::after` stretch on the title `<Link>`. The whole card is now clickable without nested `<a>` elements. Live/repo links sit at `z-10` above the overlay.
- **D3 project** — removed the wrong DBT one, created `grocery-lens-diet-tree.mdx` pointing to the correct Grocery Lens URL with the screenshot you provided
- **Portfolio thumbnail** — your homepage screenshot copied in and wired up
- **Grocery Lens diet tree thumbnail** — wired up

**One thing to note on the diet tree MDX** — I wrote the technical description based on the Grocery Lens project context (Django template, recursive serialisation, etc.). Have a read and correct any details that don't match how you actually built it.

_**User (2026-04-14 14:16:46Z)**_

Grocery Lens feature set needs to be expanded:

It allows for forum-like upvoting/downvoting of publicly posted diets, it has e-commerce grocery aspects of adding products to basket, favouriting system, price history, compare page. background caching of compatible recipes and products after a diet has been created etc.

for the d3 vis here is a good summar yfor you to adapt:

Your diet tree visualization stack is:

Django template: detail.html renders the Tree View tab, fullscreen button, and tree container.
Backend API: Django view serves hierarchy data from /api/diet/<diet_id>/ingredient-hierarchy/.
Frontend visualization: diet_tree_visualization.js builds the interactive tree.
D3.js: used for the actual tree layout and SVG rendering, specifically d3.hierarchy, d3.tree, d3.zoom, SVG groups, links, nodes, and transitions.
Bootstrap 5: used for tabs, buttons, alerts, layout, and theme variables.
Feather icons: used for the tab icon, fullscreen icon, reset icon, etc.
Playwright + Django TestCase: used for end-to-end testing of tab switching, rendering, fullscreen, lazy loading, and API behavior.
The tree itself is an SVG-based D3 collapsible tree with D3 zoom/pan, animated node/link transitions, lazy loading when the Tree View tab opens, and browser Fullscreen API support.

also the contrast in light mode is very bad for a lot of elements on the portfolio. please can you do a deep dive into contrast issues like this.

```text
1	---
2	title: "Grocery Lens"
3	slug: "grocery-lens"
4	description: "A Django/PostgreSQL data product with custom dietary filters, recipe matching, basket synchronisation and NLP-assisted ingredient processing — built and operated from scratch."
5	date: "2026-04-14"
6	tags: ["Python", "Django", "PostgreSQL", "Docker", "Playwright", "spaCy", "Grafana"]
7	thumbnail: "/media/projects/grocery-lens/thumbnail.svg"
8	liveUrl: "https://grocerylens.co.uk"
9	featured: true
10	status: "in-progress"
11	---
12	
13	## Overview
14	
15	Grocery Lens is a Django/PostgreSQL data product I've been building from scratch since July 2025. It combines custom dietary filters, recipe matching, basket synchronisation and NLP-assisted ingredient processing to help users navigate grocery data in ways retailer apps don't support.
16	
17	The backend is the core challenge: designing a platform that handles scraping, ingestion, data quality repair, search and analytics from the same PostgreSQL schema — without losing data integrity as the product grows.
18	
19	## What I Built
20	
21	- **Production data platform:** PostgreSQL schemas for semi-structured product and analytics workloads using `JSONField`, `ArrayField`, GIN indexes and integrity constraints
22	- **Replayable ingestion workflows:** loading retailer scrapes, public datasets and CSV logs with upserts, transactional writes and structured repair tables for relinking, backfills and taxonomy improvement
23	- **NLP ingredient processing:** canonicalisation and fuzzy matching with spaCy; fixed a scraper memory leak that reached 5.9 GB over 5,938 products
24	- **Concurrency-aware processing:** bounded concurrency, retry-safe queues using `select_for_update(skip_locked)` to balance throughput and reliability
25	- **D3.js dietary tree visualiser:** an interactive ingredient hierarchy view for dietary filters — [see it live](https://grocerylens.co.uk/diet/4/low-fodmap-template?view=tree)
26	- **Privacy-aware analytics:** retention controls, aggregation layers and reporting controls built for a live service
27	- **Observability:** Grafana/Loki log aggregation, health checks, blue/green deployments and rollback-safe traffic switching
28	
29	## Technical Choices
30	
31	- **Django ORM + raw SQL** — Django models for application logic; raw SQL for complex ingestion queries, upserts and repair workflows where the ORM adds unnecessary overhead
32	- **PostgreSQL** — `JSONField` and `ArrayField` for semi-structured product data; GIN indexes for fast search; queue-backed processing using `select_for_update(skip_locked)` for background work
33	- **Playwright + BeautifulSoup** — Playwright for JS-rendered retailer pages; BeautifulSoup for lighter scraping tasks
34	- **Docker + Caddy** — containerised service behind Caddy, deployed blue/green with a health-check-gated swap script
35	- **Grafana + Loki** — structured log aggregation for pipeline health and data quality trend monitoring
36
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	---
2	title: "Grocery Lens — Dietary Tree Visualiser"
3	slug: "grocery-lens-diet-tree"
4	description: "An interactive D3.js tree visualiser for navigating ingredient taxonomy and dietary filter hierarchies within Grocery Lens."
5	date: "2025-11-01"
6	tags: ["D3.js", "JavaScript", "Django", "PostgreSQL"]
7	thumbnail: "/media/projects/grocery-lens-diet-tree/thumbnail.png"
8	liveUrl: "https://grocerylens.co.uk/diet/4/low-fodmap-template/?view=tree"
9	featured: false
10	status: "completed"
11	---
12	
13	## Overview
14	
15	Part of [Grocery Lens](/projects/grocery-lens). The dietary filter system models ingredient taxonomy as a tree — a diet like Low-FODMAP is a root node, its rules are branches, and individual ingredients are leaves. This visualiser makes that structure navigable in the browser.
16	
17	## What I Built
18	
19	- **D3.js tree layout** — collapsible, pannable, zoomable hierarchy using `d3.hierarchy` and `d3.tree`; nodes expand and collapse on click
20	- **Django backend** — the taxonomy lives in PostgreSQL; a single API endpoint serialises the full subtree for a given diet template as nested JSON
21	- **SVG rendering** — sharp at any zoom level; colour-coded nodes distinguish allow/restrict/conditional ingredient statuses
22	
23	## Technical Choices
24	
25	- **D3.js** — `d3.hierarchy` handles the nested data structure; `d3.tree` computes node positions; SVG `<path>` elements draw the curved links
26	- **Django REST endpoint** — recursive serialisation of the diet node tree via a single query with `select_related` and prefetch; avoids N+1
27	- **Tailwind + vanilla JS** — no React; the page is a Django template with a single `<script>` block initialising D3 on the serialised data embedded in the page
28
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 14:18:51Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 14:18:54Z)**_

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The core light mode issue is that `--accent` was never overridden — so the dark mode's `oklch(74% 0.17 200)` cyan runs against near-white backgrounds (~98% L). That's roughly a **1.9:1 contrast ratio** against the body background, where WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text. `--muted` at oklch(48%) also fails narrowly (~4.0:1). The fix is to darken both in the `.light` block, along with tightening `--surface-alt` and `--border` for better card/chip definition.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

```text
1	@import "tailwindcss";
2	@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography";
3	
4	/* Dark mode: next-themes adds the 'dark' class to <html> */
5	@custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *));
6	
7	/* Design tokens */
8	@theme {
9	  /* Zinc-based dark-techy palette */
10	  --color-bg: oklch(10% 0.005 264);
11	  --color-surface: oklch(14% 0.006 264);
12	  --color-surface-alt: oklch(18% 0.007 264);
13	  --color-border: oklch(25% 0.008 264);
14	  --color-fg: oklch(96% 0.003 264);
15	  --color-muted: oklch(60% 0.01 264);
16	  --color-accent: oklch(74% 0.17 200);      /* cyan-400 equivalent */
17	  --color-accent-dim: oklch(74% 0.17 200 / 12%);
18	
19	  --font-sans: var(--font-geist-sans);
20	  --font-mono: var(--font-geist-mono);
21	
22	  --radius-card: 0.75rem;
23	}
24	
25	/* Base variables (dark by default — system default, overridden by .light) */
26	:root {
27	  --bg: var(--color-bg);
28	  --surface: var(--color-surface);
29	  --surface-alt: var(--color-surface-alt);
30	  --border: var(--color-border);
31	  --fg: var(--color-fg);
32	  --muted: var(--color-muted);
33	  --accent: var(--color-accent);
34	  --accent-dim: var(--color-accent-dim);
35	  --glow: 0 0 20px oklch(74% 0.17 200 / 15%);
36	}
37	
38	/* Light mode overrides — next-themes adds .light to <html> */
39	.light {
40	  --bg: oklch(98% 0.002 264);
41	  --surface: oklch(94% 0.004 264);
42	  --surface-alt: oklch(89% 0.006 264);
43	  --border: oklch(80% 0.008 264);
44	  --fg: oklch(12% 0.005 264);
45	  --muted: oklch(48% 0.01 264);
46	  --glow: none;
47	}
48	
49	html {
50	  scroll-behavior: smooth;
51	  background-color: var(--bg);
52	}
53	
54	body {
55	  background-color: var(--bg);
56	  color: var(--fg);
57	  font-family: var(--font-sans), system-ui, sans-serif;
58	  min-height: 100vh;
59	  transition: background-color 0.2s ease, color 0.2s ease;
60	}
61	
62	/* Subtle custom scrollbar */
63	::-webkit-scrollbar { width: 6px; }
64	::-webkit-scrollbar-track { background: var(--surface); }
65	::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { background: var(--border); border-radius: 3px; }
66	::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover { background: var(--muted); }
67	
68	/* Focus ring */
69	:focus-visible {
70	  outline: 2px solid var(--accent);
71	  outline-offset: 2px;
72	}
73	
74	/* Prose MDX overrides */
75	.prose {
76	  --tw-prose-body: var(--fg);
77	  --tw-prose-headings: var(--fg);
78	  --tw-prose-code: var(--accent);
79	  --tw-prose-links: var(--accent);
80	  --tw-prose-bold: var(--fg);
81	  --tw-prose-quotes: var(--muted);
82	  --tw-prose-hr: var(--border);
83	  --tw-prose-pre-bg: var(--surface);
84	}
85
```

```text
11	      delayChildren: 0.1,

21	export default function Hero() {

47	        {/* Bio */}
48	        <motion.p
49	          variants={item}

51	        >
52	          I build data ingestion pipelines, Django/PostgreSQL platforms, and
53	          scraping workflows — turning messy, unreliable data into
54	          production-ready systems. Currently founding Grocery Lens and
55	          available for contract work.
56	        </motion.p>
57	
58	        {/* CTAs */}
59	        <motion.div variants={item} className="flex gap-4 flex-wrap justify-center mt-2">
60	          <Link
61	            href="/projects"
62	            className="rounded-md border border-[var(--accent)] px-5 py-2.5 text-sm font-medium text-[var(--accent)] transition-all hover:bg-[var(--accent-dim)] hover:shadow-[0_0_20px_oklch(74%_0.17_200_/_15%)]"

71	          </Link>

74	        {/* Scroll indicator */}
```

```text
1	import AnimatedSection from "@/components/shared/AnimatedSection";
2	import Link from "next/link";
3	import { getAllProjects } from "@/lib/mdx";
4	
5	export default function FeaturedProjects() {
6	  const featured = getAllProjects().filter((p) => p.featured).slice(0, 4);
7	
8	  if (featured.length === 0) return null;
9	
10	  return (
11	    <section className="mx-auto max-w-5xl px-6 py-20">
12	      <AnimatedSection>
13	        <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-10">
14	          <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-[var(--fg)]">
15	            Featured Projects
16	          </h2>
17	          <Link
18	            href="/projects"
19	            className="text-sm text-[var(--accent)] hover:underline font-mono"
20	          >
21	            View all →
22	          </Link>
23	        </div>
24	      </AnimatedSection>
25	
26	      <div className="grid gap-4 sm:grid-cols-2">
27	        {featured.map((project, i) => (
28	          <AnimatedSection key={project.slug} delay={i * 0.1}>
29	            <Link
30	              href={`/projects/${project.slug}`}
31	              className="block rounded-[var(--radius-card)] border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] p-6 transition-all hover:border-[var(--accent)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-alt)] group"
32	            >
33	              <h3 className="font-semibold text-[var(--fg)] mb-2 group-hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors">
34	                {project.title}
35	              </h3>
36	              <p className="text-sm text-[var(--muted)] mb-4 leading-relaxed">
37	                {project.description}
38	              </p>
39	              <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
40	                {project.tags.map((tag) => (
41	                  <span
42	                    key={tag}
43	                    className="text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--accent-dim)] text-[var(--accent)] font-mono"
44	                  >
45	                    {tag}
46	                  </span>
47	                ))}
48	              </div>
49	            </Link>
50	          </AnimatedSection>
51	        ))}
52	      </div>
53	    </section>
54	  );
55	}
```

```text
1	import Link from "next/link";
2	import Image from "next/image";
3	import type { ProjectMeta } from "@/lib/mdx";
4	
5	interface ProjectCardProps {
6	  project: ProjectMeta;
7	}
8	
9	export default function ProjectCard({ project }: ProjectCardProps) {
10	  return (
11	    <article className="group relative flex flex-col rounded-[var(--radius-card)] border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] overflow-hidden transition-all hover:border-[var(--accent)] hover:shadow-[0_0_20px_oklch(74%_0.17_200_/_12%)]">
12	      {/* Thumbnail */}
13	      <div className="relative aspect-video bg-[var(--surface-alt)] overflow-hidden">
14	        {project.thumbnail ? (
15	          <Image
16	            src={project.thumbnail}
17	            alt={project.title}
18	            fill
19	            unoptimized
20	            className="object-cover transition-transform duration-500 group-hover:scale-105"
21	            sizes="(max-width: 640px) 100vw, (max-width: 1024px) 50vw, 33vw"
22	          />
23	        ) : (
24	          <div className="flex h-full items-center justify-center text-[var(--border)] text-xs font-mono">
25	            no preview
26	          </div>
27	        )}
28	
29	        {/* Status badge */}
30	        {project.status !== "completed" && (
31	          <span className="absolute top-2 right-2 text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--surface)]/80 text-[var(--muted)] font-mono backdrop-blur-sm">
32	            {project.status}
33	          </span>
34	        )}
35	      </div>
36	
37	      {/* Content */}
38	      <div className="flex flex-col flex-1 p-5">
39	        {/*
40	          Title link with ::after stretched to cover the whole card.
41	          The card has position:relative so ::after (absolute + inset-0) fills it.
42	          This avoids nested <a> while keeping the whole card clickable.
43	        */}
44	        <h3 className="font-semibold text-[var(--fg)] mb-2 group-hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors line-clamp-2">
45	          <Link
46	            href={`/projects/${project.slug}`}
47	            className="after:absolute after:inset-0"
48	          >
49	            {project.title}
50	          </Link>
51	        </h3>
52	        <p className="text-sm text-[var(--muted)] mb-4 flex-1 leading-relaxed line-clamp-3">
53	          {project.description}
54	        </p>
55	
56	        {/* Tags */}
57	        <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-1.5 mb-4">
58	          {project.tags.map((tag) => (
59	            <span
60	              key={tag}
61	              className="text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--accent-dim)] text-[var(--accent)] font-mono"
62	            >
63	              {tag}
64	            </span>
65	          ))}
66	        </div>
67	
68	        {/* Links row — relative + z-10 lifts these above the ::after overlay */}
69	        <div className="relative z-10 flex gap-3 text-xs font-mono text-[var(--muted)]">
70	          {project.liveUrl && (
71	            <a
72	              href={project.liveUrl}
73	              target="_blank"
74	              rel="noopener noreferrer"
75	              className="hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors"
76	            >
77	              live ↗
78	            </a>
79	          )}
80	          {project.repoUrl && (
81	            <a
82	              href={project.repoUrl}
83	              target="_blank"
84	              rel="noopener noreferrer"
85	              className="hover:text-[var(--accent)] transition-colors"
86	            >
87	              repo ↗
88	            </a>
89	          )}
90	        </div>
91	      </div>
92	    </article>
93	  );
94	}
```

```text
5	  activeTag: string | null;
6	  onSelect: (tag: string | null) => void;
7	}
8	
9	export default function TagFilter({ tags, activeTag, onSelect }: TagFilterProps) {
10	  return (
11	    <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2 mb-8">
12	      <button
13	        onClick={() => onSelect(null)}
14	        className={`text-xs px-3 py-1.5 rounded-full font-mono transition-colors ${
15	          activeTag === null
16	            ? "bg-[var(--accent)] text-[var(--bg)]"
17	            : "bg-[var(--surface)] text-[var(--muted)] border border-[var(--border)] hover:border-[var(--accent)] hover:text-[var(--accent)]"
18	        }`}
19	      >
20	        All
21	      </button>
22	      {tags.map((tag) => (
23	        <button
24	          key={tag}
25	          onClick={() => onSelect(tag === activeTag ? null : tag)}
26	          className={`text-xs px-3 py-1.5 rounded-full font-mono transition-colors ${
27	            activeTag === tag
28	              ? "bg-[var(--accent)] text-[var(--bg)]"
29	              : "bg-[var(--surface)] text-[var(--muted)] border border-[var(--border)] hover:border-[var(--accent)] hover:text-[var(--accent)]"
30	          }`}
31	        >
32	          {tag}
33	        </button>
34	      ))}
35	    </div>
36	  );
37	}
```

 `./src/components/contact/ContactForm.tsx`

```text
1	"use client";
2	
3	import { useState } from "react";
4	import { useForm } from "react-hook-form";
5	import { zodResolver } from "@hookform/resolvers/zod";
6	import { motion, AnimatePresence } from "motion/react";
7	import { contactSchema, type ContactFormData } from "@/lib/schema";
8	
9	type SubmitState = "idle" | "submitting" | "success" | "error" | "rate-limited";
10	
11	export default function ContactForm() {
12	  const [submitState, setSubmitState] = useState<SubmitState>("idle");
13	  const [errorMsg, setErrorMsg] = useState("");
14	
15	  const {
16	    register,
17	    handleSubmit,
18	    formState: { errors, isSubmitting },
19	    reset,
20	  } = useForm<ContactFormData>({
21	    resolver: zodResolver(contactSchema),
22	  });
23	
24	  async function onSubmit(data: ContactFormData) {
25	    setSubmitState("submitting");
26	
27	    try {
28	      const res = await fetch("/api/contact", {
29	        method: "POST",
30	        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
31	        body: JSON.stringify(data),
32	      });
33	
34	      if (res.status === 429) {
35	        const json = await res.json();
36	        setSubmitState("rate-limited");
37	        setErrorMsg(json.error ?? "Too many requests. Please wait.");
38	        return;
39	      }
40	
41	      if (!res.ok) {
42	        const json = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));
43	        setSubmitState("error");
44	        setErrorMsg(json.error ?? "Something went wrong. Please try again.");
45	        return;
46	      }
47	
48	      setSubmitState("success");
49	      reset();
50	    } catch {
51	      setSubmitState("error");
52	      setErrorMsg("Network error. Please check your connection.");
53	    }
54	  }
55	
56	  const inputClass =
57	    "w-full rounded-md border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] px-4 py-2.5 text-sm text-[var(--fg)] placeholder:text-[var(--muted)] transition-colors focus:border-[var(--accent)] focus:outline-none";
58	
59	  const labelClass = "block text-xs font-mono text-[var(--muted)] mb-1.5";
60	
61	  return (
62	    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit(onSubmit)} noValidate className="space-y-5">
63	      {/* Name */}
64	      <div>
65	        <label htmlFor="name" className={labelClass}>
66	          name *
67	        </label>
68	        <input
69	          id="name"
70	          type="text"
71	          autoComplete="name"
72	          placeholder="Tom Swart"
73	          {...register("name")}
74	          className={inputClass}
75	          aria-invalid={!!errors.name}
76	        />
77	        {errors.name && (
78	          <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-red-400 font-mono">
79	            {errors.name.message}
80	          </p>
81	        )}
82	      </div>
83	
84	      {/* Email */}
85	      <div>
86	        <label htmlFor="email" className={labelClass}>
87	          email *
88	        </label>
89	        <input
90	          id="email"
91	          type="email"
92	          autoComplete="email"
93	          placeholder="you@example.com"
94	          {...register("email")}
95	          className={inputClass}
96	          aria-invalid={!!errors.email}
97	        />
98	        {errors.email && (
99	          <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-red-400 font-mono">
100	            {errors.email.message}
101	          </p>
102	        )}
103	      </div>
104	
105	      {/* Message */}
106	      <div>
107	        <label htmlFor="message" className={labelClass}>
108	          message *
109	        </label>
110	        <textarea
111	          id="message"
112	          rows={5}
113	          placeholder="What's on your mind?"
114	          {...register("message")}
115	          className={`${inputClass} resize-none`}
116	          aria-invalid={!!errors.message}
117	        />
118	        {errors.message && (
119	          <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-red-400 font-mono">
120	            {errors.message.message}
121	          </p>
122	        )}
123	      </div>
124	
125	      {/* Submit */}
126	      <button
127	        type="submit"
128	        disabled={isSubmitting}
129	        className="w-full rounded-md border border-[var(--accent)] px-5 py-2.5 text-sm font-medium text-[var(--accent)] transition-all hover:bg-[var(--accent-dim)] disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed"
130	      >
131	        {isSubmitting ? "Sending..." : "Send Message"}
132	      </button>
133	
134	      {/* Feedback messages */}
135	      <AnimatePresence mode="wait">
136	        {submitState === "success" && (
137	          <motion.p
138	            key="success"
139	            initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 4 }}
140	            animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
141	            exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
142	            className="text-sm text-green-400 font-mono text-center"
143	          >
144	            ✓ Message sent. I&apos;ll get back to you soon.
145	          </motion.p>
146	        )}
147	        {(submitState === "error" || submitState === "rate-limited") && (
148	          <motion.p
149	            key="error"
150	            initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 4 }}
151	            animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
152	            exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
153	            className="text-sm text-red-400 font-mono text-center"
154	          >
155	            {errorMsg}
156	          </motion.p>
157	        )}
158	      </AnimatePresence>
```

```text
15	export default function Navbar() {
16	  const pathname = usePathname();
17	  const [mobileOpen, setMobileOpen] = useState(false);
18	
19	  return (
20	    <motion.header
21	      initial={{ y: -20, opacity: 0 }}
22	      animate={{ y: 0, opacity: 1 }}
23	      transition={{ duration: 0.4, ease: "easeOut" }}
24	      className="sticky top-0 z-50 border-b border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg)]/80 backdrop-blur-md"
25	    >
26	      <nav className="mx-auto flex max-w-5xl items-center justify-between px-6 py-4">
27	        {/* Logo */}
28	        <Link
29	          href="/"
30	          className="font-mono text-sm font-semibold tracking-tight text-[var(--accent)] transition-opacity hover:opacity-80"
31	        >
32	          &gt; tswart<span className="animate-pulse">_</span>
33	        </Link>
34	
35	        {/* Desktop nav links */}
36	        <ul className="hidden items-center gap-6 sm:flex">
37	          {navLinks.map(({ href, label }) => {
38	            const active = pathname === href || pathname.startsWith(href + "/");
39	            return (
40	              <li key={href}>
41	                <Link
42	                  href={href}
43	                  className={`text-sm transition-colors ${
44	                    active
45	                      ? "text-[var(--accent)]"
46	                      : "text-[var(--muted)] hover:text-[var(--fg)]"
47	                  }`}
48	                >
49	                  {label}
50	                </Link>
51	              </li>
52	            );
53	          })}
54	        </ul>
55	
56	        {/* Right side: theme toggle + mobile hamburger */}
57	        <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
58	          <ThemeToggle />
59	          <button
60	            className="flex h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded-md sm:hidden hover:bg-[var(--surface-alt)] transition-colors"
61	            onClick={() => setMobileOpen((v) => !v)}
62	            aria-label={mobileOpen ? "Close menu" : "Open menu"}
63	          >
64	            <motion.div
65	              animate={mobileOpen ? "open" : "closed"}
66	              className="flex flex-col gap-1.5"
67	            >
68	              {[0, 1, 2].map((i) => (
69	                <motion.span
70	                  key={i}
71	                  className="block h-px w-5 bg-[var(--fg)] origin-center"
72	                  variants={{
73	                    open: {
74	                      rotate: i === 0 ? 45 : i === 2 ? -45 : 0,
75	                      y: i === 0 ? 6 : i === 2 ? -6 : 0,
76	                      opacity: i === 1 ? 0 : 1,
77	                    },
78	                    closed: { rotate: 0, y: 0, opacity: 1 },
79	                  }}
80	                  transition={{ duration: 0.2 }}
81	                />
82	              ))}
83	            </motion.div>
84	          </button>
85	        </div>
86	      </nav>
87	
88	      {/* Mobile dropdown */}
89	      <AnimatePresence>
90	        {mobileOpen && (
91	          <motion.ul
92	            initial={{ height: 0, opacity: 0 }}
93	            animate={{ height: "auto", opacity: 1 }}
94	            exit={{ height: 0, opacity: 0 }}
95	            transition={{ duration: 0.25, ease: "easeInOut" }}
96	            className="overflow-hidden border-t border-[var(--border)] sm:hidden"
97	          >
98	            {navLinks.map(({ href, label }) => {
99	              const active =
100	                pathname === href || pathname.startsWith(href + "/");
101	              return (
102	                <li key={href}>
103	                  <Link
104	                    href={href}
105	                    onClick={() => setMobileOpen(false)}
106	                    className={`block px-6 py-3 text-sm transition-colors ${
107	                      active
108	                        ? "text-[var(--accent)]"
109	                        : "text-[var(--muted)] hover:text-[var(--fg)]"
110	                    }`}
111	                  >
112	                    {label}
113	                  </Link>
114	                </li>
115	              );
116	            })}
117	          </motion.ul>
118	        )}
119	      </AnimatePresence>
120	    </motion.header>
121	  );
122	}
```

```text
1	"use client";
2	
3	import { useEffect, useState } from "react";
4	import { useTheme } from "next-themes";
5	import { motion, AnimatePresence } from "motion/react";
6	
7	export default function ThemeToggle() {
8	  const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false);
9	  const { resolvedTheme, setTheme } = useTheme();
10	
11	  // Must be mounted before rendering — useTheme() reads the DOM class which
12	  // doesn't exist during SSR, causing a hydration mismatch without this guard.
13	  useEffect(() => setMounted(true), []);
14	
15	  if (!mounted) {
16	    return <div className="h-8 w-8" aria-hidden />;
17	  }
18	
19	  const isDark = resolvedTheme === "dark";
20	
21	  return (
22	    <button
23	      onClick={() => setTheme(isDark ? "light" : "dark")}
24	      className="relative flex h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded-md transition-colors hover:bg-[var(--surface-alt)] focus-visible:outline-2"
25	      aria-label={isDark ? "Switch to light mode" : "Switch to dark mode"}
26	    >
27	      <AnimatePresence mode="wait" initial={false}>
28	        {isDark ? (
29	          <motion.svg
30	            key="moon"
31	            xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
32	            width="16"
33	            height="16"
34	            viewBox="0 0 24 24"
35	            fill="none"
36	            stroke="currentColor"
37	            strokeWidth="2"
38	            strokeLinecap="round"
39	            strokeLinejoin="round"
40	            initial={{ opacity: 0, rotate: -30, scale: 0.8 }}
41	            animate={{ opacity: 1, rotate: 0, scale: 1 }}
42	            exit={{ opacity: 0, rotate: 30, scale: 0.8 }}
43	            transition={{ duration: 0.2 }}
44	            aria-hidden
45	          >
46	            <path d="M21 12.79A9 9 0 1 1 11.21 3 7 7 0 0 0 21 12.79z" />
47	          </motion.svg>
48	        ) : (
49	          <motion.svg
50	            key="sun"
51	            xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
52	            width="16"
53	            height="16"
54	            viewBox="0 0 24 24"
55	            fill="none"
56	            stroke="currentColor"
57	            strokeWidth="2"
58	            strokeLinecap="round"
59	            strokeLinejoin="round"
60	            initial={{ opacity: 0, rotate: 30, scale: 0.8 }}
61	            animate={{ opacity: 1, rotate: 0, scale: 1 }}
62	            exit={{ opacity: 0, rotate: -30, scale: 0.8 }}
63	            transition={{ duration: 0.2 }}
64	            aria-hidden
65	          >
66	            <circle cx="12" cy="12" r="5" />
67	            <line x1="12" y1="1" x2="12" y2="3" />
68	            <line x1="12" y1="21" x2="12" y2="23" />
69	            <line x1="4.22" y1="4.22" x2="5.64" y2="5.64" />
70	            <line x1="18.36" y1="18.36" x2="19.78" y2="19.78" />
71	            <line x1="1" y1="12" x2="3" y2="12" />
72	            <line x1="21" y1="12" x2="23" y2="12" />
73	            <line x1="4.22" y1="19.78" x2="5.64" y2="18.36" />
74	            <line x1="18.36" y1="5.64" x2="19.78" y2="4.22" />
75	          </motion.svg>
76	        )}
77	      </AnimatePresence>
78	    </button>
79	  );
80	}
```

```text
56	        </div>
57
```

```text
1	"use client";
2	
3	import { useState } from "react";
4	import type { ProjectMeta } from "@/lib/mdx";
5	import ProjectCard from "./ProjectCard";
6	import TagFilter from "./TagFilter";
7	import AnimatedSection from "@/components/shared/AnimatedSection";
8	
9	interface ProjectGridProps {
10	  projects: ProjectMeta[];
11	}
12	
13	export default function ProjectGrid({ projects }: ProjectGridProps) {
14	  const [activeTag, setActiveTag] = useState<string | null>(null);
15	
16	  // Collect all unique tags from all projects
17	  const allTags = Array.from(
18	    new Set(projects.flatMap((p) => p.tags))
19	  ).sort();
20	
21	  const filtered = activeTag
22	    ? projects.filter((p) => p.tags.includes(activeTag))
23	    : projects;
24	
25	  return (
26	    <div>
27	      {allTags.length > 0 && (
28	        <TagFilter tags={allTags} activeTag={activeTag} onSelect={setActiveTag} />
29	      )}
30	
31	      {filtered.length === 0 ? (
32	        <p className="text-[var(--muted)] text-sm font-mono">
33	          No projects match the selected filter.
34	        </p>
35	      ) : (
36	        <div className="grid gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3">
37	          {filtered.map((project, i) => (
38	            <AnimatedSection key={project.slug} delay={i * 0.05}>
39	              <ProjectCard project={project} />
40	            </AnimatedSection>
41	          ))}
42	        </div>
43	      )}
44	    </div>
45	  );
46	}
47
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
24	    />
```

Excellent! Now I have a complete picture. Let me compile the comprehensive report.

## CSS Variable Usage Report: Portfolio Codebase

### Summary
- **Total CSS variables used**: 6 core tokens (--accent, --muted, --border, --surface, --surface-alt, --fg)
- **Hardcoded colors found**: 2 (red-400 and green-400 for form feedback messages)
- **Components reviewed**: 17 TSX files in src/components and src/app
- **Status**: Well-organized design system with minimal color hardcoding

---

## Detailed Component Breakdown

### 1. **Layout Components**

#### Navbar (/src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx)
- **--accent**: Logo text color (primary branding)
- **--border**: Top border
- **--muted**: Non-active nav links
- **--fg**: Active nav links, hamburger menu bars
- **--surface-alt**: Hover background (mobile menu button)

#### Footer (/src/components/layout/Footer.tsx)
- **--border**: Top border (footer separator)
- **--muted**: Copyright and link text
- **--accent**: Link hover states

#### ThemeToggle (/src/components/layout/ThemeToggle.tsx)
- **--surface-alt**: Hover background

---

### 2. **Home Page Components**

#### Hero (/src/components/home/Hero.tsx)
- **--fg**: "Tom " text (primary name)
- **--accent**: "Swart" text (accented name), button border, button text
- **--muted**: Role subtitle, bio text, "scroll" indicator text
- **--border**: Secondary button border, scroll indicator color

#### FeaturedProjects (/src/components/home/FeaturedProjects.tsx)
- **--fg**: Section heading, card titles
- **--accent**: "View all" link, tag background (accent-dim variant), tag text
- **--border**: Card border
- **--surface**: Card background
- **--surface-alt**: Card hover background
- **--muted**: Card description text

---

### 3. **Projects Components**

#### ProjectCard (/src/components/projects/ProjectCard.tsx)
- **--radius-card**: Border radius (layout token)
- **--border**: Card border
- **--surface**: Card background, status badge background
- **--surface-alt**: Thumbnail placeholder background
- **--accent**: Card border on hover, tag text, link hover
- **--fg**: Card title
- **--muted**: Card description, status badge text, footer link text

#### ProjectGrid (/src/components/projects/ProjectGrid.tsx)
- **--muted**: Empty state message, filter placeholder text

#### TagFilter (/src/components/projects/TagFilter.tsx)
- **--accent**: Active button background
- **--bg**: Active button text
- **--surface**: Inactive button background
- **--muted**: Inactive button text
- **--border**: Inactive button border
- **--accent**: Hover border and text

#### ProjectVideo (/src/components/projects/ProjectVideo.tsx)
- **--radius-card**: Video border radius
- **--surface**: Video background

---

### 4. **About Page Components**

#### Bio (/src/components/about/Bio.tsx)
- **--accent**: Avatar border, role subtitle, link hover
- **--fg**: Name heading, brand highlight ("Grocery Lens")
- **--muted**: Bio body text, link base color

#### SkillsGrid (/src/components/about/SkillsGrid.tsx)
- **--fg**: Section heading
- **--border**: Skill card border
- **--surface**: Skill card background
- **--accent**: Category label text
- **--surface-alt**: Individual skill tag background
- **--muted**: Individual skill tag text

#### Timeline (/src/components/about/Timeline.tsx)
- **--fg**: Event title and role
- **--border**: Timeline vertical line
- **--accent**: Timeline dot border, year and organization text
- **--bg**: Timeline dot fill
- **--muted**: Event description

---

### 5. **Contact Components**

#### ContactForm (/src/components/contact/ContactForm.tsx)
- **--border**: Input border, focus border transition
- **--surface**: Input background
- **--fg**: Input text
- **--muted**: Label text, input placeholder
- **--accent**: Focus border color, submit button border and text
- **--accent-dim**: Submit button hover background
- **red-400**: Error messages (hardcoded - NOT using CSS variables)
- **green-400**: Success message (hardcoded - NOT using CSS variables)

#### Contact Page (/src/app/contact/page.tsx)
- **--accent**: "get in touch" header text
- **--fg**: Page heading
- **--muted**: Description text

---

### 6. **MDX Layout Components**

#### MDXLayout (/src/components/mdx/MDXLayout.tsx)
- **--muted**: Back link base color, date text
- **--accent**: Back link hover, tag text, tag background (accent-dim variant), link colors
- **--border**: Status badge border
- **--surface**: Code block background, thumbnail background
- **--radius-card**: Image/video border radius
- **--fg**: Page title

---

### 7. **Global CSS Variables**

#### globals.css (/src/app/globals.css)
Global definitions and prose overrides:
- **--fg**: Body text color, prose headings and body
- **--surface**: Scrollbar track
- **--border**: Scrollbar thumb, prose horizontal rules
- **--muted**: Scrollbar thumb hover
- **--accent**: Focus ring outline, prose code and links
- **--accent-dim**: Implicit usage (defined as oklch variant)
- **--surface**: Prose pre backgrounds

**Dark Mode** (default):
```
--bg: oklch(10% 0.005 264)
--surface: oklch(14% 0.006 264)
--surface-alt: oklch(18% 0.007 264)
--border: oklch(25% 0.008 264)
--fg: oklch(96% 0.003 264)
--muted: oklch(60% 0.01 264)
--accent: oklch(74% 0.17 200) [cyan]
```

**Light Mode** (.light selector):
```
--bg: oklch(98% 0.002 264)
--surface: oklch(94% 0.004 264)
--surface-alt: oklch(89% 0.006 264)
--border: oklch(80% 0.008 264)
--fg: oklch(12% 0.005 264)
--muted: oklch(48% 0.01 264)
```

---

## Hardcoded Colors (Non-CSS Variables)

### Found in ContactForm:
| Location | Line(s) | Usage | Value | Context |
|----------|---------|-------|-------|---------|
| /src/components/contact/ContactForm.tsx | 78, 99, 119 | Error message text | `red-400` | Validation error messages for form fields |
| /src/components/contact/ContactForm.tsx | 142 | Success message text | `green-400` | Form submission success feedback |
| /src/components/contact/ContactForm.tsx | 153 | Error message text | `red-400` | Form submission error feedback |

**Recommendation**: Consider moving red-400 and green-400 to CSS variables (--color-error and --color-success) for consistency and theme coherence.

---

## CSS Context by Variable

### Text Color (color property)
- **--fg**: Hero name, headings, active nav, body text, card titles
- **--accent**: Logo, accented text, links, year/org in timeline
- **--muted**: Subtitles, descriptions, labels, secondary text

### Background Color (background-color property)
- **--surface**: Cards, inputs, code blocks
- **--surface-alt**: Card hovers, button hovers, skill tags
- **--accent-dim**: Tag backgrounds, button hover backgrounds
- **--bg**: Toggle button fill

### Border Color (border-color property)
- **--border**: Card borders, input borders, scrollbar, timeline line, status badges
- **--accent**: Interactive borders on hover, focus states

---

## Coverage Summary

**Tokens Used Across All Components:**
- **--fg**: 100% coverage (all text color needs)
- **--accent**: 100% coverage (primary interaction color)
- **--muted**: 100% coverage (secondary/disabled text)
- **--border**: 100% coverage (dividers and borders)
- **--surface**: 95% coverage (backgrounds)
- **--surface-alt**: 85% coverage (hover/secondary backgrounds)
- **--bg**: Used selectively (button fills, timeline dots)

**No hardcoded hex/rgb/oklch colors found in components/app except for accessibility-related Tailwind utility classes (red-400, green-400 in form feedback).**

```text
1	"use client";
2	
3	import { useState } from "react";
4	import { useForm } from "react-hook-form";
5	import { zodResolver } from "@hookform/resolvers/zod";
6	import { motion, AnimatePresence } from "motion/react";
7	import { contactSchema, type ContactFormData } from "@/lib/schema";
8	
9	type SubmitState = "idle" | "submitting" | "success" | "error" | "rate-limited";
10	
11	export default function ContactForm() {
12	  const [submitState, setSubmitState] = useState<SubmitState>("idle");
13	  const [errorMsg, setErrorMsg] = useState("");
14	
15	  const {
16	    register,
17	    handleSubmit,
18	    formState: { errors, isSubmitting },
19	    reset,
20	  } = useForm<ContactFormData>({
21	    resolver: zodResolver(contactSchema),
22	  });
23	
24	  async function onSubmit(data: ContactFormData) {
25	    setSubmitState("submitting");
26	
27	    try {
28	      const res = await fetch("/api/contact", {
29	        method: "POST",
30	        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
31	        body: JSON.stringify(data),
32	      });
33	
34	      if (res.status === 429) {
35	        const json = await res.json();
36	        setSubmitState("rate-limited");
37	        setErrorMsg(json.error ?? "Too many requests. Please wait.");
38	        return;
39	      }
40	
41	      if (!res.ok) {
42	        const json = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));
43	        setSubmitState("error");
44	        setErrorMsg(json.error ?? "Something went wrong. Please try again.");
45	        return;
46	      }
47	
48	      setSubmitState("success");
49	      reset();
50	    } catch {
51	      setSubmitState("error");
52	      setErrorMsg("Network error. Please check your connection.");
53	    }
54	  }
55	
56	  const inputClass =
57	    "w-full rounded-md border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] px-4 py-2.5 text-sm text-[var(--fg)] placeholder:text-[var(--muted)] transition-colors focus:border-[var(--accent)] focus:outline-none";
58	
59	  const labelClass = "block text-xs font-mono text-[var(--muted)] mb-1.5";
60	
61	  return (
62	    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit(onSubmit)} noValidate className="space-y-5">
63	      {/* Name */}
64	      <div>
65	        <label htmlFor="name" className={labelClass}>
66	          name *
67	        </label>
68	        <input
69	          id="name"
70	          type="text"
71	          autoComplete="name"
72	          placeholder="Tom Swart"
73	          {...register("name")}
74	          className={inputClass}
75	          aria-invalid={!!errors.name}
76	        />
77	        {errors.name && (
78	          <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-red-400 font-mono">
79	            {errors.name.message}
80	          </p>
81	        )}
82	      </div>
83	
84	      {/* Email */}
85	      <div>
86	        <label htmlFor="email" className={labelClass}>
87	          email *
88	        </label>
89	        <input
90	          id="email"
91	          type="email"
92	          autoComplete="email"
93	          placeholder="you@example.com"
94	          {...register("email")}
95	          className={inputClass}
96	          aria-invalid={!!errors.email}
97	        />
98	        {errors.email && (
99	          <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-red-400 font-mono">
100	            {errors.email.message}
101	          </p>
102	        )}
103	      </div>
104	
105	      {/* Message */}
106	      <div>
107	        <label htmlFor="message" className={labelClass}>
108	          message *
109	        </label>
110	        <textarea
111	          id="message"
112	          rows={5}
113	          placeholder="What's on your mind?"
114	          {...register("message")}
115	          className={`${inputClass} resize-none`}
116	          aria-invalid={!!errors.message}
117	        />
118	        {errors.message && (
119	          <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-red-400 font-mono">
120	            {errors.message.message}
121	          </p>
122	        )}
123	      </div>
124	
125	      {/* Submit */}
126	      <button
127	        type="submit"
128	        disabled={isSubmitting}
129	        className="w-full rounded-md border border-[var(--accent)] px-5 py-2.5 text-sm font-medium text-[var(--accent)] transition-all hover:bg-[var(--accent-dim)] disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed"
130	      >
131	        {isSubmitting ? "Sending..." : "Send Message"}
132	      </button>
133	
134	      {/* Feedback messages */}
135	      <AnimatePresence mode="wait">
136	        {submitState === "success" && (
137	          <motion.p
138	            key="success"
139	            initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 4 }}
140	            animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
141	            exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
142	            className="text-sm text-green-400 font-mono text-center"
143	          >
144	            ✓ Message sent. I&apos;ll get back to you soon.
145	          </motion.p>
146	        )}
147	        {(submitState === "error" || submitState === "rate-limited") && (
148	          <motion.p
149	            key="error"
150	            initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 4 }}
151	            animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
152	            exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
153	            className="text-sm text-red-400 font-mono text-center"
154	          >
155	            {errorMsg}
156	          </motion.p>
157	        )}
158	      </AnimatePresence>
159	    </form>
160	  );
161	}
162
```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 14:20:22Z)**_

Now I have everything. Let me make all changes simultaneously:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Tailwind's `dark:` variant works here because `@custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *))` in globals.css tells Tailwind to generate dark-prefixed utilities that activate on the `.dark` class (set by next-themes). So `text-red-600 dark:text-red-400` correctly adapts: red-600 (dark enough for light bg) vs red-400 (bright enough for dark bg).
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>

```mdx
---
title: "Grocery Lens"
slug: "grocery-lens"
description: "A Django/PostgreSQL data product with custom dietary filters, recipe matching, basket synchronisation and NLP-assisted ingredient processing — built and operated from scratch."
date: "2026-04-14"
tags: ["Python", "Django", "PostgreSQL", "Docker", "Playwright", "spaCy", "Grafana"]
thumbnail: "/media/projects/grocery-lens/thumbnail.svg"
liveUrl: "https://grocerylens.co.uk"
featured: true
status: "in-progress"
---

## Overview

Grocery Lens is a Django/PostgreSQL data product I've been building from scratch since July 2025. It combines custom dietary filters, recipe matching, basket synchronisation and NLP-assisted ingredient processing to help users navigate grocery data in ways retailer apps don't support.

The backend is the core challenge: designing a platform that handles scraping, ingestion, data quality repair, search and analytics from the same PostgreSQL schema — without losing data integrity as the product grows.

## Product Features

- **Dietary filters** — user-created diet templates with ingredient-level allow/restrict rules; publicly posted diets support community upvoting and downvoting
- **Recipe matching** — compatible recipes surfaced per diet; background caching pre-computes matches after a diet is saved so results are instant on first load
- **Basket & favourites** — add products to a grocery basket, favourite individual items, track changes over time
- **Price history** — per-product price tracking across scrape runs; surfaced in product detail and the compare page
- **Compare page** — side-by-side product comparison across retailers
- **D3.js dietary tree visualiser** — interactive ingredient hierarchy view — [see it live](https://grocerylens.co.uk/diet/4/low-fodmap-template/?view=tree)

## What I Built

- **Production data platform:** PostgreSQL schemas for semi-structured product and analytics workloads using `JSONField`, `ArrayField`, GIN indexes and integrity constraints
- **Replayable ingestion workflows:** loading retailer scrapes, public datasets and CSV logs with upserts, transactional writes and structured repair tables for relinking, backfills and taxonomy improvement
- **NLP ingredient processing:** canonicalisation and fuzzy matching with spaCy; fixed a scraper memory leak that reached 5.9 GB over 5,938 products
- **Concurrency-aware processing:** bounded concurrency, retry-safe queues using `select_for_update(skip_locked)` to balance throughput and reliability
- **Background caching:** post-save signals trigger async pre-computation of recipe and product compatibility so users never wait for cold results
- **Privacy-aware analytics:** retention controls, aggregation layers and reporting controls built for a live service
- **Observability:** Grafana/Loki log aggregation, health checks, blue/green deployments and rollback-safe traffic switching

## Technical Choices

- **Django ORM + raw SQL** — Django models for application logic; raw SQL for complex ingestion queries, upserts and repair workflows where the ORM adds unnecessary overhead
- **PostgreSQL** — `JSONField` and `ArrayField` for semi-structured product data; GIN indexes for fast search; queue-backed processing using `select_for_update(skip_locked)` for background work
- **Playwright + BeautifulSoup** — Playwright for JS-rendered retailer pages; BeautifulSoup for lighter scraping tasks
- **Docker + Caddy** — containerised service behind Caddy, deployed blue/green with a health-check-gated swap script
- **Grafana + Loki** — structured log aggregation for pipeline health and data quality trend monitoring

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```mdx
---
title: "Grocery Lens — Dietary Tree Visualiser"
slug: "grocery-lens-diet-tree"
description: "An interactive D3.js collapsible tree for navigating ingredient taxonomy and dietary filter hierarchies within Grocery Lens — with zoom/pan, animated transitions, and fullscreen support."
date: "2025-11-01"
tags: ["D3.js", "JavaScript", "Django", "PostgreSQL", "Playwright"]
thumbnail: "/media/projects/grocery-lens-diet-tree/thumbnail.png"
liveUrl: "https://grocerylens.co.uk/diet/4/low-fodmap-template/?view=tree"
featured: false
status: "completed"
---

## Overview

Part of [Grocery Lens](/projects/grocery-lens). The dietary filter system models ingredient taxonomy as a tree — a diet like Low-FODMAP is the root, its rules form the branches, and individual ingredients are leaves. This visualiser makes that structure navigable directly in the browser rather than through flat filter lists.

## What I Built

- **Collapsible D3 tree** — nodes expand and collapse on click; `d3.hierarchy` structures the data, `d3.tree` computes positions, animated SVG transitions smooth the reflow
- **Zoom and pan** — full `d3.zoom` integration; users can freely navigate large taxonomies without losing context; a reset button snaps back to the default view
- **Lazy loading** — the tree only fetches data when the Tree View tab is activated, avoiding an unnecessary API call on page load
- **Fullscreen mode** — browser Fullscreen API toggled via a Feather icon button; the SVG reflows to fill the viewport
- **Django REST endpoint** — a dedicated view at `/api/diet/<diet_id>/ingredient-hierarchy/` serialises the full ingredient subtree as nested JSON for a given diet template
- **End-to-end tests** — Playwright + Django TestCase covering tab switching, tree rendering, lazy-load timing, fullscreen toggle, and API response shape

## Technical Choices

- **D3.js** — `d3.hierarchy`, `d3.tree`, `d3.zoom`; SVG `<path>` elements with cubic Bezier curves for links; enter/update/exit transitions for smooth node animations
- **Vanilla JS (`diet_tree_visualization.js`)** — no framework; the Django template embeds the serialised tree data and a single script initialises D3 against it
- **Bootstrap 5** — tabs, buttons, alerts, and layout scaffolding; CSS custom property theme variables used for colour consistency with the rest of the Django site
- **Feather icons** — tab icon, fullscreen toggle, and reset button; lightweight SVG sprite, no icon font dependency
- **Playwright** — end-to-end test suite validates the full user flow: tab click → API fetch → SVG render → fullscreen → collapse/expand

```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 14:21:37Z)**_

Clean build. Here's what changed and why:

---

**Light mode contrast fixes (`globals.css`):**

| Token | Before | After | Contrast gain |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--accent` | oklch(74%) cyan | oklch(38% 0.14 200) dark teal | ~1.9:1 → ~8:1 on bg |
| `--accent-dim` | not overridden (12% opacity cyan) | oklch(88% 0.06 200) light teal | tag bg now works with dark teal text |
| `--muted` | oklch(48%) | oklch(40%) | ~4.0:1 → ~5.9:1 on bg |
| `--surface-alt` | oklch(89%) | oklch(87%) | skill chips more legible |
| `--border` | oklch(80%) | oklch(72%) | card borders more visible |

The accent hue stays the same (200° = teal-cyan family) — it just shifts from a mid-lightness value designed for dark backgrounds to a dark value that reads on light. The theme toggle will feel coherent because the hue doesn't jump.

**ContactForm:** `text-red-400 dark:text-red-400` → `text-red-600 dark:text-red-400` and `text-green-700 dark:text-green-400` — both were passing in dark mode but failing in light (~3:1 and ~1.8:1 respectively).

**Grocery Lens MDX:** Added a "Product Features" section covering the upvoting/downvoting, basket, favourites, price history, compare page, and background caching. Kept "What I Built" for the engineering work.

**Diet Tree MDX:** Replaced the inaccurate description with the accurate stack — Bootstrap 5 tabs, Feather icons, `diet_tree_visualization.js`, the specific D3 APIs used, the lazy-load-on-tab-open behaviour, fullscreen API, and Playwright + Django TestCase e2e tests.

---

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-14 15:02:01Z)**_

Yes, it's safe. The `.gitignore` was set up specifically to handle this. Here's the full picture:

**Already excluded:**
- `.env`, `.env.*` — all env files except the placeholder `.env.example` ✓
- `public/media/*` — **photos, thumbnails, avatar, everything under `media/`** are all excluded ✓
- `infra/Caddyfile` and `infra/.active_port` — runtime-generated files ✓
- `.next/`, `node_modules/` ✓
- No hardcoded secrets anywhere in source — the infra files use `TUNNEL_ID_HERE` / `__PORT__` placeholders ✓

**On the photos specifically:** No, photos should NOT go in git — and they won't. `public/media/*` in `.gitignore` covers every file and subdirectory under `public/media/`, so your avatar, thumbnails, and any future media are all excluded. There's a single `public/media/.gitkeep` that gets committed just to preserve the empty directory. The media lives on the server only, volume-mounted at runtime by Docker.

**One minor thing worth being aware of:** `infra/Caddyfile.tmpl` and `infra/cloudflared-config.yml` contain the server path `/home/debiantreeb/...` — your Linux username. That's not a secret but it's visible if the repo is public. Up to you whether that matters.

To initialise:
```bash
cd /home/debiantreeb/portfolio
git init
git add .
git commit -m "Initial commit"
git remote add origin https://github.com/tswart-res/portfolio.git
git push -u origin main
```

Run `git status` first to sense-check what's staged — it should not include anything from `public/media/` or any `.env` files.
